clues: KNOWN BY EVERYBODY BUT NOBODY ACTUALLY KNOWS THEM
ROLE MODEL WHOSE ROLES INCLUDE MODEL
TOWERING ICON WHO'S LESS THAN ONE FOOT TALL
I TELL YOU KEN THINKS SHE'S A DOLL
CAME OUT OF NOWHERE NOW IT IS EVERYWHERE
EASY TO GET INTO EVEN THOUGH THERE'S A BOUNCER
SOUR SPORT THAT GOOD SPORTS RELISH

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to the very first episode of Best Guess Live! All right, now you've probably heard some incredible news about Netflix over the past week or so, and I'm here to tell you it's all true. You really can win big money playing Best Guess Live every weekday; you just have to be faster than the rest of the country. Tonight, you are all part of Best Guess Live history. There will never be another first episode of Best Guess Live until the inevitable reboot in 2047.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here's how the game works. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round, we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues to figure it out. After each clue, you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. We do not accept typos.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money, and if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you'll win that entire prize pot for that round. And honestly, right now 8,000 players—these might be the best odds you've ever had to win. In fact, in my opinion, I think you should probably tell a friend right now to download the app and start playing so you have twice as good odds. But that's just me, do what you want.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Now is your chance to do it though. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Now, since this is such a historic moment, we want to see you guys playing. So, if it's possible at some point, have someone in the room film you playing Best Guess Live and send us the video. Tag us on your socials with #BestLive, and we'll happily watch all of them. Enough talk, though; let's start giving away some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: All right, round one. 8,316 players in the room. This round is worth $5,000. I mean, where else do you have those odds for $5,000? Let's load the crystal ball. Okay, and that is what you're trying to figure out. I wish you guys the best of luck. Let's go ahead and play. Here is your first clue.
Clue 1: GLOBAL MULTIMEDIA STAR. (20 seconds on the clock starts now.)

It's interesting... This is the first clue of clues. This is the first 20 seconds of 20 seconds. I guess none of you have been hosting this show in trials for the past year, so it’s more of a me finding it interesting thing. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Clue number one was GLOBAL MULTIMEDIA STAR. I can see your answers. Avery said "Dua Lipa." Mint Spiral 671 said "Justin Bieber"—definitely a global multimedia star. Space Girl Mandy said "Logan Paul." Someone else said "Cher."
Clue 2: KNOWN BY EVERYBODY BUT NOBODY ACTUALLY KNOWS THEM. (20 seconds starts now.)

You're going to learn that these clues build upon each other, and they eventually reveal something very clearly. In the beginning, it's a hard guess to make, but who knows who's going to get it. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Dairybox Diva 686 said "Banksy," the graffiti artist. That is known by almost everybody, but nobody actually knows them. Great answer. Pwos 21 said "Marshmello," the DJ. Miss M's 916 said "Sia." And then Best Sigzy970 said "Siri."
Clue 3: ROLE MODEL WHOSE ROLES INCLUDE MODEL. (20 seconds starts now.)

It is not too late to play! We've gained hundreds of players since this first puzzle started, so you can still enter in your answer. But there's another clue coming, so even if you got it wrong, stay around for the next puzzle worth $10,000. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Scaly Nutmeg 174 said "Tyra Banks"—a role model whose roles include model, that's very accurate. Another person said "Alexa." Neems 212 said "Madonna." And then Clement Fuel 471 said "Kim Kardashian."
Clue 4: TOWERING ICON WHO'S LESS THAN ONE FOOT TALL. (20 seconds starts now.)

I don't think Kim is that tall, but is she less than one foot? I've never met her in person. Sia... I think she's fairly average height. Trying to think who else that could be under one foot. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: 644 of you submitted on clue number four. ADHD Tweety Bird said "Tinkerbell." Gentle Leak said "Minion." Known by everybody but nobody actually knows them... and then It's Red 0204 said "Yoda." Yoda cannot be less than 12 inches tall! That's like the most popular movie in the world; you should have known that.
Clue 5: I TELL YOU KEN THINKS SHE'S A DOLL. (20 seconds starts now.)
This is your last clue, your last chance. About 5,000 of you have submitted answers so far, which means 5,000 of you haven't. Last chance! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: I said the last one was a gimme, and Sherry Neiel 12 said "Ken." I like you thinking that I gave you the answer in the middle of the clue. Bold move, but let's see what was actually inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was BARBIE. Very clear towards the end. But again, it doesn't matter if you got it right; it matters who got it right on the earliest clue.
Clue 5: "I tell you Ken thinks she’s a doll." Of course, it’s made by Mattel and there’s Ken. 3,600 of you got it right on this clue.
Clue 4: "Towering icon is less than one foot tall." She's very small. 357 of you got it right on clue number four.
Clue 3: "Role model whose roles include model." 110 people got it right on clue number three.
Clue 2: "Known by everybody but nobody actually knows them." 1,199 people guessed; two people got it right!

Clue 1: "Global multimedia star." My first guess was The Rock, and I'm sure a lot of you made that mistake. Did anyone actually get BARBIE? We had 1,973 people guess on clue number one, and two people got it right!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Those two people are each going home with $2,500! How exciting! Congratulations to Helen Gibb and Kimberly JCD. Now, please welcome the other host of Best Guess Live, an absolute living legend... the incredible Howie Mandel!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Hunter March, thank you, Hunter! Wow, you're a tough act to follow, but I think doubling the prize to $10,000 for round two will help. This is very exciting! You know how it works: five clues, 20 seconds, and only one best guess. Let's load this crystal ball.
Clue 1: CAME OUT OF NOWHERE, NOW IT IS EVERYWHERE. (20 seconds starts now.)

You should be telling people: it came out of nowhere, now it’s everywhere! What do you think it is? It’s a group game; I wouldn’t play this alone. I want one person to walk away with $10,000 on the first night! Wouldn’t that be amazing? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Empty Frog said "AI." Came out of nowhere, now it’s everywhere—that’s true. I personally have no eye anyway. Canary Eel 716 said "snow." Standing in Los Angeles, it’s not everywhere!
Clue 2: CAUSES INTENSE EXCHANGES BETWEEN FRIENDS. (20 seconds starts now.)
First night, I want everybody to win. I can’t believe it, we have almost 9,000 people playing! We've been practicing with like three people; it's so much better to have 9,000. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: 3,500 guesses already. A Little Mug says "a meme." Omar Coming 512 says "money." It was nowhere, now it’s everywhere. You think you won, didn’t you, Omar? I hope you’re right.
Clue 3: EASY TO GET INTO EVEN THOUGH THERE'S A BOUNCER. (20 seconds starts now.)
Record yourself! Maybe we could play the videos. I’ll look at all your videos! I had no idea it would be this successful. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Audio Yak 514 said "club" because there’s a bouncer in a club. They were nowhere, and now clubs are everywhere. I wish I was a member.
Clue 4: SMALLER SERVINGS, MASSIVE POPULARITY. (20 seconds starts now.)
Remember, spelling counts! Even if you think you know the answer, make sure you spell it right. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Reputation said "sushi." Well, they are smaller servings. Maybe that could be good appetizers. We’re into food; a lot of people are hungry!
Clue 5: SOUR SPORT THAT GOOD SPORTS RELISH. (20 seconds starts now.)
This is your final chance to guess today! Tomorrow I would go to a huge crowd and ask everybody to help. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: 6,179 submitted answers. Somebody said "tennis," somebody said "whiffle ball." Let’s find out what it was.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It's PICKLEBALL! Let's take a look at how the clues fit.
Clue 5: "Sour sport that good sports relish." Sour pickles! 740 of you got it right on clue number five.
Clue 4: "Smaller servings, massive popularity." It’s a much smaller serving than tennis. 123 of you got it correct and spelled it right.
Clue 3: "Easy to get into even though there’s a bouncer." It’s good for everybody, and it bounces! 60 people got it right.
Clue 2: "Causes intense exchanges between friends." Intense exchanges... 1,700 people submitted; eight people got it right.
Clue 1: "Came out of nowhere, now it is everywhere." I never heard of pickleball five years ago, and now everybody's playing it. 3,122 people took a shot at that first guess; 10 of you got it right!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That means 10 people are each taking $1,000! What a night! $1,000 for just sitting there with your mobile device!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: We just want to thank everyone at Netflix and our incredible staff and crew. Congratulations again to all of our winners. We're here live every weekday at 8:00 Eastern, 5:00 Pacific. If you had fun, tell a friend. Join us tomorrow when we'll have two new puzzles and another $15,000 to give away. Good night everybody!

clues: BACK AND FORTH BILLIONS OF TIMES
KNOW IT ALL WHO IS STILL LEARNING
HELPS WRITE YOUR RESUME BUT MAY TAKE YOUR JOB
CLASSIC ROCK GROUP THAT'S STILL TOGETHER

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! I'm your host Hunter March. Tonight we have almost 10,000 people in here already and it is only going up. Want to shout out some first-time players: we got Story 75, we got Dorcas—or it's Darkness, I think it's Dorcas—and SassyC 25. And then Billy, welcome back, second night in a row.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Speaking of second night, last night we had two winners on our very first puzzle who got the answer right on the very first clue. And to make it even crazier, those two winners live in the same house! It's unbelievable, but here's the proof... 345 right there. That is crazy! They both guessed Barbie with the least amount of information we've ever given anybody. But they deserve it. Thank you guys for sharing that video. The rest of you should have somebody film you while playing Best Guess Live and then you send it in. Use the hashtag #BestLive, send it to us. Whoever films you, whoever already loses, if they've lost the game, their job is camera operator.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Anyway, now is your chance to get in the game and take your share of $15,000. You just have to beat every other person in America, especially those two girls. Here's how the show works: each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we'll load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. And we do not accept typos. Last night a bunch of people thought Barbie was spelled with a Y... we saved untold dollars. Anyway, everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you'll win that entire prize spot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: All right, let's start giving away some money. 12,000 players. This round one is worth $5,000. All right, let's load the crystal ball with the first secret item. There it is... shiny, mystical, magical.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here comes clue number one. I'm excited for this. Uh, let's—before we do that, I actually want to chat with you guys for a brief second about how exciting it would be to win. We showed you the video earlier, but in fact, you know, how about I get a piece of paper in here right now... Oh, if you don't have it... Uh, okay. We actually might have the wrong puzzle loaded up. Don't worry about it, we're going to fix it right now. This is part of a day-two struggle, people. Uh, if you see the puzzle... Oh, you guys don't even see it yet. If I gave it to you right now, the clue would have been the clue from yesterday. You guys would all win until we get it loaded up though, which I actually know some of it, but we want to make sure we get it right. Okay, we've got it here now. All right, nobody left. I can see there's still 12,482 of you. Here's your first clue.
Clue 1: ENTER AT YOUR OWN RISK. (20 seconds on the clock starts now.)

Enter at your own risk. I think that's an appropo clue for just this entire game show. Five seconds left. If you want to go for that first clue, win that first clue glory, I wish you the best of luck. Otherwise, you can wait. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: 1,700 people guessed. Wacky Zebra 6767 said "cave." Worthy Sailor 418 said "Jaws." And then someone said "Gatherer Mar"—oh that's their name—they said "poker."
Clue 2: BACK AND FORTH BILLIONS OF TIMES. (20 seconds starts now.)
Back and forth billions of times. Okay, could be poker if you have a problem. Five seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Less guesses on this one. Sarah XO Gold said "Maize" from Allentown, Pennsylvania. Someone I'll call "Fat Finger" said "ocean." I'm sure it does go back and forth billions of times with the waves.
Clue 3: KNOW IT ALL WHO'S STILL LEARNING. (20 seconds starts now.)
12,616 players. 5,006 submitted guesses already. Has anyone got it right? Last night might have been a fluke, who knows. People might not get it right till clue number five. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Miss Jess 479 from Greenwood, Arkansas said "Men." She deserves all of the winnings! Somebody else said "internet," somebody said "debate."
Clue 4: HELPS WRITE YOUR RESUME BUT MAY TAKE YOUR JOB. (20 seconds starts now.)

15 seconds left. We have "know it all who's still learning," "men," "back and forth billions of times"... You also do have to go back and forth billions of times with men just to get them to put dishes the right way. At least that's what my fiance says about me. Time's up.
Clue 5: OPEN AI HAD A BOT. (20 seconds starts now.)

Here's your final clue. How does the thing go... what is it a parody of... "E-I-E-I-O." Oh, "Open AI had a bot." Anyway, "Open AI had a bot, A-I-A-I-O." Listen, that is so belittling. I messed up Old MacDonald. I am a know-it-all who is still learning. This is your last chance. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's go ahead and see what was inside the crystal ball. The answer was CHATGPT. Specifically, you had to get that one. The last clue, Open AI, actually gave it to you.
Clue 5: "Open AI, the company that invented ChatGPT." 719 people guessed it right on this clue.
Clue 4: "Helps write your resume but may take your job." 584 people got it right on clue four.
Clue 3: "Know it all who's still learning." I think "men" was the right answer, but apparently 91 other people got the actual right answer.
Clue 2: "Back and forth billions of times." People have had billions of interactions with ChatGPT. 1,700 people guessed; one person got it right!
Clue 1: "Enter at your own risk." You have to hit enter, and then you have to double-check your answers. 3,200 people guessed; one person got it right!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That person is going home with $5,000! And that person is Dum Dum. They're a Netflix lover. This is great. They're our best guesser for the first puzzle.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: This round is worth even more: $10,000. Let's load our crystal ball. I'm not going to make it as easy.
Clue 1: NATURAL BUT NOT NATURAL. (20 seconds starts now.)
Wow, "Dum Dum"... Oh, that's funny, their name is Dum Dum too. I just realized they thought it was Dum Dum, but really it's just Dum Dum. Five seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: 173 guesses. Good Vapor 165 said "gas." Elbell said "Makeup." Your girl Snoopy said "Hair." My co-host has some of the most natural hair you'll ever see.
Clue 2: CLASSIC ROCK GROUP THAT'S STILL TOGETHER. (20 seconds starts now.)
10 seconds left. Definitely less guesses this time. I told you I was going to make it a harder puzzle. Is this going to be our first puzzle where someone doesn't get it on the first try? Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Renee 2001 said "AC/DC." Oasis, they're back together. U2, always been together. Rush... too young to know a lot about them, but I know they have a big fan base.
Clue 3: 1 3 26 16 BINGO. (20 seconds starts now.)
A lot of numbers. Good luck. 12,000 people playing. 13 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Stout Pegasus 220 said "Korn"—the band. If there is a song by Korn that has "1 3 26 16 BINGO" in it, I'll give you something. Eel Lurman 999 said "ZZ Top."
Clue 4: THE ULTIMATE FACE OFF. (20 seconds starts now.)
I have a guess... I also know the answer, but I also have a guess as to what I would have guessed at this point. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Sable Spirit 530 said "Queen." Maybe between their original singer and their new singer. "Wink Chicken Den"—whatever—said "lips." Weird Curve 914 said "ABBA."
Clue 5: DAKOTA HILLS POTUS GRILLS. (20 seconds starts now.)
Final clue for your chance at $10,000. There are still 4,000 of you that haven't guessed. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Michaela Lonnie said "The Rolling Stones." Classic rock group that's still together... I don't know how it works for "Potus Grills" though.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was MOUNT RUSHMORE! Pretty insane, you guys did a great job.
Clue 5: "Dakota Hills Potus Grills." We kind of gave it to you. It's in Dakota Hills, the POTUS faces. 127 people got it right on this clue.
Clue 4: "The ultimate face off." Obviously, it's depictions of faces. 48 people got it right.
Clue 3: "1 3 26 16 Bingo." That's the numbers for each of the presidents on the mountain. 63 people got it right out of 1,300 guesses.
Clue 2: "Classic rock group that's still together." They're a group of faces carved into rock. 68 people got it right out of 2,900 guesses.
Clue 1: "Natural but not natural." It's obviously a natural mountain with a man-made sculpture. 16 people somehow got it right!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Those 16 people are each going home with $625! You guys are legends. Big Pancake 92, Squalligans, Kdum 13—our best guessers.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That's it for this episode of Best Guess Live! Congratulations to all of our winners. Join me back here tomorrow. Bring a friend—if you're smart, make it one of those two girls that won yesterday; they seem to know what they're doing. Tell your friends about it, you've got more chances to win. Good night!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! That's right, the show where everyone is a winner as long as you win. Well, don't worry about that. Anyway, I'm your host Hunter March. We're off to an amazing start. Over 30 people have won big money already and it's only day three. There's more to come. Now's your chance to get some of the cash. You just have to be faster than—oh my gosh, 12,297 other players who are playing right now. Good luck!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here's how the game works. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item. Now it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. And make sure you're confident before you lock it in. We do not accept typos. Mount Rushmore threw a lot of people for a lot of loops. "Mount who knows rushmore," get out of here.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Anyway, everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. If you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you'll win that entire prize pot for that round. And we've heard from winners; the best part is when you think you got it right and you're waiting to see how many people got it right too. I hope that happens to you. And if it does, if you think you're going to win, or if you think you're going to lose and you're going to have a good reaction, make sure to film it. We love it. Send it to us with the hashtag #BestLive. Send it to me at Hunter March, send it to Netflix Games. Anyway, you got to be fast, you got to be right. I think I'm ready to start giving away some money. Let's do it!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one. There are 14,000 players in here right now. Round one is worth $5,000. And remember there's a second round right afterwards worth $10,000, so stay tuned for that. Let's load the crystal ball with our first secret item. Oh my god, oh look at that thing! Oh it's so gold, it's so beautiful. It's a ball of possibility, it's a ball of money for you guys. Here comes your first clue.
Clue 1: CAN FEEL ENDLESS. (20 seconds starts now.)

Okay the timer's going. We're not playing games anymore. You guys get how the show works. Can feel endless... I can only imagine what jokey answers I'm going to get for this, but I hope someone actually wins it. Who knows. 5 seconds left. We got four more clues after this. All right, time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: 2,261 people locked in their guess. We got a lot of people saying "time." Whiskey Vixen said "time." Mustard Fizz said "summer"—sometimes summer does feel endless, and then you get a job and it feels way too short.
Clue 2: RULES DO NOT APPLY. (20 seconds starts now.)

4,500 people guessed on the first clue. I love the boldness! Good on you guys. There's 14,600-something people playing right now. Rules do not apply. That is your second clue. Can feel endless, rules do not apply. Interesting. Okay, time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: I got a lot more guesses in there. Desi 27 said "Love." I don't know if Desi's in a good relationship or a bad one, but it can feel endless either way. Horned Mink said "Water." Caged Goldfish said "sleep." I'm a sleepwalker, so rules definitely do not apply.
Clue 3: HAS MULTIPLE PERSONALITIES. (20 seconds starts now.)

If you're just joining us, it's not too late for you to submit your best guess. Who knows if someone's got it right yet. We'll find out at the end. And again, reminder: there's a $10,000 puzzle coming up right after this. Has multiple personalities... "Love" is back in if you've been with any of my exes! All right, time's up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: On clue number three, Dya80 said "Weather." Sunny Osborne 35 said "people." True! Wow, that is so true. And then someone else said "Oh" from King of Prussia, Pennsylvania. I highly doubt that's a real place.
Clue 4: CUE THE GADGETS. (20 seconds starts now.)

Okay, not giving you guys time to think anymore. We're going right at this baby. Clue number one: can feel endless. Clue number two: rules do not apply. Clue number three: has multiple personalities. Clue number four: cue the gadgets. Let's see how many people get this right. We've got 8,000 submitted answers so far, and time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: So far for all the clues, 8,677 submitted answers. Harry I Imp 930 said "IKEA." Cool Braden 21 said "dreams." Very true. In my dreams I've got a lot of gadgets, I've got multiple personalities.
Clue 5: I'LL TAKE AN ASTON MARTINI, SHAKEN NOT STIRRED. (20 seconds starts now.)

I'll take an Aston Martini, shaken not stirred. Cue the gadgets, has multiple personalities, rules do not apply, can feel endless. Those are your five clues. And these are your last five seconds. I beg of you, get in your answer. There's no point in leaving it blank. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Claire Puricelli said "movies." Tough Press 102 said "car." Trick 912 said "Internet."

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's find out what's inside the crystal ball. It's JAMES BOND. The one, the only, and the many: JAMES BOND. Let's find out who got it right on the earliest clue.
Clue 5: "I'll take an Aston Martini, shaken not stirred." That one's a gimme, right? 1,953 people got it right. That's great.
Clue 4: "Cue the gadgets." He's got Q, who is the gadget guy. 1,616 people guessed; of those, 49 people got it right! Whoa, now that's a good amount of people.
Clue 3: "Has multiple personalities." Because James Bond has been played by almost every actor in the world. 1,700 people guessed; zero people got it.
Clue 2: "Rules do not apply." He has a license to kill. It's one of the toughest licenses to get. Zero people got it right.
Clue 1: "Can feel endless." Well, it seems like James Bond has been alive for a thousand years. 4,500 people felt bold; zero people got it right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Meaning the 49 of you who got it right on clue number four are all going home with $102 just for hanging out! Congratulations to Red Light 2025—one of our best guessers—Blessings 143, and Chunky Banker 755.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, $10,000. You know how it works: five clues, 20 seconds, only one best guess. Let's load the crystal ball. In it, you see lightning, I see $10,000!
Clue 1: HIGHLY ANTICIPATED. (20 seconds starts now.)

Highly anticipated. For those of you who maybe it's your first night, welcome. These are riddles that build upon each other. The first clues are there to help you out, and then each clue builds upon itself until we get to one concrete answer. Time is up.

lines.text: Noisy Trapper said "Christmas." Linear Way 224 said "GTA 6." Ebony Burp said "payday." Anastasia said "Wicked."

On the first puzzle, 5,000 people locked in on clue number one. On this puzzle, 1,800 people locked in on the first clue. Ah, we got you! You thought you had us. Time's up.

lines.text: Biggest 217 said "Baby"—highly anticipated, under construction, that's a really good guess. Kyle the Best said "ballroom."

14,761 players playing right now. Highly anticipated, under construction, gets fired but still does the job... gets fired but still does the job... interesting. Time is up.

lines.text: Cookie Got Drip said "roller coaster"—what are you talking about? If there was a fire on a roller coaster, the last thing it’s going to do is keep running. Unkj1 said "Wood." Ah, under construction, highly anticipated... every time new wood drops, I’m like, "Wow, finally!"

20 seconds on the clock. Maybe "wood" is still in it. Time's up.

lines.text: I think we got like over 900 guesses—all of them say "pizza." Like there are so many pizzas in front of me right now. You guys all used a friend and I know who you used to get pizza! Good job, way to not give it your own go. We know that when everyone gets the same answer, there's a common denominator.
Clue 5: THE FINALE FOR CAMPING AND GLAMPING. (20 seconds starts now.)

Now think about it, guys. Highly anticipated, under construction, gets fired but still does the job, the name is addictive, the finale for camping and glamping. This was a tough puzzle, but I love it. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Recap
lines.text: Before we get to the answer, I want to look at some of these. We got "campfire," we got "bonfire," we got "fireworks."

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer is S'MORES! Wow, S'MORES. Let's see who got it right.
Clue 5: "The finale for camping and glamping." Well, you make s'mores at the end of a camping trip. 1,838 people got it right.
Clue 4: "The name is addictive." Well, it’s short for "some more," like "give me some more." 1,880 people guessed; 72 people got it right.

Clue 3: "Gets fired but still does the job." You roast them on a fire, and that is what does the job. 1,656 people guessed it, and nine people got it right! Pull out your camera if you're one of those people!
Clue 2: "Under construction." Well, you construct a s'more with graham crackers, chocolates, and marshmallows. 1,335 people guessed; zero people got it right.
Clue 1: "Highly anticipated." It's usually at the end of a long hike. 1,813 people guessed; zero people got it right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Meaning the nine people who got it right on clue number three are going home with $1,111 each! Wow! Congratulations to Absurd Vanilla 216, Hefty Flax 729, Go Bucks 34, and Junior Putter 9456. I'm so happy you guys each won over $1,000!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: If you didn't win, come back tomorrow. It's that easy. We'd love if you love the game to rate us in the app store. That's it for this episode of Best Guess Live! Congratulations again to all of our winners. We're here every weekday at 5:00 Pacific, 8:00 Eastern. Good night, we'll see you tomorrow! Bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! I'm your host, Hunter March. It's Glasses Thursday! Put your glasses on; I'm tired of pretending I don't have an astigmatism. We're deep into our first week, and I just want to say it is way more fun giving away all this money than I could have ever imagined. Thank you guys for all the comments and feedback; we're thrilled that you're enjoying the show. Keep it coming! We're not just playing a game here; we're building a little community—a community where a lot of money is being given away, the best type of community.

We're also playing a game, so let's go over the rules. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Each round has one puzzle. Before each round, we're going to load our crystal ball with a SECRET ITEM. Now, that SECRET ITEM could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues. After each clue, you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. Not one per clue—one best guess per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in, because we do not accept typos.

Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. If you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you will win that entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. And remember, if possible, I would love it if you guys could film yourselves playing. Have a friend film you, or film it on your computer, and share it with the hashtag #bestguesslive. I think you guys got it; I'm ready to start giving away some money.

Shout out to some winners from our last few nights: Jedi Knight, Odin's Finest, Sunflower, and Madam Amazing. I wish you guys the best of luck. Round 1 is worth $5,000. Let's go ahead and load the crystal ball with the first SECRET ITEM.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: There it is, very mystical, very magical. You guys know how it works. Here is Clue Number 1: BORN IN THE USA. Your 20 seconds starts now. BORN IN THE USA. I feel like I could say this in a thousand different ways and throw you guys for a thousand different loops, but I won't; I'm just saying BORN IN THE USA. No singing! I don't know if they're upset about me singing because of a legal reason or because of my voice; either way, I'm offended.

Clue 1: BORN IN THE USA. Plush Scout 584 said "Ford." Smoky Lotus said "Lady Gaga." Another person said "Miley"—I assume Cyrus and not my niece. Pinkish Comet 385 said "basketball." These are all good guesses. Are they correct? We'll find out.

Let's move on to Clue Number 2: LOVES A GOOD MIXER. That's Clue Number 2. You have 20 seconds; starts now. BORN IN THE USA, LOVES A GOOD MIXER. Remember, guys, if these puzzles feel hard, the good news is we do it every day for a chance to win.
Time is up. Leg Legend the Gamer said "Bruce Springsteen." Good guess, and you can play again tomorrow. Strong Coco said "whiskey." LOVES A GOOD MIXER, BORN IN THE USA. These are great guesses.

Clue Number 3: PROUD TO FLAUNT A COLORFUL COAT. 20 seconds starts now. BORN IN THE USA, LOVES A GOOD MIXER, PROUD TO FLAUNT A COLORFUL COAT. I don't know if Bruce Springsteen has worn anything besides denim forever, so I don't know if that's going to stick.

Time's up. From Davenport, Florida, Jessica said "Santa." Everyone knows he was BORN IN THE USA, moved to the North Pole for the weather, but he started here. Virtual Turtle said "baseball" and Jeffron said "eagle." Let's find out Clue Number 4: ACTS NUTTY SOMETIMES. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

BORN IN THE USA, LOVES A GOOD MIXER, PROUD TO FLAUNT A COLORFUL COAT, ACTS NUTTY SOMETIMES. Lady Gaga might be back in; she is one of my favorite people. Time's up. Rogue Aarina from Texas said "parrot." A parrot does ACT NUTTY SOMETIMES and is PROUD TO FLAUNT A COLORFUL COAT. There's no way a parrot started in the USA. Someone else said "Sherwin Williams," the paint company. Royal Cell said "fudge." If your fudge has a colorful coat, I would not eat that fudge.

Let's move on to the next and final clue. Clue Number 5: TASTES SO NICE THEY NAMED IT TWICE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. BORN IN THE USA, LOVES A GOOD MIXER, PROUD TO FLAUNT A COLORFUL COAT, ACTS NUTTY SOMETIMES, TASTES SO NICE THEY NAMED IT TWICE. Time's up.

I see one good guess in right now. Angular Film 642 from Los Angeles said "Twix." Not a bad guess. Twix—I think there's some play on "two" there, maybe with "mix." Who knows? Let's find out what's inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer is M&M'S! I'll explain it right now. I actually have some M&M'S right now—so good. I prefer peanut next time. Let's take a look at how the clues fit and find out who is taking home the money.

Clue Number 5: TASTES SO NICE THEY NAMED IT TWICE. Two M's, you get it. 516 people got it right on Clue Number 5. Clue Number 4: ACTS NUTTY SOMETIMES. Sometimes there are nuts in M&M'S. 291 got it right. Clue Number 3: PROUD TO FLAUNT A COLORFUL COAT. Well, they do have a colorful, candy-coated shell. 25 people got it right. That's pretty impressive.

Clue Number 2: LOVES A GOOD MIXER. Well, they're all different colors, and they're frequently added to a bunch of different treats; in fact, they're in trail mix often. Zero people got it right. Clue Number 1: BORN IN THE USA. They were created right here in the United States. 3,249 wrong guessers.
This means the 25 of you who got it right on Clue 3 are each going home with $200! Congrats to UF Gators 03, Trey M, Scarlet Curve, and Letic. Good job, you guys!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: $10,000 buckaroonies, baby! You guys know how it works: five clues, 20 seconds, only one best guess. Let us load the crystal ball. Let's start with our first clue: AGAINST THE ODDS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. AGAINST THE ODDS. I feel like this whole game, winning against all these people, is AGAINST THE ODDS. Time is up.

Sam Ray from Austin, Texas, said "poker." Definitely AGAINST THE ODDS. "You Me" said "the lottery." Splashy Yolk said "salmon"—maybe like against the current? I doubt it. Someone named 664 guessed "evens." Let's go into Clue Number 2: FAME AND FORTUNE FOREVER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

AGAINST THE ODDS, FAME AND FORTUNE FOREVER. I'm trying to think of how salmon could possibly have FAME AND FORTUNE FOREVER. I might rule out salmon. Clue Number 2 done. A lot of people said "lottery." If someone guesses something on a clue before you, answering the same thing on the next clue is a horrible strategy!

Clue Number 3: SPLIT FROM ITS BETTER HALF. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. 16,580 players in this lobby right now competing for $10,000. AGAINST THE ODDS, FAME AND FORTUNE FOREVER, SPLIT FROM ITS BETTER HALF. Time is up. Cozy Cottage 355 said "twin." An identical twin would be SPLIT FROM itself and potentially its better half. Benji Ho from Pensacola, Florida, said "Star Wars." Maybe because they were split?

Let's move on to Clue Number 4: EXPERIENCED INCREDIBLE UPS AND DOWNS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. AGAINST THE ODDS, FAME AND FORTUNE FOREVER, SPLIT FROM ITS BETTER HALF, EXPERIENCED INCREDIBLE UPS AND DOWNS. We loved farm salmon and now we're told not to eat it? Salmon's still not in! Time's up.

Gio from Draper, Utah, said "atom"—split from its better half, fame and fortune for Oppenheimer? Previous Finch said "Elvis"—definitely had his UPS AND DOWNS, did SPLIT FROM HIS BETTER HALF, and has FAME AND FORTUNE FOREVER. Someone else said "Share."

This final clue might make it a little clearer. Clue Number 5: I'M THE KING OF THE WORLD! 20 seconds starts now. I was told to really enunciate, really play into it. If you think you're winning, have someone film you and tag me at Hunter March. Time's up. I'M THE KING OF THE WORLD. 3,724 people guessed on that one.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's see what's inside the crystal ball: THE TITANIC. Let's take a look at how the clues fit. Clue Number 5: I'M THE KING OF THE WORLD. The famous line from the movie. 3,243 people got it right, so that would be about $3 each.

Clue Number 4: EXPERIENCED INCREDIBLE UPS AND DOWNS. Well, one really incredible up, and one really not-so-incredible down. 30 people got it right on Clue 4. Really, really good. Clue Number 3: SPLIT FROM ITS BETTER HALF. Of course, it did split in half. Six people got it right! If you are one of those six, you're looking at about $1,500 each.

Clue Number 2: FAME AND FORTUNE FOREVER. THE TITANIC has made very many people famous, and there's still a fortune down there inside that ship. One person got it right! Clue Number 1: AGAINST THE ODDS. Everyone thought this ship was unsinkable; the odds were that it would succeed, and yet it went AGAINST THE ODDS. 1,200 people guessed, and absolutely no one got it right.
This means the one person who guessed THE TITANIC on Clue 2 is going home with $10,000! That's how easy it is to win $10,000 on our show.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Katie Kappy, congratulations! You're our best guesser! I want to see you tomorrow. We're $60,000 down and just getting started. I will see you tomorrow. Until then, keep the comments and videos coming. Good night, everybody!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! I'm your host Hunter March. Thank you for joining us on this fantastic Friday. It's especially fantastic because as some of you know the new Knives Out movie Wake Up Dead Man is premiering on Netflix right now as we speak. So before you go watch the world's greatest detective Benoit Blanc solve another mystery, you can be Benoit Blanc and solve a couple mysteries right here and win your share of $15,000. Another night, more money, but be fast—the competition will be murder. I'm legally obligated to add that this game involves no murder whatsoever.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Anyway, here's how the game works. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues. After each clue you will have 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact right answer, and we do not accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money, and if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you will win that entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: And remember, if possible, make a video of you and or your friends playing Best Guest Live and send it to us or tag us on your socials with the hashtag #BestLive. I think you got it. We got almost 16,000 people right now ready to win some money. Let's go baby!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Okay, round one: $5,000. Let us load the crystal ball with our first secret item. While that loads, I want to say hello to the "Chosen One," first-time player. "Google Peed," first-time player. And "PMP Pom 17." Good luck, you guys. $5,000 on the line, and then right after this $10,000.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: But we'll start with five. Clue number one: STANDS OUT IN A CROWD. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Stands out in a crowd. I see a bunch of new people in here tonight. Tell your friends about it. Get them in on the thing. Share the money. Go in on some sort of pool, turn it into bar trivia, I don't care. Just play the game. 3 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Okay, STANDS OUT IN A CROWD. 2,400 submitted guesses. Out of the 17,000 people playing, "Dilbertos" said Waldo—who literally does not stand out in a crowd. The whole point of Waldo is that he's remarkably difficult to find in a crowd.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Anyway, clue number two: ALWAYS IN INCREDIBLE SHAPE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Waldo's back in. Stands out in the crowd, always in incredible shape. I will say Waldo must be in incredible shape to wear horizontal stripes. Am I right? Yes, you're right. 3 seconds left. Time's up. "Turtle X 216" from Owasso, Oklahoma, said "Shadow." "Prickly Miner" said "Diamond." "Tonto 620" said "Stat." These are good guesses.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's move on to clue number three: AN INNOVATION TO SEE AND BE SEEN. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. 4,664 of you have already submitted your guesses. You got time. This is your third clue, you'll have two more. An innovation to see and be seen. Time is up. "M K Eloa Hilani" said "Clouds." "Mayor Plays" said eyes—one of the greatest innovations we ever had! And then someone else said mirror.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: This next clue might rule some people out. Let's go on to clue number four: 365 POSITIVE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. 365 POSITIVE. Interesting. Eyes are 365 positive for me; I have terrible vision. 365 positive... 2 seconds left. Time is up. "Baby K" said star. "Big Nick 28" said sun—365, it's positive, it's hot. And then "Baked Beans" said camera.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Only one clue though to figure out who's going home with this $5,000. Clue number five: NO LEAD IN THIS ZEPPELIN. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. No lead in this zeppelin. It has to be the exact right answer. 5 seconds left to submit. Time is up. "Cody J Woods" said moon. "Brighten" said nature. Someone's getting $5,000. Let's figure out what that right answer was.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Inside the crystal ball: the GOODYEAR BLIMP. The Goodyear blimp has been around for a long time. 388 people did figure it out. But the question is who got it right on the earliest clue.
Clue 5: "No lead in this zeppelin." It is in fact a zeppelin.
Clue 4: "365 positive." One of my favorite clues actually, because 365 positive is just a good year! Apparently you guys didn't get it; nobody got it right on 365 positive.

Clue 3: "An innovation to see and be seen." It's visible everywhere at the big events; it films everything. One person got GOODYEAR BLIMP! The fact that you got it on that clue, I think you work in the Goodyear blimp.
Clue 2: "Always an incredible shape." It has an incredibly distinct and unique shape that always stays. Zero people got it right.

Clue 1: "Stands out in a crowd." It literally stands out in a crowd—any big crowd you look up and you see the Goodyear blimp. 25,542 people took a swing on clue number one, and zero people got it right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Which means the one person who guessed GOODYEAR BLIMP on clue number three is going home with $5,000! That person is Tactful Teller 26. Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: For the 17,000 of you waiting for puzzle number two, here is $10,000. Yesterday we had one of our most exciting moments: one person went home with the entire $10,000 prize. I gave her a call—Mary Kate from Indiana—and she’s excited to do some charity for the holidays and do some good for her community. I hope there's another incredible person playing right now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's load up the crystal ball. You get one guess per round, 20 seconds per clue. Let's go. Clue number one: WINS A POPULARITY CONTEST. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Mary Kate officially won the popularity contest of distant relatives who all of a sudden want to get lunch! 3 seconds left. Time's up. "Ice Skater Gamer" said Batman. Someone else said "Glinda." "Teacher Mom" from Canton, Ohio, said Barbie.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: BUILT TO BE DEMOLISHED. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Built to be demolished. "Day All Day 97" guessed pizza for the first two clues. "Rainy Larkspur" said Legos. These are all great guesses.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: WAX ON WAX OFF. 20 seconds starts now. 5,000 submitted answers so far. Wax on, wax off... 3, 2, 1. Time's up. "Cal Rosia" said car. "Gentle Notebook" said beard. "Melo Mizen" said cheese—wax on the cheese!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: PRESENTED AROUND THE PRESENT TIME. 20 seconds start now. Presented around the present time... "Content Hermit" also said Legos. Everyone else said car.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: YOU WISH AND CUT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. You wish and cut... I think you guys are figuring it out. Almost everybody got their guesses in. "Justine Jade" said camera. "Andrea Renee" said candle. And "The Riddler" said hair—you wish and cut, every haircut they mess it up!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: For $10,000, it was BIRTHDAY CAKE.
Clue 5: "You wish and cut." Well, you make a birthday wish and then you cut. 662 people got it right.
Clue 4: "Presented around the present time." Well, you eat the birthday cake around the time that you're opening birthday presents. 175 people got it right.
Clue 3: "Wax on wax off." Well, candle wax—it goes in, it goes out. 45 people got it right on clue number three.
Clue 2: "Built to be demolished." Well, you bake a cake just to deliciously tear it apart. 3,356 people guessed, and 14 of them got it right!
Clue 1: "Wins a popularity contest." Everyone loves a birthday cake! Nobody got it right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Meaning 14 people are going home with $714.28 each! Congratulations to N173, Mojo Ryan, T3 Kids, and Cyber Narwhal 514.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That's it for week one of Best Guest Live. We've given away $75,000 this week! Congratulations again to all of our winners. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Go watch Knives Out and then have a great weekend. I'm going to take my knife out and cut into this delicious birthday cake! Good night everybody.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! Hey, week two of Best Guess Live, we did it! We made it a week. I'm your host Hunter March, and we did have an amazing premiere week. There were a ton of winners, fun puzzles, Howie broke a ton of lights with a pickleball, and we had more players join us every single day. Today we already have—oh my god—we have 17,000 players already! That's amazing. Yes, that's you guys. That is you guys. But we're just getting started; we have a lot of surprises in store for you in the next coming days and weeks. But right now, I have a shiny $15,000 to give away.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here's how the show works. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues. After each clue you will get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one best guess per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact right answer and we don't accept typos. Don't send me angry DMs, I told you. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will win their share in the money, and if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you will win that entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. And don't forget, we really want your thoughts and comments and especially videos of you winning. Just tag us across all your socials with #BestLive. Thank you guys for doing that by the way. Let's start giving away some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Okay, almost 20,000 of you in here competing for $5,000. Right after this is $10,000 so stay tuned. Let's load the crystal ball with the first secret item. Ooh shiny, ooh fancy. Here comes clue number one, I'm excited. Okay, 20,000 players, it's official—our first 20 banger! That's not the clue, sorry, don't put that on screen.
Clue 1: BACK TO BASICS. (20 seconds on the clock starts now.)

Back to basics, kind of like... it reminds me of the good old days where we only had 18,000 viewers last week. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Sorry, I got excited; you guys had a lot of extra time for that one. That's a mistake on my part, very in favor of you. While we're here, I want to shout out some returning players: Kels 217, thanks for coming back, and Pickled Ginger 4. Time's up.

lines.text: Present P 337 said "Hanes." Evie Ebie said "jeans." Squeaky Hamster said "roots." Wonder Cashew said "pencil."
Clue 2: LOUD WITH THE CROWD. (20 seconds starts now.)
Back to basics, loud with the crowd. Roots—different roots. The other Roots are loud with the crowd, but are they back to basics? Time's up.

lines.text: Caveman 6901 said "Cheers." Polar Rogue said "Ya."

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's find out what's inside that crystal ball. That's right, ladies and gentlemen, it is the one, the only... PARIS HILTON. A lot of people don't know this, but I hosted a podcast with Paris Hilton. Let's see who guessed correctly.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: One person is taking home the entire $10,000! V Mama is our best guesser! They're going home with $10,000! That's amazing.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That's it for this episode of Best Guess Live! Again, if you love the game, send us a message with the hashtag #BestLive. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5:00 p.m. Pacific. We really appreciate you guys playing, so come back tomorrow and bring a friend! Good night everybody!

LEADER OF THE PACK of cookies in an entire market. 2,165 people tried to guess. How many people got it right? If it's you, pull out your phone because three of you are going home with $3,333 each! That's amazing.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations on watching the weirdest show you'll ever see. We're one of the only programs where we take a break right in the middle and come back 15 minutes later, and that's the best part about Best Guest Live: you're always kind of guessing what's going to happen. No, the rest of the shows are going to be normal, don't you worry. And congratulations again to all of our winners. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Next week is the million-dollar giveaway, so please tell your friends, team up with them, share the money, whatever you want to do. And tomorrow I'll have another $15,000 up for grabs. Good night!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Bonjour! Best Guess Live! You don't have to change your Netflix, no, that is not French coming out of your speakers, that's me... kind of, maybe? I have no idea how the French feel, but anyway, we're doing a whole French episode! I'm your host Hunter March. Actually, we're coming to you live from "Paris" to celebrate the release of the new season of Emily in Paris. If Paris is good enough for Emily, it's good enough for me, so enjoy all the beautiful sights of "Pali" while we play today.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Currently, there are 24,000 of you logged in right now, and frankly, I'm a little relieved. If you guys don't know what happened yesterday... good! Nothing happened. Nothing at all happened. Last night we had some technical issues. It's what happens anytime you do a live show with cutting-edge technology; things can go wrong. But we have fixed the problem. The problem's name was Tom and he's gone... no, no, there isn't even a Tom, that's the best part! And the silver lining is that we did get our tech issues out of the way before, as you guys know, next week you're all playing for a million dollars! It's so hard to comprehend. I don't know if I've ever had a chance to play anything for a million dollars where I actually could use my brain. It's just pretty unbelievable.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: I hope you guys are excited, but first things first, we got $15,000 to play for right now. Here's how the show works: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact right answer; we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money, and if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you will win that entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: And remember, if possible, make a video of you and your friends playing Best Guess Live. Send it to us at @HunterMarch at NetflixGames with the hashtag #bestguesslive. All right, enough about that. There's 31,000 players in here right now—the most we've ever had! 31,000 people ready to win some money. Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball with the first secret item. Look at that little vortex of fortune.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here comes clue number one: LIGHT TRAVELER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. 32,100-something players in the lobby right now, all playing for a chance at $5,000. You don't have to guess on the first one. I see some good guesses already coming in, some bold guessers. Time's up. Clue one: LIGHT TRAVELER. "Pudgy Snot" said "Santa"—by the way, every episode about a thousand people guess Santa; I'm going to let you guys know we might not do that today. "Mac 2323" said "ant"—it's a light traveler for sure. "Void Avocado" said "sound." These are great guesses.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's move on to clue number two: CAN BE SALTY AND SWEET. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I'm going to be giving you three more clues. We have 33,000 people—I think there's 6,000 new people here tonight! I'm going to give you more information to help you solve this riddle. They're tough, they're meant to be tough, but people have been figuring them out. Time is up. "Savage K-Bob 212" from New Philadelphia, Ohio, said "Men." All right, I'm just going to move on. "Panda MB" said "Popcorn"—can be salty and sweet. I do love movie theater popcorn.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's find out what the next clue is. Clue number three: AN EXPERT AT CONTROLLING SMALL AIRCRAFT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Let's go over all the clues: LIGHT TRAVELER, CAN BE SALTY AND SWEET, AN EXPERT AT CONTROLLING SMALL AIRCRAFT. Time's up. "GB Wood" from New York, New York, said "Han Solo." We're kind of on the same page there. "Stubby Turnip 589" said "Air."

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's move on to clue number four: HAS THE LADIES IN THE PALM OF HIS HAND. 20 seconds starts now. LIGHT TRAVELER, CAN BE SALTY AND SWEET, AN EXPERT AT CONTROLLING SMALL AIRCRAFT, HAS THE LADIES IN THE PALM OF HIS HAND. I think the person who put Han Solo is feeling very confident right now. Time's up. "Olivia Isabelle" said "stud"—I guess it's the most literal thing ever.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's move on to our final clue. Your fifth clue is your last chance to get in an answer. Even if you don't know what it is, put something fun in there! Clue number five: PUTS THE APE IN SKYSCRAPER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. That's your final 20 seconds. Time is up. "Purple Cobra" said "Godzilla"—so close! One of the only things the same size as what I'm about to... I think they fought in a bunch of movies.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's find out what's inside the crystal ball. The correct answer was KING KONG! Very, very tough riddle, but you guys got it. 6,738 people got King Kong. The question is, where did they get it?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: PUTS THE APE IN SKYSCRAPER. I mean, that one's a gimme. Over 6,000 people got it right on that clue. Clue number four: HAS THE LADIES IN THE PALM OF HIS HAND. Problematic these days, don't do that King Kong, be respectful! But anyway, 85 people figured it out. Clue number three: AN EXPERT AT CONTROLLING SMALL AIRCRAFT. He's not the most gentle with it, but he does know how to "control" small aircraft. Out of the 4,672 people who guessed on clue number three, ONE person guessed KING KONG!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: I love that they heard "expert at controlling small air" and it just clicked for them. Are they going home with the money? Clue two: CAN BE SALTY AND SWEET. Well, he was sweet to one person and then kind of salty to everyone else—not the nicest monkey there is. No one got it right there. Clue number one: LIGHT TRAVELER. When he came here, no bags! If you guessed that no one got it right on clue number one, you were right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: So, clue number three: AN EXPERT AT CONTROLLING SMALL AIRCRAFT. You're going home with $5,000 all to yourself! That person's name is Sparty Fan. Tonight, literally our best guesser so far.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's go on to round number two. This one's worth $10,000! You know how it works: five clues, 20 seconds, only one best guess. Let us load the crystal ball. Wow, it's beautiful. Clue number one: AT THE TOP OF THE WISH LIST. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. 33,849 players playing live right now. Time's up. "Soo 1791" said "pony"—honestly, that could be my fiance. "Jolly Leo 230" from Lufkin, Texas, said "money." "Dyer Lamb 339" said "name."

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: SEASONS GREETINGS ALL YEAR LONG. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. AT THE TOP OF THE WISH LIST, SEASONS GREETINGS ALL YEAR LONG. It's crazy that I said Santa will not be the answer tonight; it feels like Santa, but it's NOT Santa. Time's up. "Clueless" from Bakersfield, California, said "dreams." Someone from Madison, Wisconsin, said "Dear."

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: ENJOYS A HOT BUBBLE BATH. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. 34,000 people competing for $10,000! Time's up. "Special Nebula" said "Elf"—what websites are you on? Jazelle Trujillo said "Olaf"—I think a hot bubble bath would literally kill Olaf.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: ARRIVES ORDERLY IN MANY SHAPES AND SIZES. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Arrives orderly in many shapes and sizes. 5 seconds left. I hope someone who sent me nice things ends up winning! Time's up. "Dal Duel" said "soap"—interesting. "Sweet Mircat" said "dog." "Vintage Slug" said "car."

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue: PASS THE KETCHUP S'IL VOUS PLAÎT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Did I do the French right? Pass the ketchup, s'il vous plaît. Let's go through them: AT THE TOP OF THE WISH LIST, SEASONS GREETINGS ALL YEAR LONG, ENJOYS A HOT BUBBLE BATH, ARRIVES ORDERLY IN MANY SHAPES AND SIZES, and PASS THE KETCHUP S'IL VOUS PLAÎT. Time is up!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let us see what is in the crystal ball. The correct answer was FRENCH FRIES! At the beginning of the episode when I said this whole episode is French-themed, I thought I ruined it! Apparently no one remembered that. Clue number five: PASS THE KETCHUP S'IL VOUS PLAÎT. Well, pass the ketchup please... French and fries. 1,800 people got it right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: ARRIVES ORDERLY IN MANY SHAPES AND SIZES. 9 people got it right. Clue number three: ENJOYS A HOT BUBBLE BATH. Well, it's a hot oil bubble bath, but it's a bubble bath! 5,000 people guessed, and 2 people got it right! Clue number two: SEASONS GREETINGS ALL YEAR LONG. Well, they're "seasoned" no matter the time of year. Zero people got it right. Clue number one: AT THE TOP OF THE WISH LIST. Well, they're one of the most sellable foods; they're always at the top of the menu in my opinion. Zero people got it right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Which means the two people who got it right on clue number three are getting $5,000 each! Congratulations to Miss Sunshine 6 and DD Systems! That's amazing.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That's it for this special "Parisian" episode of Best Guess Live! Congratulations again to all of our winners. We're here every weekday at 5:00 Pacific, 8:00 Eastern. Don't forget that next week we're giving away a million dollars! You can win up to a million dollars each day. Tomorrow, the great Howie Mandel will be here with another $15,000 up for grabs. Au revoir!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! No, Hunter didn't shave his head—I'm Howie Mandel. Happy Friday! It's prime time on the East Coast, drive time on the West Coast, and winning time absolutely everywhere. It's also fight time right here on Netflix—the Jake Paul vs. Anthony Joshua fight is starting soon. So before you go watch that battle, let's have a battle of our own right here! I am so excited; this is my first time hosting by myself since we launched. It'll be nice to get some reps in before we play for a million dollars next week!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Wait, wait, wait... we're playing for a million? Really? A million dollars next week! Talk about a holiday surprise. This is amazing. A million dollars! Talk about a deal—see what I did there? And you don't have to win a million dollars; it's all up to you people. It’s not a lottery, it’s easy! Am I allowed to play? Well, listen to this: not only is a million dollars up for grabs next week, but I have $15,000 to put on the table right now. And I don't even have a table, so hopefully I put it into your pocket.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here's how it works: Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds. Each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load the crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I will give you five clues. You get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer, and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money, and if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you will win the entire prize pot for that round. Can you imagine next week if somebody won a million? Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Remember, if possible, make a video of you and your friends playing Best Guest Live and send it to us or tag us on your socials with #BestGuessLive.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: All right, let's start giving away some money. Round one is worth $5,000. Please, can somebody load the crystal ball for me for that first secret item? Wow, ask and ye shall receive. What could it be? All right, here is your first clue: MIXED EMOTIONS. 20 seconds on the clock starts right now! Mixed emotions... you know, if it was me, I would just guess because that's why it's called Best Guess. Take a wild guess because the chances are the people that get it on this clue will probably win more money than people who figure it out by the fifth clue.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Time is up! Did we get any wild guesses? We did! "A Buck" said "emoji"—mixed emotions, emojis are all different emotions. Somebody said "love." Is love a mixed emotion? Maddie Mama said "this." I don't get some of your answers, but you could be right because I'm stupid.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your second clue: TURN UP THE VOLUME. 20 seconds on the clock starts right now. So the first clue was mixed emotions, and 5,000 of you submitted answers. That's pretty good. Turn up the volume... Dave Stevia, you said "music"—that makes sense. Mixed emotions could be love songs, emotional songs. Kaylee E25 said "sports."

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your third clue: THE LIFE OF ITS OWN PARTY. The clock starts now. Just going over my clues so far: number one was mixed emotions, the second clue was turn up the volume. KLA689 said "DJ," ABS 362 said "radio." You guys are all in the same line. 4,340 of you submitted on the third clue.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your fourth clue: UNATTRACTIVE IN A SAUNA. Somebody been looking at me? The clock starts now! Unattractive in a sauna... wow, this is pretty good. You know, we've already got almost 35,000 people playing right now! That is amazing. When I was here on the first night, we had 9,000 players. More and more of you are joining. "Clean Captain 247" said "Olaf" and "Catamal" said "hair." It's not attractive in the sauna.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: And now finally, your fifth clue: THE FRUITCAKE OF HOLIDAY ATTIRE. Clock starts now! The fruitcake of holiday attire. You said a "bow tie"—you don't like bow ties? You're against bow ties? I know that a bow tie is unattractive in a sauna, but you don't know who's going to be there! These are all interesting guesses; I love seeing what you do and I love that you try. There is no bad guess. You don't have to be smart, you don't have to be educated. The best guess that comes in first could walk away—this could be your living! All right, let's see what it is.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Open that crystal ball, please! An UGLY SWEATER! That makes sense, right? The fruitcake of holiday attire. It is the fruitcake—not the worst food, but the worst thing you can wear. 7,731 of you submitted; 4,000 of you—more than half—got it right on the fifth clue.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: But did anybody get it before that? Well, this is pretty amazing. "Unattractive in a sauna"—I get it. Unattractive... what's another word for unattractive? Ugly. And what happens in a sauna? You sweat. An UGLY SWEATER! 3,854 of you guessed on clue four; only seven got it correct! Wow, wow, wow.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: I want to see if anybody got a wild guess on the first clue: "Mixed emotions." People often have very mixed emotions about ugly sweaters—"Oh, thank you... or isn't this great?" right? Because it's really ugly. 5,049 of you guessed, and none of you got it right. Clue number two was "turn up the volume"—that makes sense, UGLY SWEATERS are very loud and garish! 5,500 people guessed; not one of you got it right. The only clue I haven't talked about is clue number three: "the life of its own party"—as in an ugly sweater party is always around the holidays! Seven of you guessed it right on clue four; on clue three, no one guessed it right.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Seven of you are splitting the $5,000! I love that. Every one of you is taking home $714.28! Lovely Cat 815, Katherine, Boxy Tummy 247, Grim Bizaar 475—you guys are amazing! Ready for round two? The prize is now going up to $10,000!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Five clues, 20 seconds, only one best guess. Let's load that crystal ball. Here we go, clue number one: NOT A GIRLY BEVERAGE. Clock starts now. Not a girly beverage... interesting. Somebody said "matcha"—that's not girly! "Hanu Dude" said "eggnog," "Enviousness" said "eggnog" also. Eggnog is not for the ladies, okay.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your second clue: SPLIT PERSONALITY. Clock starts now! First clue was not a girly beverage. Somebody said "moonshine," "Helilia" said "moonshine," and "JD404" from Deerfield Beach—I love Deerfield Beach! "Monster" said "a monster" and "Sage Moore 533" said "a joker."

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your third clue: DO YOU ALREADY KNOW. Clock starts now. Not a girly beverage, split personality, and "do you already know." There's about 35,000 people here playing. "Tint the Dragon" said "coffee," "Guy Character" said "blackjack," and "Dread Honeydew 835" said "tea."

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your fourth clue: A TEAM PLAYER. Clock starts now. Not a girly beverage, split personality, do you already know, and finally a team player. "Kindly K01" said "football," "November" said "Fanta," "Kitten 978" said "poker." I love seeing what you're doing. I want to see people playing—I will post if I see the hashtag and you tag me and Netflix!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Finally, the final clue: I PITY THE FOOL WHO DOESN'T GET THIS. The clock starts now! I pity the fool who doesn't get this. Not a girly beverage, split personality, do you already know, a team player, and my final clue: I pity the fool who doesn't get this. I think I know now! Wow, people are submitting like crazy. 9,500 people got it on this final clue.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Let's open the crystal ball! Look who it is: MR. T! It all makes sense now, doesn't it? "I pity the fool"—is that a good impression? I pity the fool who doesn't get this! That was his catchphrase. 5,269 of you got that correct on clue five.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Now you got to listen to clue four: the way I said it! I didn't say "a team player," I said "A-Team player"—as in The A-Team! See that? 1,300 got that correct on clue four. Clue number three was "do you already know"—MR. T has that world-famous mohawk, which is the "do"—as in "hairdo you already know"! You may think that's tough, but 3,616 people guessed; one person got it! One person got it on that! They got the clue! That's what this is about—just take wild guesses.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Let's look at the first clue: "not a girly beverage." MR. T... beverage as in "tea," right? "Mister" as in not girly—MR. T! 4,564 people guessed on that first clue; zero got it right. Clue number two: "split personality." He is a tough guy who's also known for being really sweet to kids; he's soft and he's hard. 4,105 guessed, but nobody got it right. So that one guess on "do you already know" on clue number three is taking the entire $10,000!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Our best guesser tonight is Flowery Jumper 326! Flowery Jumper, I hope you recorded this; I want to see you online! $10,000 just for playing a game and having fun!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That is it for this episode of Best Guest Live! Congratulations again to all the winners. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. And don't forget that next week we're going to play for—I'm getting close because I don't believe this—$1 million! It's going to be absolutely bananas. So tell all your friends and family to come and get their piece of our huge jackpot. Have a great weekend! Goodbye everybody!

lines.text: Here's how the show works: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with the secret item. Now, it could be anything. Then i'll give you five clues. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! Yeah, that's right, I'm your host, Hunter March. Great news everybody—it's Monday! The long, arduous weekend is finally over. Sure, the weekend did have some amazing NFL playoff games and the Golden Globe ceremony, which was cool, and a lot of us didn't have to go to work, but the weekend is always the worst part of the week because there's no Best Guess Live, right? Can't win money on the weekends.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: But now, actually, you have 10 chances to take our money before you have to suffer through another weekend. Thank god it's Monday! That's what I always say. Vote for more Mondays! And I have some incredible news that will make you even happier: starting this week, every Wednesday on Best Guess Live is going to be Wild Wednesday! What makes it wild, you ask? So I'll tell you what makes it wild: well, we're going to double the jackpots on Wednesday! Every Wednesday we will be playing for $30,000! Just a little something from Netflix to help you get over the midweek blues.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Get excited! But first, let's get this magnificent Monday underway. Here's how the game works: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with the secret item. Now it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round.
