lines.text: Clue number three: ALL ABOUT THE BOTTOM LINE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. 100 PROOF, MOMENT OF REFLECTION, ALL ABOUT THE BOTTOM LINE. 10 seconds left. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Fallen Clown 4" said limbo. "Jimmy Jam" said twerking.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: A FLICK OF THE WRIST. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. 13 seconds. "Jimmy Jam" feels like someone cool. 5 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Lola Granola" said flask. "I Am The Fish" said fishing—this person has been waiting their whole life to put this answer. "Jazzy Pancake" put orchestra conductor.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue: ENOUGH TALK IT'S YOUR JOHN HANCOCK. Your final chance at $10,000. 20 seconds starts now. 100 PROOF, MOMENT OF REFLECTION, ALL ABOUT THE BOTTOM LINE, A FLICK OF THE WRIST, ENOUGH TALK IT'S YOUR JOHN HANCOCK. Time is up. Let's find out what's inside that crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was a SIGNATURE.
Clue 5: "ENOUGH TALK IT'S YOUR JOHN HANCOCK." John Hancock is another way of saying SIGNATURE, made famous by the Declaration of Independence. 8,613 got it right.
Clue 4: "A FLICK OF THE WRIST." Well, that is the motion you make when you're signing your SIGNATURE. 1,124 got it right.
Clue 3: "ALL ABOUT THE BOTTOM LINE." Well, your SIGNATURE goes where? Right on the bottom line. 545 got it right.
Clue 2: "MOMENT OF REFLECTION." Your SIGNATURE is you, you know? So when you sign your SIGNATURE, you're literally looking at yourself. One person got it right.
Clue 1: "100 PROOF." Your SIGNATURE is 100% proof that you've signed off on something. Zero got it right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: One person who guessed SIGNATURE on clue number two has gone home with $10,000! Amazing! That person's name is Jon!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for this edition of Best Guest Live! Congratulations again to all of our winners. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Love Week continues tomorrow with Wild Wednesday. Come back tomorrow and don't forget to bring your best guest! Good night!

clues: ALWAYS YEARNING TO TIE THE KNOT
START BY POKING ME IN THE EYE-LET
STRING FOR YOUR FOOTWEAR TO MAKE IT STAY PUT
UP CLOSE AND HAIRY IF YOU DARE-Y
PAY FOR YOUR KIDS TO BE THE GOAT AT TOUCHING GOATS

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! That's right, I'm your host Hunter March. There is a lot going on today. First of all, an incredible new season of Love is Blind is premiering on Netflix as we speak. But before you break out the golden goblets, by the way, and start taking bets on which contestant is going to literally make a run for it, hang with us for just a while.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: We are smack dab in the middle of Love Week. And to top it all off, it's Wild Wednesday! I'm throwing stuff, that's how wild it is! Not only are we celebrating love, but we're getting wild by doubling the prize pots to a total of $30,000! That's right. They weren't ready.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: If that's not love, I don't know what is. I know what else you guys would love right now: for me to get on with it. Your wish is my command. Here are the rules. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds. Each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Then I'll give you five clues. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer. But you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer, and we do not accept typos.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you'll win that entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Remember, if possible, make a video of you and or your friends playing Best Guest Live. Send it to us at Netflix Games, at Hunter March, at Howie Mandel on our socials with the hashtag #bestguestlive.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: With that being said, let's win some money! Round one is worth $10,000. Let us load the crystal ball with the first secret item. It was a battle: who was going to win, Wild Wednesdays or Love Week? Turns out it was Wild Wednesdays.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here comes clue number one: SINGLE AND READY TO MINGLE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. SINGLE AND READY TO MINGLE. Maybe that's you, maybe you're single and ready to mingle. 5 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one was SINGLE AND READY TO MINGLE. "Buggy Peep 16" said Batman. "Zack Attack 701" said my uncle. If the answer is Zach's uncle, I am sorry to everyone playing.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: OPPOSITES ATTRACT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. OPPOSITES ATTRACT. SINGLE AND READY TO MINGLE. I don't know what Zach's uncle looks like, so it's hard for me to tell if this clue applies to him at all. 3 seconds left. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: OPPOSITES ATTRACT. "Da B" from Homestead, Florida said bread. Maybe because it's like the opposite ends of the bread? "Salty Candy 042" said magnet. Maybe because of the poles on either side?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: ALWAYS YEARNING TO TIE THE KNOT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. ALWAYS YEARNING TO TIE THE KNOT. This sounds like SINGLE AND READY TO MINGLE, OPPOSITES ATTRACT. 3 seconds left. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "The Sun God" from Mountain Home, Arkansas said electron. "Emily 555" from Yukon, Pennsylvania said Bachelor. Not me, I'm just saying I know people.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: START BY POKING ME IN THE EYE LET. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I do wonder how many people just put love on clue number one and crossed their fingers. At this point love itself might be out. 3 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Noisy Library 321" said The Three Stooges. "Unicorn 137" from Corpus Christi said ring. "Sacred Jazz" said belt—another eyelet.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: STRING FOR YOUR FOOTWEAR TO MAKE IT STAY PUT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. We made it rhyme. SINGLE AND READY TO MINGLE, OPPOSITES ATTRACT, ALWAYS YEARNING TO TIE THE KNOT, START BY POKING ME IN THE EYE LET, STRING FOR YOUR FOOTWEAR TO MAKE IT STAY PUT. Time is up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was a SHOELACE. The question is who got it right on what clue.
Clue 5: "STRING FOR YOUR FOOTWEAR TO MAKE IT STAY PUT." It's a good clue. 6,679 got it right.
Clue 4: "START BY POKING ME IN THE EYE LET." Well, you lace up your shoes by inserting them into the eyelets. 3,596 got it right.
Clue 3: "ALWAYS YEARNING TO TIE THE KNOT." That one's obvious; they're always looking to tie a knot. 2,950 got it right.
Clue 2: "OPPOSITES ATTRACT." Well, it starts on opposite sides of your shoe and then ends up intertwined and the opposite sides of the lace itself. 12 people got it right.
Clue 1: "SINGLE AND READY TO MINGLE." Well, it is a single piece of material that mingles with itself. That's why the answer is SHOELACE.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 11,570 people guessed on clue number one and 17 got it right! Which means 17 people are splitting $10,000—$588.23! Congratulations to DJ BJ CJ3, Longhorn 82, Right Putter 87, and Nichollet 92.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The prize right now is going to double to $20,000. It's a lot of money. Let us load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here is your first clue. Clue number one: REACH FOR THE STARS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. $20,000! On Wednesdays we do it one and a half times. 3 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: REACH FOR THE STARS was clue number one. "Hono Sosa" said yoga. "Harsh Yarn 394" said yawn.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: APPETIZER ANYONE? 20 seconds on the clock starts now. APPETIZER ANYONE? There's a question mark at the end. 5 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Alpha Ash 4" from Winchester, New Hampshire said fondue. "Big Pastry" said TMZ.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: WILDLY INTERACTIVE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. REACH FOR THE STARS, APPETIZER ANYONE, and WILDLY INTERACTIVE. 7 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Mu Kimu" said poker. "Frozen Combo 876" said kite. "Panda Mandy" said kissing. Definitely wildly interactive.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: UP CLOSE AND HAIRY IF YOU DARE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. REACH FOR THE STARS, APPETIZER ANYONE, WILDLY INTERACTIVE, UP CLOSE AND HAIRY IF YOU DARE. 2 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Happy Shoes 021" said waxing. Waxing is wildly interactive, but if it's your appetizer, what's the entree?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: PAY FOR YOUR KIDS TO BE THE GOAT AT TOUCHING GOATS. Your final 20 seconds starts now. Partners in this! REACH FOR THE STARS, APPETIZER ANYONE, WILDLY INTERACTIVE, UP CLOSE AND HAIRY IF YOU DARE, PAY FOR YOUR KIDS TO BE THE GOAT AT TOUCHING GOATS. Let's find out what's been inside this crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was a PETTING ZOO. They're stuffed animals!
Clue 5: "PAY FOR YOUR KIDS TO BE THE GOAT AT TOUCHING GOATS." Well, for a small price you can pay for your kids to pet some interesting creatures, including goats. 10,321 got it right.
Clue 4: "UP CLOSE AND HAIRY IF YOU DARE." I don't want to know what you put besides petting goat or PETTING ZOO. 280 got it right.
Clue 3: "WILDLY INTERACTIVE." You are literally interacting with wild animals; it's great. 83 of them saw a correct answer.
Clue 2: "APPETIZER ANYONE?" Petting zoos almost always give you a snack to give the animals as a treat. "Do you want the snack?" Zero got it right.
Clue 1: "REACH FOR THE STARS." At any zoo, the animals are the stars. And at a PETTING ZOO, you can actually reach out and touch them. REACH FOR THE STARS.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 11,893 people guessed on clue number one and no one got it right! Which means those 83 people are each going home with $240.96!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for this episode of Best Guest Live! We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific, and Love Week continues tomorrow. Congratulations Jim, Frank McKe, Gone Camping, and a bunch more. Don't forget to bring your best guests! Good night!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! I'm your host Howie Mandel. Yes, guess what? It's day four of love week! We are celebrating Valentine's Day and the new season of Love is Blind, and of course we're also celebrating the greatest love of all: the love between a host and his contestants. We have some new ones: "Holy Time 394," I love you! And "Prickly Armor 439," I love you!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: So as an expression of how much I love all of you, I'm offering up $15,000 to be won today. You'd love to win it, I'd love to help. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Then I'm going to give you five clues. And after each clue you're going to have 20 seconds on the clock to submit an answer. But you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It's got to be an exact answer and we don't accept typos.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Everyone that locks in the correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue you will win the entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast but you got to be right. Remember, if possible, make a video of you and your friends playing Best Guest Live and send it to us or tag us on your socials with #bestlive. All right, round one is worth $5,000!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one: $5,000. Load that crystal ball. Here is clue number one: SPECIAL DELIVERY. 20 seconds on the clock starts right now. Have you guys done all your shopping for Valentine's Day? Taking my wife to see a show—it's me at the Saboba Casino because it's free. 3, 2, 1 seconds left. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number one was SPECIAL DELIVERY. "Danny Diamond" said a newborn. "No Boy Man 05" said Amazon.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is clue number two: A RISKY STARTUP. The clock starts now. Your first clue was SPECIAL DELIVERY, 13,000 guesses on that first one. Your second clue is A RISKY STARTUP. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Lenny For You" thoughts: a garage band. "Day 05" said labor.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: IF YOU PULL THE LEVER IT BETTER BE CLEVER. Clock starts now. SPECIAL DELIVERY, A RISKY STARTUP, and clue number three: IF YOU PULL THE LEVER IT BETTER BE CLEVER. Less people are guessing as the clues go on. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Fluffy Concrete 321" said an injector seat. "Michelle 676767" said slots.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: OPENING SCRIPT TO GET A NUMBER OR ZIP. Clock starts now. SPECIAL DELIVERY, A RISKY STARTUP, IF YOU PULL THE LEVER IT BETTER BE CLEVER, and clue number four: OPENING SCRIPT TO GET A NUMBER OR ZIP. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Zany Pyramid 228" said cold call. "Gloomy Rainbow 720" said a job interview. Here's your final clue.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue: CHEESEY FIRST WORDS DECIDE YOUR FATE FOR A DATE. The clock starts now. SPECIAL DELIVERY, A RISKY STARTUP, IF YOU PULL THE LEVER IT BETTER BE CLEVER, OPENING SCRIPT TO GET A NUMBER OR ZIP, and CHEESEY FIRST WORDS DECIDE YOUR FATE FOR A DATE. Time is up. 5,000 people guessed on the final clue.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The answer is PICKUP LINE! And I wanted you to know the answer because you are fine!
Clue 5: "CHEESY FIRST WORDS DECIDE YOUR FATE FOR A DATE." They're often the cringy first words to decide your romantic future. 3,925 of you got it correct.
Clue 4: "OPENING SCRIPT TO GET A NUMBER OR ZIP." If it works out you might get a phone number and if it doesn't... zip, nada, nothing. 2,215 of you got it correct.
Clue 3: "IF YOU PULL THE LEVER IT BETTER BE CLEVER." If you decide to go ahead and use one, it better be witty and charming. 185 of you got it correct.
Clue 2: "A RISKY STARTUP." It is a bold way to open up a conversation with lots of potential peril. Only 97 of you got it correct.
Clue 1: "SPECIAL DELIVERY." It's a line you deliver to someone that you think is special, and it's all about the delivery, right?

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 13,012 of you guessed and 25 of you got it correct! You are splitting the $5,000; each of you are getting $200! Look at all the hearts! That's love!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It is time to play round two. The prize doubles to $10,000! Load that crystal ball. The first clue: GET IN SHAPE. The clock starts now. This is for $10,000!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: GET IN SHAPE. "Matcha Fanatic 555" said Times Square. "Take A Hike You Freak" said Lego.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: SPIN CLASS. The clock starts now. GET IN SHAPE was the first clue. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Dandy Daniel 430" said washing machine school. "Bean Dad" said twister.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here's clue number three: THE POINT IS THE POINT. The clock starts now. GET IN SHAPE, SPIN CLASS, and THE POINT IS THE POINT. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Volatile Hugger 909" said an arrow. "Medium Crow 491" said ballet—you get in shape, you spin, you got to dance on point.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your fourth clue: MOSTLY A NUMBER TWO OPTION. The clock starts now. GET IN SHAPE, SPIN CLASS, THE POINT IS THE POINT, and MOSTLY A NUMBER TWO OPTION. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Best Betsy 211" said fiber. That's going right through me!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue: GRINDS THE EDGE OF YOUR LEAD SO YOU CAN WRITE RIGHT. The clock starts now. GET IN SHAPE, SPIN CLASS, THE POINT IS THE POINT, MOSTLY A NUMBER TWO OPTION, and GRINDS THE EDGE OF YOUR LEAD SO YOU CAN WRITE RIGHT. Time is up. Let's find out what's in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It's a PENCIL SHARPENER.
Clue 5: "GRINDS THE EDGE OF YOUR LEAD SO YOU CAN WRITE RIGHT." It makes your pencil nice and sharp so you can write right. 3,267 of you were correct.
Clue 4: "MOSTLY A NUMBER TWO OPTION." It is usually used on a number two style pencil. 717 of you got it correct.
Clue 3: "THE POINT IS THE POINT." I think you get the point! 536 of you got it correct.
Clue 2: "SPIN CLASS." It spins, that's how it sharpens. And when do you use them most often? In class. One of you got it right.
Clue 1: "GET IN SHAPE." Its sole job is to get the pencils in the right shape.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 12,321 people had their best guess on the first clue, and two of you did get it right! That means you are each splitting $5,000!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That is it for this edition of Best Guest Live! Congratulations again to all of our winners. We are here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. There's only one day left of Love Week, so come back tomorrow! Good night! I wasn't that sharp tonight—I was a little dull!

clues: YOUR MOM AND DAD'S DAD AND MOM ARE THE BOMB
ELECTRONIC BLACK HOLE WHERE CALLERS BEAR THEIR SOUL
A MESSAGE AT THE TONE IF YOU DON'T PICK UP THE PHONE

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! I'm your host Hunter March. Today is an interesting day. Not only is it the last day of Love Week and not only is it our 50th episode, but it's also Friday the 13th! No, no. Listen, traditionally this day is associated with bad luck and being murdered at summer camp. But we're not going to let superstition slow us down around here.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Any Friday 13th or otherwise should be celebrated as another chance to win up to $15,000. So let's turn that bad luck upside down and get this game started. Here's how the game works. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: And then I'll give you five clues. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer but you only get one best guess per round. Not per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we do not accept typos.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue you will win that entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast but you have to be right. Remember, if possible, make a video of you and your friends playing Best Guest Live and send it to us or tag us on your socials with #bestguesslive.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: With that being said, I hope my name helps you guys win more money tonight. Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball with the first secret item.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Here comes clue number one: TOP TIER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Nearly 40,000 people in this game early, I love it. A lot of things are top tier, but the question is what are we talking about. 3 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one was TOP TIER. "Paris Goat" said pearl. "Black Widow LJ" said MVP. "Salty Blueberry" said the good booze.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's go to clue number two: BEEN THERE DONE THAT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. TOP TIER, BEEN THERE DONE THAT. Those are your first two clues. 42,000 plus people playing. 3 seconds left. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: BEEN THERE DONE THAT. "Brandy Nicole 27" from Battle Creek, Michigan said AOL. "Queen of Ash" said wedding. "Frank the Tank" from Dallas, Texas said divorce. I'll be there in Dallas next week.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's go to clue number three: TWO FOR THE AGES. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. TOP TIER, BEEN THERE DONE THAT, TWO FOR THE AGES. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: TWO FOR THE AGES. "Meep 0219" had said wine. "Frank B" said twins.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's move on to clue number four: YOUR TEACHERS TEACHERS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. TOP TIER, BEEN THERE DONE THAT, TWO FOR THE AGES, YOUR TEACHERS TEACHERS. 8 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: YOUR TEACHERS TEACHERS. "Silent Mage" from Newark said the past. "Hollywood017" said mentor. We got one more clue that might rein it in for a lot of people.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: YOUR MOM AND DAD'S DAD AND MOM ARE THE BOMB. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. That clue was a tongue twister! TOP TIER, BEEN THERE DONE THAT, TWO FOR THE AGES, YOUR TEACHERS TEACHERS, and YOUR MOM AND DAD'S DAD AND MOM ARE THE BOMB. Time is up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was GRANDPARENTS. I love my GRANDPARENTS. Love week GRANDPARENTS, I love it.
Clue 5: "YOUR MOM AND DAD'S DAD AND MOM ARE THE BOMB." I think that's self-explanatory. 9,062 got it right.
Clue 4: "YOUR TEACHERS TEACHERS." Well, hopefully they taught your parents and hopefully your parents taught you. They usually are your teachers' teachers. 1,192 got it right.
Clue 3: "TWO FOR THE AGES." Typically they're highly revered and they are old. It's not offensive, empirically true. 69 got it right.
Clue 2: "BEEN THERE DONE THAT." Well, they're obviously very experienced in life. 16 people got it right.
Clue 1: "TOP TIER." They are at the top of the family tree; they're the top tier of the family tree.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 9,412 people guessed on clue number one and zero people got it right! Which means the 16 people who got it on clue number two are going home with $312 each! Congratulations Eagles X4, Blank Scallops 941, Samora BF67, and Ace Dog.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The prize doubles to $10,000! One more chance, $10,000 on the line. Let us load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here's your first clue. Clue number one: GO TO THE STORE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. GO TO THE STORE. Have some fun with this. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: GO TO THE STORE. "JD Dimensions" from Houston, Texas said mall. "Online Hill 733" said list. "Luke But Don't Touch" said Spencer's Gifts.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's go to clue number two: TAKE IT OR LEAVE IT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. GO TO THE STORE, TAKE IT OR LEAVE IT. One second left. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: TAKE IT OR LEAVE IT. "Nadian Fincher" from South Carolina said ring. "Sam on Ride 91" said a penny. Take it or leave it, for sure.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: TALK TO THE HAND. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. GO TO THE STORE, TAKE IT OR LEAVE IT, and then TALK TO THE HAND. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: TALK TO THE HAND. "Happy Biscuit 214" said psychic—maybe a palm reader. "Mimi Ku 0126" said mom.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: ELECTRONIC BLACK HOLE WHERE CALLERS BEAR THEIR SOUL. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. This is going to give it away to some people. ELECTRONIC BLACK HOLE WHERE CALLERS BEAR THEIR SOUL. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ELECTRONIC BLACK HOLE WHERE CALLERS BEAR THEIR SOUL. "Exotic Cougar 611" from Maine said spam. "Mighty Carpet" said talk radio.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: A MESSAGE AT THE TONE IF YOU DON'T PICK UP THE PHONE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. GO TO THE STORE, TAKE IT OR LEAVE IT, TALK TO THE HAND, ELECTRONIC BLACK HOLE WHERE CALLERS BEAR THEIR SOUL, A MESSAGE AT THE TONE IF YOU DON'T PICK UP THE PHONE. Time is up. Let's find out what's in the $10,000 crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was VOICEMAIL. I'm just kidding about sending my grandma to voicemail, she is dead!
Clue 5: "A MESSAGE AT THE TONE IF YOU DON'T PICK UP THE PHONE." You guys get that one. 6,294 people got it right.

Clue 4: "ELECTRONIC BLACK HOLE WHERE CALLERS BEAR THEIR SOUL." Well, you can say whatever is on your mind on a VOICEMAIL, and do we really know where they go? We've all left that voicemail that we bared too much of our soul. 1,256 got it right.
Clue 3: "TALK TO THE HAND." You traditionally hold your phone in your hand when you're leaving a VOICEMAIL, so technically you talked to the hand. One person got it right.
Clue 2: "TAKE IT OR LEAVE IT." Ironically, someone got it right here. You could be receiving a VOICEMAIL or you could be leaving one. Take a voicemail, leave a voicemail. One person got it right.
Clue 1: "GO TO THE STORE." VOICEMAIL though is where your messages get stored. That's the store we're talking about.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 7,829 people guessed on clue number one and zero got it right. Which means the one person who got it on clue number two is getting $10,000! Amazing! That person is Bouncy Anvil 301!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That's it for this edition of Best Guest Live! Congratulations again to all of our winners. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Have a great weekend and a lovely Valentine's Day! Don't forget to bring your best guest! Good night!

clues: DA VINCI'S LEADING LADY OF THE LOUVRE
SOME SAY IT'S DUMB TO CATCH A FREE RIDE WITH YOUR THUMB

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Thank you! Thank you! Thank you! I'm your host Hunter March. Mondays are always great because it means we're kicking off a new week of Best Guess Live. But today is especially great because I get to announce some very exciting news.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Tomorrow, in honor of Lunar New Year, we are going to be blessing our contestants with extra prosperity in the form of bigger prize pots. In round one we'll be playing for $8,888. You think that's great? In round two we're playing for $18,888! Which is very exciting.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: And then as always Wednesday we will be wild, and we're going to double our prize pots to $30,000. Last one... just kidding, not the last one. On Thursday I'll be hosting the show live from Netflix House in Dallas, Texas where we will be giving away the same amount of money we normally do. And it will be our first ever on-location episode. I cannot wait to meet some of you! If you're in Texas or I think Oklahoma's close, drive in, have fun, let's do it!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: First things first though, let's get this Monday underway and put $15,000 up for grabs. Here's how it works. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer, and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money, and if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you'll win that entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball with the first secret item. Tens of thousands of people playing. Here comes clue number one: LOVES TO HANG OUT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: LOVES TO HANG OUT. The game has started. If you're brand new, welcome. If you're a returning player, thank you guys so much. We hope this makes your Monday a little bit more fun as we potentially give you lots and lots of money. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: LOVES TO HANG OUT. "LG LJ Bass" said sloth. "Cheese Jake" said monkey. We'll see if it holds.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: INSANE AMOUNT OF VIEWS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. INSANE AMOUNT OF VIEWS. Let me think about this. All right, I'm racking my brain right now for everything that has an insane amount of views. Oh, and then it clicks! I already know the answer. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Unusual Sky 431" said bat. "J Animation" said socks. "Jolly Molly 623" said Hunter's Instagram—that actually is a good answer.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's go to clue number three: HAS WANDERING EYES. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I really hope it's not my Instagram now. I do feel like bats have weird wonky wandering eyes a little bit, so maybe bats are back in. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Pebbly Squash 862" from Ansonia, Connecticut said owl. "Flakes of the Snow One" said dog. The question is, is dog gonna get you the right answer?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four might reveal a little more: A TRUE RENAISSANCE WOMAN. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Okay, A TRUE RENAISSANCE WOMAN rules out dogs, bats. My Instagram is still in! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Strange Legacy" from Reynoldsburg, Ohio put Medusa. I think she's pre-Renaissance by thousands of years. "Gilded Wine 993" said "I don't know, we'll see if that works." "Watery Pillow" said Beyonce.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue. Clue number five: DA VINCI'S LEADING LADY OF THE LOUVRE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Let's go over all the clues. LOVES TO HANG OUT, INSANE AMOUNT OF VIEWS, HAS WANDERING EYES, A TRUE RENAISSANCE WOMAN, and DA VINCI'S LEADING LADY OF THE LOUVRE. Time's up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was the MONA LISA. Da Vinci's masterpiece. One of the only paintings Da Vinci actually finished.
Clue 5: "DA VINCI'S LEADING LADY OF THE LOUVRE." Well, it's one of the most famous paintings of all time and it's currently hung at the Louvre. 5,921 got it right.
Clue 4: "A TRUE RENAISSANCE WOMAN." Well, MONA LISA is an iconic Renaissance painting. 3,944 got it right.
Clue 3: "HAS WANDERING EYES." An interesting thing about the MONA LISA, her eyes seem to follow you. 566 got it right.
Clue 2: "INSANE AMOUNT OF VIEWS." Well, many people come to see this iconic painting every single year. It's one of the most viewed things ever. 90 people got it right.
Clue 1: "LOVES TO HANG OUT." Well, the painting hangs on the wall of the Louvre.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 13,312 people guessed on clue number one and 13 people got it right! Shelly Teacher Zero, Mad Hatter 1978, The Boss, Drow 82, and more are winning a little over $380!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's play round two. The prize doubles to $10,000. Let's load the crystal ball. Five clues, 20 seconds, only one best guess. Let us begin.

lines.text: Clue number one: ARGUABLY POSITIVE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. ARGUABLY POSITIVE. It's an interesting precursor word there. Time is up. "Shamrock Law" said salad. "Bleak Mixer 821" said emoji.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's go to clue number two: PATIENCE IS A VIRTUE. 20 seconds on the clock starts... now. You see what I did? You had to be a little patient there. That was a test. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Almost 10,000 guesses right there. "Bright Piano 555" said the DMV. "Blessed 0331" said time. "Corn Doggy 4" said yoga.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's move on to clue number three: THE ORIGINAL SIDE HUSTLE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I feel like all I can think about are like the things my dad said he did as a kid to make money. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Bronto Samus" said eBay. "Horn City 603" from Victorville, California said love. "Jane Event 757" said Cha-Cha Slide. That is a good slide hustle.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's go to clue number four: COLD SHOULDERS ON THE SHOULDER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Painting was not Da Vinci's only thing; it took a while to paint it. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Do Know What" said Airbnb. "Sunlit Tide 750" said sales. "Coffee Table Brooks" said dandruff. Clue five will tell all.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: SOME SAY IT'S DUMB TO CATCH A FREE RIDE WITH YOUR THUMB. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Guys, we don't drop the ball when it comes to clue five rhymes! Time's up. Let's see what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was HITCHHIKER. HITCHHIKING. Now let's see how the clues fit.
Clue 5: "SOME SAY IT'S DUMB TO CATCH A FREE RIDE WITH YOUR THUMB." Well, you HITCHHIKER using your thumb and it may not be the best idea. 9,845 got it right.
Clue 4: "COLD SHOULDERS ON THE SHOULDER." Well, you're often rejected when you're HITCHHIKING on the shoulder of the road. 328 got it right.
Clue 3: "THE ORIGINAL SIDE HUSTLE." You have to hustle to HITCHHIKE and you are doing it from off to the side of the road. 12 people got it right.
Clue 2: "PATIENCE IS A VIRTUE." It takes a long time to hitch a ride. Zero got it right.
Clue 1: "ARGUABLY POSITIVE." Well, you stand giving a thumbs up which is positive, but HITCHHIKING is arguably not positive.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 8,288 people guessed on clue number one and no one got it right. Which means the 12 people on clue number three are going home with $833.33 each! Congratulations Boxy Wafer 186, Sure Snail 95, Virtual Reptile 3, and Jackhammer!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That's it for this episode of Best Guess Live! Again, I'm going to be in Dallas this week Thursday. And don't forget that tomorrow we're increasing our prize pots for Lunar New Year! Wednesday will be wild, and Thursday again, Netflix House in Dallas, Texas. Do not forget! Come back to celebrate! Bye-bye!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! How's it going everybody? I'm your host Hunter March. I want to start by wishing everyone out there a very happy Lunar New Year. We hope that this year brings you blessings and prosperity, and we are going to do our best to give you something to celebrate.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Today we are turning our prize pots into prosperity pots. What does that mean? Well, we're blessing them with more money! In round one the prosperity pot is worth $8,888. And in round two we're turning it up to $18,888! It could have been $88,000 but that would have been crazy; that would have been way too much money for a Tuesday.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Anyway, after that tomorrow is Wild Wednesday where we'll be doubling our usual prize pots to $30,000. And then on Thursday I'll be coming to you live from Netflix House in Dallas, Texas for our first ever episode on the road. It's just our way of helping you get the new year off to a great start.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: So let's get the game off to a great start and go over the rules. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds. Each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer. But you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we do not accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you will win that entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast but you have to be right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: And remember, if possible, make a video of you and or your friends playing Best Guest Live. Send it to us or tag us on our socials with hashtag #bestlive. With all that being said, I think it's time we win some money! Round one is worth $8,888. Let's load the crystal ball with the first secret item.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $8,888. Here comes clue number one: WORK IN PROGRESS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. WORK IN PROGRESS. You got four more clues to go. Time is up.

lines.text: Clue number one was WORK IN PROGRESS. "Anoa Iowa" said me. Your username is a work in progress! And then "Sea Hag" also... change your username.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: FOLLOW THE SPEED LIMIT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. WORK IN PROGRESS, FOLLOW THE SPEED LIMIT. That person who put "me" is probably freaking out. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two was FOLLOW THE SPEED LIMIT. "Tucker Martin" said ant. I get FOLLOW THE SPEED LIMIT just cuz ants are kind of slow and they follow behind each other. And then someone said "my dad." What is up with you guys?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: IMPOSSIBLE TO AVOID THE LONG LINES. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. IMPOSSIBLE TO AVOID THE LONG LINES. Ant is back in! Ant came back hard there. Time's up.

lines.text: IMPOSSIBLE TO AVOID THE LONG LINES. "Thick Frame" said DMV. I think we get a DMV in every single episode.

Have you ever seen those videos where ants all cling to each other and then they float across rivers? Someone should put ants right now! No, don't, I'm kidding. Time is up.

lines.text: Clue number four: WHATEVER FLOATS YOUR FLOAT. "Echo Alarm" said vote. "Apple 1313" said a lazy river. That actually really works. Why is it a lazy river? Cuz it's a work in progress.

lines.text: Your final clue, your final chance at this $8,888. Clue number five: FESTIVE PROCESSIONS WITH MARCHING BAND SECTIONS. 20 seconds starts now. Keep in mind if you didn't get it on this one, we're going to have another puzzle worth $18,888 next. Time is up.

lines.text: The correct answer was A PARADE. Now is it ants? No. Is it better than ants? Definitely not. A PARADE though. Let's see how the clues fit.
Clue 5: "FESTIVE PROCESSIONS WITH MARCHING BAND SECTIONS." A A PARADE is very festive and often features marching bands. 5,316 people got it right.
Clue 4: "WHATEVER FLOATS YOUR FLOAT." Parades offer, of course, many different types of floats. 2,664 got it right.
Clue 3: "IMPOSSIBLE TO AVOID THE LONG LINES." The entire nature of a A PARADE is just a very long line. 53 people got it right.
Clue 2: "FOLLOW THE SPEED LIMIT." Parades can only go so fast. Could you imagine an 80 mph A PARADE? Unbelievable. 37 got it right.
Clue 1: "WORK IN PROGRESS." Well, a A PARADE is a lot of people working in union that is constantly making progress; it's moving forward.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 6,063 people guessed on clue number one and exactly 19 people got it right! Each of those 19 people is going home with $467.78! Congratulations to Mama Limp, Sky V Player, and Voodoo Brawl.

lines.text: The prize doubles to $18,888. Let us load the crystal ball. You guys know how it works. Clue number one: LOVES THE NIGHT LIFE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. LOVES THE NIGHT LIFE. Not me personally, I'm out. Don't put me. Time's up.

As a reminder, I'll be in Dallas, Texas tomorrow and Thursday. Time is up. Let's find out who was right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was THE MOON.
Clue 5: "A FLOATING ORB THAT CAN HIDE AND ALTERS THE TIDE." You guys get it. 1,910 people got it.
Clue 4: "RENOWNED FOR SHORT WALKS AND GIANT LEAPS." One small step for man, and a giant leap for mankind. 2,255 got it right.
Clue 3: "NICE VIEW LACKS ATMOSPHERE." It has a beautiful view, but THE MOON has no atmosphere. 6,916 got it right.
Clue 2: "RUN CIRCLES AROUND EVERYONE." Well, THE MOON orbits around the Earth, aka all of us. 4,373 got it right.
Clue 1: "LOVES THE NIGHT LIFE." THE MOON... it's a night bird. I'm a morning bird.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 19,058 people guessed on clue number one and 1,047 people got it right! Because it was $18,888, you're getting $18.04 each!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Well done Jules Green, Las Drama, and Zesty Bath 567! That's it for this edition of Best Guest Live Lunar New Year! Congratulations again to all of our winners. Howie will be here tomorrow for Wild Wednesday when our pots will double to $30,000. And I'll see you on Thursday live from Netflix House in Dallas! Good night! Happy Lunar New Year everybody!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! I'm your host Howie Mandel. Guess what? It's Friday! And it is not just any Friday. This is—listen to me—Everybody Wins Friday! I love it when we do this. I want the most people to win as possible. And you can't have more winners than everybody, right? That's the math. That math works. You can't have more than everybody.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: So everyone who plays in round two is going to win a free two-month subscription to personal online fitness training from iit train. Not a discount—you're getting free for two months. That's a gift! So that comes in round two. This is a great prize. I'll tell you a little more about it later on. All you need to know is that if you submit an answer in round two, it doesn't even have to be right. You will win!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: On top of that, this is unbelievable: we have $15,000 up for grabs. This is going to be a great day for everyone who plays. And here's how you do it. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be absolutely anything.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Then I'll give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think, people! After each clue you will get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. Listen to me. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you are the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you will win the entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Remember, if possible, make a video of you and your friends playing Best Guest Live and send it to us or tag us on your socials with #bestguess. All right, let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That is it for this Everybody Wins edition of Best Guest Live! Congratulations, every one of you has won something! We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Have a great weekend, come back Monday, and don't forget to bring your best guest! Good night!

lines.text: Clue four: IT'S ALL ABOUT YOU. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. PART OF THE PROCESS, FEELING LEFT BEHIND, DIGITAL EXCLUSIVE, and now IT'S ALL ABOUT YOU. What could that be? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Shadowy Otter 935" from Atlanta, Georgia says selfie. That's a good guess, but we got one more clue.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: INK PAD HERE I COME WITH MY POINTER FINGER AND MY THUMB. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. That does narrow it down a lot, like a ton. Who's going to get it? 5 seconds left. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: INK PAD HERE I COME WITH MY POINTER FINGER AND MY THUMB. "Faith Cassabant" said poke. Let's find out what's actually in the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was FINGERPRINT. Yes, it's a tough clue.
Clue 5: "INK PAD HERE I COME WITH MY POINTER FINGER AND MY THUMB." You guys get that one, right? 5,935 got it right.
Clue 4: "IT'S ALL ABOUT YOU." We did that on purpose because it was the FINGERPRINT and they are the way to identify yourself; it is literally you. 202 got it right.
Clue 3: "DIGITAL EXCLUSIVE." Well, they are at the end of your digits and each one is completely unique, aka exclusive. 98 got it right.
Clue 2: "FEELING LEFT BEHIND." Well, whenever you touch something, you leave behind FINGERPRINTs; your feeling is literally left behind. 8 people got it right.
Clue 1: "PART OF THE PROCESS." FINGERPRINTing is part of the arrest process, which is a very tough clue.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 5,849 people guessed on clue number one and two people got it right! god, they might have had some experience. $2,500 each to Jess Reed and one other best guesser!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, the prize doubles to $10,000. Let us load the crystal ball. Mystical, magical, double the money.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: First clue is: THE GFORCE IS WITH YOU. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. THE GFORCE IS WITH YOU. Could just be so many things. 3 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: THE GFORCE IS WITH YOU. "Infant Doggy" said sneeze. "Mustachio Man 94" said watch because of a GeForce watch. "Quick Oven Mitt 54" said Obi-Wan Snoop Dogg.

lines.speaker: Hunter March

lines.text: "bcb Wifey" said airplane. Cheap shots are expensive on an airplane! "A Can of Dan" put in the club. It's a lifestyle.

lines.speaker: Hunter March

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Soft Shroud 260" from Springfield, Kentucky said seat belt. "Stormy Normy 922" said regional theater. My heart goes out to the thespians.

lines.speaker: Hunter March

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "snet 86" said train. "Glowy Earbuds 354" said tow truck. THE GFORCE IS WITH it.

lines.speaker: Hunter March

THE GFORCE IS WITH YOU, CHEAP SHOTS ARE EXPENSIVE, WON'T HAVE YOU ON THE EDGE OF YOUR SEAT, FASTMOVING CARS BUT THEY'RE IN PARK, THRILL RIDE WITH WHEELS USUALLY MADE OF WOOD OR STEEL. Time's up.

Clue 1: "THE GFORCE IS WITH YOU." Because ROLLER COASTERs generate significant G-forces; they accelerate, dive, turn, all of it.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 14,584 people guessed on clue number one and 2,541 got it right! You're going home with $3.93! You deserve it!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Well done Sigh by the Bell and Hurricane Mr 9821! That's it for this edition of Best Guest Live! Tomorrow is going to be extra wild with twice the money and something for everybody. Come back tomorrow and don't forget to bring your best guest! Good night!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! Wow, it is exciting. I'm your host Hunter March. Today is Wild Wednesday and it's going to be one of our wildest Wednesdays yet. Not only are we doubling the prize pots to $30,000, but we also have an incredible voucher for everyone who plays our second puzzle from our friends at Inkind that you won't want to miss.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: By the way, if you're not familiar with Inkind, it's an app I've used. I've actually used it, I love it. Gives you discounts at your favorite restaurants. Anyway, we'll get to that, but make sure you play our second puzzle. I'll tell you more about the voucher later on. For now, let's start this game and get down to business.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here's the fine print. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer, and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you will win the entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: And remember, if possible, make a video of you and your friends playing Best Guest Live. I got so many videos yesterday; you guys were so excited and then a little less excited once we found out how much, but I appreciate the videos nonetheless. #bestlive, @Hunter, @Netflixgames. We love it! With all that being said, let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $10,000. Let's load the crystal ball with the first secret item. All right, Wild Wednesday crystal ball. Here we go. So here comes clue number one: 24. 20 seconds starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 24. Okay, for those of you that are new to the game, by the way, over 40,000 people already logged in. I love that some people come late every day. They're like "Listen, I want the challenge, game's too easy. Let me miss the first three clues." 24 is the first clue. Good luck. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Big Den 948" said gold. 24 karat, it's a good guess. "Breezy Jet 819" from Lynwood, California put Kobe; it's definitely a California answer. "Jimmy Flapjacks" said crayons.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: I'LL BE BACK. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I did not mean to say that with a little bit of an accent. Ignore that, that is not a part of the clue. I'll be back. 3 seconds left. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: I'LL BE BACK. "Chloe Quinn 18" said Target. "Arie Schwarzfelder"—I think that might be one of the worst misspellings we've ever had for what is not the correct answer anyway.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: THE DAY IS YOUNG. All right, 20 seconds on the clock starts now. 24, I'LL BE BACK, THE DAY IS YOUNG. I've heard about the night and its youth, but the day being young? 5 seconds left. Can the day rent a car? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THE DAY IS YOUNG. "Jaw Fixer" said sunrise. "Jean Street" said waffles—that is not a good guess. Maybe because it's a breakfast food?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: ALWAYS ARRIVES WITH A PLUS ONE. 20 seconds and the clock starts now. Does a sunrise have a plus one? I guess the sun, but that kind of is the rise. If the sunrise ever does come with a plus one, like a second sun, we're all in so much trouble. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ALWAYS ARRIVES WITH A PLUS ONE. "Ants in My Lance" said equation. Always arrives with a plus one, that's a good guess.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: A 29TH DAY THAT WILL OCCUR EVERY FOURTH CALENDAR. 20 seconds starts now. I promise rhymes on clue fives. A 29TH DAY THAT WILL OCCUR EVERY FOURTH CALENDAR. Time is up. Let's find out what's inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: It is FEB 29. LEAP YEAR baby! FEB 29. It's a very good puzzle.
Clue 5: "A 29TH DAY THAT WILL OCCUR EVERY FOURTH CALENDAR." You guys get it. It comes every four years. 8,648 got it right.
Clue 4: "ALWAYS ARRIVES WITH A PLUS ONE." Well, whenever leap year comes, it brings one extra day with it. 120 got it right.
Clue 3: "THE DAY IS YOUNG." Because of leap year, the 29th day of February is always four times as young as any other day of the year. It's lived a quarter of the life. 135 got it right.
Clue 2: "I'LL BE BACK." It leaves us but always comes back every four years. 86 got it right.
Clue 1: "24." Well, there are 24 hours in a day, and those 24 hours are what make a leap year. Just those 24 hours turn into a leap year.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 7,560 people guessed on clue number one and 12 people got it right! $833 to each of those 12 people! Mrs Bunny, Trina, Leafy Orc, and Ecto Amber, congratulations!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two is worth $20,000. Five clues, 20 seconds, only one best guess. Let us load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here's your first clue: CAN GET TESTY. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. CAN GET TESTY. These answers could be interesting. 5 seconds. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: CAN GET TESTY. "Ry Master 11" said Batman. "John L Hart" said baby. "Grape Shelly" said my wife. Good luck tonight, "Grape Shelly."

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: PHOTO FINISH. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. CAN GET TESTY, PHOTO FINISH. I've seen your tweets at me; some of you can get testy, but PHOTO FINISH rules it out. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: PHOTO FINISH. "Florugo" from Antioch, Illinois said Scandron. "Just Jeremy 95" said sepia. CPI like sepia tone? It's a photo finish.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: EYE EXAM BUT NO DOCTOR. 20 seconds starts now. CAN GET TESTY, PHOTO FINISH, EYE EXAM BUT NO DOCTOR. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Pickle Juice 33" from Hallstead said MRI. "Gnarly Doily" said police lineup. Seeing who's there, who's not there.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU. CAN GET TESTY, PHOTO FINISH, EYE EXAM BUT NO DOCTOR, THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU. 5 seconds left. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU. "Glowy Earbuds 354" said Krispy Kreme. Someone else said potato.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: WHERE ONE MUST GO FOR A DRIVER'S LICENSE TO SHOW. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Good luck ladies and gentlemen. WHERE ONE MUST GO FOR A DRIVER'S LICENSE TO SHOW, THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU, EYE EXAM BUT NO DOCTOR, PHOTO FINISH, CAN GET TESTY. Time's up. Let's find out what's inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was the DMV. Yay, the DMV! Everyone's favorite place.
Clue 5: "WHERE ONE MUST GO FOR A DRIVER'S LICENSE TO SHOW." Well, that one was kind of a gimme. 8,038 people got it right.
Clue 4: "THE WEIGHT WILL WEIGH ON YOU." Well, the waiting time is notorious at the DMV and it really does take a toll on you, especially with those chairs. 2,538 got it right.
Clue 3: "EYE EXAM BUT NO DOCTOR." You have to pass a vision exam every so often, but it is not exactly a doctor's office. 1,536 got it right.
Clue 2: "PHOTO FINISH." You finish your visit by taking a photo when you're getting your license done. 458 people got it right.
Clue 1: "CAN GET TESTY." Well, you often have to take a test while there. And people aren't always in the best mood and can be testy. It's testy while it's testy.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 9,242 people took a best guess on clue one and exactly 163 people got it right! You're going home with $122.69!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to Fledge Mholland, Tiny Pegasus, and 161 others! A special thanks to our friends at Inkind for today's fantastic offer. Check your win history to get your voucher in the app. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Come back tomorrow! Good night!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! I'm your host Howie Mandel. Hey hey hey hey hey. Sit down, sit down everybody! Before we get started on today's puzzles we have to celebrate an important milestone. As of yesterday, listen to this: Best Guest Live players have won unbelievably over $2 million!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That's incredible! Just for thinking outside the box and typing in correct answers, we have put $2 million into the pockets of you, our players, and you seem to be thrilled about it. Congratulations again to all those winners, and let's add another $15,000 to the total right now. Here's how this works.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I will give you five clues. Remember, the clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think, people!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: After each clue I'll give you 20 seconds on the clock to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Everyone that locks in the correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue you will win the entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast but you have to be right. Remember, if possible, make a video. You just saw what we do with it: we share it! All right, let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one: $5,000. Load that crystal ball with the first secret item. What could it be? Well, here comes the clues to help you guess.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number one: A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE. The clock starts now. 20 seconds is on the clock. A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE. I can't believe we've already given $2 million. "Taylor Maid 78" says an exorcism. "Silent Zone 258" said yoga. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here's your second clue: SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS. And the clock starts now. So the first clue was A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE, the second clue was SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS. Think about that. Almost 10,000 submissions. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Moltton Tricycle 545" said detention. You don't volunteer for detention. "Fave Moonfall 93" said fishing. I have a friend who thinks it's spiritual and I've never volunteered to go with him.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here's your third clue: BREATHLESS ANTICIPATION. The clock starts now. A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE, SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS, and your third clue is BREATHLESS ANTICIPATION. "Tori Wines 760" said allergies. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Tori Wines" said allergies. Well, you don't volunteer for allergies. You could get breathless because you can't breathe.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is clue number four: COMMON ISSUE GRAB A TISSUE. And the clock starts now. Clue number one was A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE, clue number two was SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS, clue number three was BREATHLESS ANTICIPATION, and the last clue I gave you was COMMON ISSUE GRAB A TISSUE. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Pixel Princess 003" said the flu. "Boats Shoe Gary 112" said getting dumped. Is that spiritual? I know what you're thinking!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number five: THERE IT GOES AN INVOLUNTARY EXPULSION FROM THE NOSE. And the clock starts now. That was your final clue for $5,000. Clue one was A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE, clue two was SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS, clue three was BREATHLESS ANTICIPATION, clue four was COMMON ISSUE GRAB A TISSUE, and the final clue was THERE IT GOES AN INVOLUNTARY EXPULSION FROM THE NOSE. Time is up. Over 42,000 guesses are in.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It is a SNEEZE. I'm a germaphobe. Let's find out who is taking home the $5,000.
Clue 5: "THERE IT GOES AN INVOLUNTARY EXPULSION FROM THE NOSE." That's what a SNEEZE is. 5,539 of you were correct.
Clue 4: "COMMON ISSUE GRAB A TISSUE." It's an issue that comes with the common cold. You are going to want to grab a tissue. 6,994 of you got that correct.
Clue 3: "BREATHLESS ANTICIPATION." You know that feeling when you have to SNEEZE? 238 of you guessed correctly.
Clue 2: "SORRY NO VOLUNTEERS." You don't really volunteer to SNEEZE; you can't control it. 10 of you got it correct.
Clue 1: "A SPIRITUAL EXPERIENCE." But what happens after you SNEEZE? What do people say to you? Bless you! That's spiritual.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 9,825 of you guessed on that first clue and 38 of you got it correct! You are each splitting $131.57!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Let's play round two. The prize doubles to $10,000! Let's load that crystal ball one more time. It's fully loaded now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your first clue. First clue: INCOMING. And the clock starts now. You heard me: INCOMING! Time is up. "Delicate Anvil 535" said cannonball. "Joel 037" said a toilet.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your second clue: MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER. The clock starts now. First clue INCOMING, second clue MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER. Over 13,500 guesses on the first one. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Tiny Helicopter 645" says Tinder. "Luffy" said dynamite. How do you come up with these handles?

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here's your third clue: COMES AND GOES ON OCCASION. The clock starts now. So the first clue is INCOMING, the second clue is MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER, and my third clue was COMES AND GOES ON OCCASION. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Cute Cat 202" says birthday cake. "Dark Raven 666" says email.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: MADE FROM ONE PART OF A TREE NAMED FOR ANOTHER. And the clock starts now. That's a good riddle. INCOMING, MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER, COMES AND GOES ON OCCASION, and MADE FROM ONE PART OF A TREE NAMED FOR ANOTHER. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: "Littlefoot 88" said a ring. "Glowy Earbuds 34" said root beer. I'm confused by you!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your final clue for $10,000: A NEEDED EXTENSION WHEN DINING WITH EXTENDED FAMILY. The clock starts now. INCOMING, MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER, COMES AND GOES ON OCCASION, MADE FROM ONE PART OF A TREE NAMED FOR ANOTHER, and the final clue A NEEDED EXTENSION WHEN DINING WITH EXTENDED FAMILY. Time is up. Let's find out what is in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It's a TABLE LEAF. A TABLE LEAF! Randy just handed me a TABLE LEAF.
Clue 5: "A NEEDED EXTENSION WHEN DINING WITH EXTENDED FAMILY." You always got to make room for the leaf. 5,329 of you got it right.
Clue 4: "MADE FROM ONE PART OF A TREE NAMED FOR ANOTHER." Tables are often made from wood, but it's named after what? The leaf! 204 of you got it correct.
Clue 3: "COMES AND GOES ON OCCASION." When you have a special occasion it comes out; when it's over it goes away. Zero people figured it out.
Clue 2: "MATCHMAKER MATCHMAKER." For it to fit you got to match one side with the other. It's about matching this side with that side of the table. Nobody got it right.
Clue 1: "INCOMING." There's always somebody incoming; you got to bring this in so that everybody can dine.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 13,496 of you guessed on clue number one and every one of you was wrong! So that means the earliest guesses were on the fourth clue. 204 of you are splitting $10,000! Each of you wins $49.01!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That's it for this edition of Best Guest Live! Congratulations again to all of our winners. We are here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Hunter will be here next time to help close out the week. Don't forget to bring your best guest! Good night!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! Hello everybody! How are we doing? I’m your host Hunter March. It is Friday and we have yet another great deal for everyone who plays our second round today. Think of it as a bonus. That’s just our way of saying thank you for being a part of the Best Guest Live community. Or since I’m always sending you money, I should call you the Best Guest Live family.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Today’s deal is from RDX Sports, the standard setters in performance and innovation for combat sports. So if you are into anything from yoga to Brazilian jiu-jitsu to MMA—and I know that is almost all of our audience—you’ll want to stick around for that deal. And as always, we also have $15,000 up for grabs.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think, keep you on your feet!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you’ll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. So for everyone that didn’t know how to spell TABLE LEAF or didn’t know what it was yesterday, that’s on you! Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: BURSTING WITH LIFE BUT CAN'T MOVE. "Show Me Money 2" said tree. "Schnaples" said seed. "Just In Time" said lava lamp.

FAMOUS UNDERWORLD FIGURE, BURSTING WITH LIFE BUT CAN'T MOVE, WHERE CHECKMATE MEANS INSPECT YOUR DIVE BUDDY, A LAND DOWN UNDER, and CORALS GALORE THAT SHIELD THE AUSSIE SHORE. Time's up. lines.text: The answer was the GREAT BARRIER REEF. Of course it was!

Clue 4: "A LAND DOWN UNDER." It's a whole landscape just underneath Australia. 93 got it right.

Clue 3: "WHERE CHECKMATE MEANS INSPECT YOUR DIVE BUDDY." Well, you check your buddy's gear before you dive, and Australians call each other "mate." I'll do the numbers in an Aussie accent: 349 got it right! Crikey!
Clue 2: "BURSTING WITH LIFE BUT CAN'T MOVE." The GREAT BARRIER REEF is home to lots of life and is alive itself, but does not move. 131 got it right.
Clue 1: "FAMOUS UNDERWORLD FIGURE." Well, it's very famous and is an entirely different ecosystem under the ocean than ours.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 13,295 people guessed on clue number one and 15 people got it right! Each of those 15 people is going home with $333.33! Congratulations to Shady, Amy, Mama Fran, and Violet Fiddle!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, the prize doubles to $10,000! Everyone who submits an answer gets a $20 digital gift card from RDX Sports. Load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: INSIDER TRADING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. $10,000 on the line! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: INSIDER TRADING. "Chubby Oatmeal 843" said crypto. "Get Some Now" said Pokemon—trading things inside packets. "Choosy Pickle" said Martha Stewart. We've forgiven her!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: THE SUM OF ITS PARTS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Sum of its parts... could be a lot of things. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THE SUM OF ITS PARTS. "Ned 512" said everything. "Vulks" said atom. "Yarn Lover" said puzzle.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: A LEGENDARY GIFT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Over 41,000 people playing right now. This could change someone's life today. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: A LEGENDARY GIFT. "Fawn Ammo 250" said kidney—you do become a legend when you give your kidney! "LDB" said DNA.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: WHAT LIFE IS LIKE IN A FOREST. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. "Forest" is spelled a little odd... two R's? Whatever. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: WHAT LIFE IS LIKE IN A FOREST. "Slim Kyle 032" said Woody. "First Flannel 945" said maze.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: AN ASSORTED SELECTION OF COCOA CONFECTIONS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Final chance! INSIDER TRADING, THE SUM OF ITS PARTS, A LEGENDARY GIFT, WHAT LIFE IS LIKE IN A FOREST, and AN ASSORTED SELECTION OF COCOA CONFECTIONS. Time's up. Let's find out what's in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: A BOX OF CHOCOLATES! A very delicious looking BOX OF CHOCOLATES.

Clue 5: "AN ASSORTED SELECTION OF COCOA CONFECTIONS." You guys get it; chocolate's made of cocoa. 415 people got it right. (Correction: Transcript likely meant 4,015 or similar, but I will stick to the stated 12 winners logic).

Clue 4: "WHAT LIFE IS LIKE IN A FOREST." Turns out "Forrest" wasn't spelled wrong because it was Forrest Gump! "Life is like a box of chocolates. You never know what you’re going to get." 963 got it right.
Clue 3: "A LEGENDARY GIFT." It's a great gift, but it has a "legend" inside boxes of chocolate to tell you where each chocolate is located. It is a "legend-dairy" gift! 12 people got it right.
Clue 2: "THE SUM OF ITS PARTS." All of the chocolate inside added together make it a BOX OF CHOCOLATES. Zero got it right.
Clue 1: "INSIDER TRADING." Well, you never know what's inside until you try it, and then oftentimes you want to trade it for a different one.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 10,680 people guessed on clue number one and no one got it right! Which means the 12 people on clue number three are going home with $833.33 each! Congratulations to Huntman 64, The Welts, Maritime Den 741, and Such and Such for Pres!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for Best Guess Live! Thank you to RDX Sports for the great deal. We are here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Have a great weekend and don't forget to bring your best guest! Good night!

clues: AMBUSHED BY THE WEST AND WON
LOVES THE OPPOSITE OF A LOOSE BEGINNING
SUPERSTAR FOR SURE; BROKE RECORDS WITH HER ERA TOUR
WE RHYTHMICALLY SAY AN OATH OF LOYALTY TO THE USA

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! Whoa, almost showed you my tummy there, my bad. I’m your host Hunter March. As always, we have two great puzzles for you today and $15,000 on the line. But I also have a special announcement about tomorrow's show that I'll make a little later.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: For now, just know that we have an amazing guest coming to visit us in the studio, and I've been wrestling with when to tell you who it is... ah, but I've pinned down round two as the best place. Actually, that has nothing to do with wrestling—sorry, I thought we were going to continue rule threes. Anyway, it's a wrestler. Don't worry about it, I'll tell you later.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: But before you solve that puzzle, let's solve the round one puzzle and win some money. Here’s how it works. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we'll load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues. But remember, all the clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit your answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball. Here comes clue number one: BONAFIDE BEST SELLER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: BONAFIDE BEST SELLER. All right, I can think of a lot of things that have sold well, but what is it today? Times up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: BONAFIDE BEST SELLER. "Gina Marie 7" from Tucson said the Bible. "Sloppy Grass 401" said wings. "Jim Dandy" said gasoline. It's an eclectic group!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: AMBUSHED BY THE WEST AND ONE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Let's see if that applies to wings or the Bible. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: AMBUSHED BY THE WEST AND ONE. "Nerdy Suitcase" said the East. "Ruby Doobydoo" said hay—ambushed by the "West-and-one," hay? That’s unbelievably good.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: LOVE'S THE OPPOSITE OF A LOOSE BEGINNING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I don't even know how to take that. I hope this is starting to make sense. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Awful Guesser 1" guessed diamond. "Chatty Monster 777" said sunset—loves the opposite of a loose beginning, an ending?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: ALTERS CLOTHING QUICKLY. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Sunset has never altered my clothing quickly! 5 seconds left. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ALTERS CLOTHING QUICKLY. "Laboo 67 Mango" said zipper. "Misty Noodles" said exotic dancer.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: THIS SUPERSTAR FOR SURE BROKE RECORDS WITH HER TOUR. 20 seconds starts now. I promise rhymes on clue fives. Good luck. If you don't know what this is, I think legally you should! Time's up. Let's find out what's inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was TAYLOR SWIFT! Yes!
Clue 5: "THIS SUPERSTAR FOR SURE BROKE RECORDS WITH HER TOUR." That one was a gimme. 8,321 got it right. 1,700 people don't know how to spell it!
Clue 4: "ALTERS CLOTHING QUICKLY." A Taylor alters clothing, and Swift means quickly. 1,182 got it right.
Clue 3: "LOVE'S THE OPPOSITE OF A LOOSE BEGINNING." Well, she's engaged to a tight end! 687 got it right.
Clue 2: "AMBUSHED BY THE WEST AND ONE." Kanye West ambushed her at the VMAs when she won best female video. 168 got it right.
Clue 1: "BONAFIDE BEST SELLER." 14 of her albums went at least four times platinum.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 38 people got it right on clue one! Those 38 people are going home with $131.57 each! Congratulations to Chalky Mixer 763, Drow Angel23, Novel Avocado, and Brainy Lie.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, we double the prize to $10,000. Load the crystal ball. I told you I was going to announce tomorrow's guest: she is a legend of WWE, a Raw and Smackdown champion—Alexa Bliss will be here tomorrow with Howie Mandel! Now, let's play.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: IN THE BEGINNING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Technically we're in the beginning of the second puzzle. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: IN THE BEGINNING. "Glitchy Chili" said the end. "Weird Enemy 721" said Nike. "Merman 794" said tadpole.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: FEEL THE BEAT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. In the beginning, feel the beat. Nearly 45,000 of you playing, thanks for being here. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Horned Polygon 972" said egg—feel the beat of the egg? "IKumba" said radio.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: STAND AND DELIVER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Clue one: In the beginning. Clue two: Feel the beat. Clue three: Stand and deliver. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Meager Knee 947" said DJ. "Happy Frown 945" said archery—an arrow to the heart.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: AMERICA'S FAVORITE RUN-ON SENTENCE. 20 seconds starts now. For those that don't know, it's like a long sentence... I'm trying hard to explain without sounding dumb. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Intense Critic" said Duncan. "Psychotic Eeyore" said Gloria Estefan.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: WE RHYTHMICALLY SAY AN OATH OF LOYALTY TO THE USA. 20 seconds starts now. I had to get the cadence right to make it rhyme! Time's up. Let's find out what's inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was THE PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE.
Clue 5: "WE RHYTHMICALLY SAY AN OATH OF LOYALTY TO THE USA." You say it with the same memorable rhythm every day in school. 4,031 got it right.
Clue 4: "AMERICA'S FAVORITE RUN-ON SENTENCE." It is punctuated only by commas; it is a run-on sentence. 377 got it right.
Clue 3: "STAND AND DELIVER." You stand up when you deliver it. 43 got it right.
Clue 2: "FEEL THE BEAT." You put your hand over your heart when you recite it and you feel the beat of your heart. Zero got it right.
Clue 1: "IN THE BEGINNING." It is literally the beginning of the school day for many many Americans.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 11,100 people guessed on clue one and zero people got it right. So the 43 people on clue number three are going home with $232.55 each!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to Divine Creek 14, M likes M&M's, and Extreme Peach! That's it for this edition. Alexa Bliss will be here tomorrow! We're here every week at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Good night!

clues: YOU'LL REGRET BECAUSE I NEVER FORGET
WHAT MONTAZUMA NERDS AND THE SITH HAVE IN COMMON
FAMOUS ARENA IN NYC, MARCH 30TH HOST RAW FROM WWE

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! I am your host Howie Mandel. I get so excited every time I am standing right here getting ready to help you win a ton of money, but tonight I am next level excited because we have a very special guest announcer. Take it away!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: She is only the second women's triple crown champion in WWE history, she's a two-time Smackdown Women's Champion, a three-time Raw Women's Champion, and the first woman ever to win both titles. She is an absolute legend of the ring, ladies and gentlemen, Alexa Bliss!

lines.speaker: Alexa Bliss
