lines.text: Hello Best Guest Livers! Oh, it is so great to see you! Thank you for being here. I wouldn't miss it; I love this show.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Well, I love doing it! It's even better with you here. Alexa, turn out the lights... no, I can't, I'm not talking to you. I would imagine there's half the players playing in the dark at home right now. Okay, you've been champion, you've won Money in the Bank, how does being on Best Guess Live stack up to any of those moments?

lines.speaker: Alexa Bliss
lines.text: Well, I'm in the ring, my goal is to pump up the crowd, but here I actually get to help the crowd win money. Pretty cool, right?

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Very cool! In fact, I'm about to make it even cooler. If anyone guesses either puzzle on the first clue, I'm going to hit Howie here with a "Twisted Bliss."

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Oh, the lawyers are saying we can't do that. And I'm saying please, please don't! I'm going to make the clues harder. Well, can I do an "Insult to Injury"? No? "Code Red"? Mrs. Mandel says no. I'm just going to go ahead and read the rules.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load that crystal ball. Here is the first clue: RIGHT BACK AT YOU. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: RIGHT BACK AT YOU. "Miles Away 903" said a yo-yo. That's clever. "Sh 17" said a mirror. "Jump Dogwood 154" said tennis. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: RETINA SWAP. Clock starts now. First clue was Right Back At You. "Moldy Sunshine 033" said contacts. "Moab 508" said wink. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here's your third clue: DELICIOUS OVER ICE. Clock starts now. You thinking? Trying to think. "Kevin on Earth 142" said hockey, which is delicious over ice. Have you ever tasted hockey? Just my puck. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here's the fourth clue: YOU'LL REGRET BECAUSE I NEVER FORGET. Clock starts now. "Jumpy Recliner" thinks an elephant. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: WHAT MONTAZUMA NERDS AND THE SITH HAVE IN COMMON. Clock starts now. Clue one: Right Back At You. Clue two: Retina Swap. Clue three: Delicious Over Ice. Clue four: You'll Regret Because I Never Forget. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The answer is REVENGE.
Clue 5: "WHAT MONTAZUMA NERDS AND THE SITH HAVE IN COMMON." Revenge of the Nerds, Revenge of the Sith, and Montazuma's REVENGE. 3,179 got it correct.
Clue 4: "YOU'LL REGRET BECAUSE I NEVER FORGET." If someone is set on REVENGE, they hold that grudge. 483 got it correct.
Clue 3: "DELICIOUS OVER ICE." Refers to the phrase "REVENGE is a dish best served cold." 53 got it correct.
Clue 2: "RETINA SWAP." An eye for an eye! 5 got it correct.
Clue 1: "RIGHT BACK AT YOU." REVENGE is literally when you get back at somebody.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 15,692 guesses on that first one and 8 people got it right! Each of you is getting $625!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round two is worth $10,000. Let's load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number one: OBVIOUSLY OUT OF SHAPE. Clock starts now. "Milky Dave 854" said a hippopotamus. For a hippo, that's their look! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Second clue: DOESN'T PRODUCE PRODUCE. Clock starts now. "Jolly Genius 734" said a supermarket. They sell it, they don't produce it. "Rusty Level 212" said a zombie. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Third clue: ACRONYMS FEEL RIGHT AT HOME. Clock starts now. "Express Walrest 623" said SOS. "John 84" said IKEA. I always feel out of shape building IKEA furniture. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Fourth clue: AN INCREDIBLE TURN OF EVENTS. Clock starts now. "Twinkle Nose 844" said Elimination Chamber. Still traumatized by that? I was the first one eliminated! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: FAMOUS ARENA IN NYC MARCH 30TH HOST RAW FROM WWE. Clock starts now. A clue and a plug all in one! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The answer is MADISON SQUARE GARDEN.
Clue 5: "FAMOUS ARENA IN NYC MARCH 30TH HOST RAW FROM WWE." Raw takes place at MADISON SQUARE GARDEN on March 30th. 7,728 got it correct.
Clue 4: "AN INCREDIBLE TURN OF EVENTS." MADISON SQUARE GARDEN hosts thousands of different events, sometimes more than one a day—hockey in the afternoon, basketball at night. 9 got it correct.
Clue 3: "ACRONYMS FEEL RIGHT AT HOME." MSG is famous for hosting the Knicks of the NBA, the Rangers of the NHL, and the WWE. 11 got it correct.
Clue 2: "DOESN'T PRODUCE PRODUCE." It's a garden but it doesn't grow fruits or vegetables. 7 got it correct.
Clue 1: "OBVIOUSLY OUT OF SHAPE." It's circular but has "Square" in the name. It is out of its own shape!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 8,455 guesses on that first clue and 2 people got it right! That's $5,000 each! Congratulations to Perry and Mila and G Nova Bourne!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That's it for Best Guest Live! Alexa, anything you want to say?

lines.speaker: Alexa Bliss
lines.text: Sure! Catch RAW every Monday night only on Netflix!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: I approve that message. We'll see you tomorrow! Good night!

clues: COVERED IN POSTERS THAT ARE EASY TO LIKE
THIS CLUE WAS META BEFORE META
STARTS AT BOTTOM AND STAYS THERE
A HOLE IN ONE YOU DON'T CELEBRATE
A RUBBER FAIL WHEN YOUR CAR HITS A NAIL

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! How's it going? I'm your host Hunter March with glasses. This is the game where right answers could mean dramatic money and wrong answers could mean, well, comedy gold. Really, honestly, it's worth it. Speaking of which, I want to take a second to talk to all of the people out there—and there are a lot of you—who send in ridiculous answers just trying to crack me up. You are sacrificing your chance at big money every night just to get in a cheap joke, and I love you for it! Your kids might not like it when they can't go to college, but I love it anyway.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: For all of you out there actually trying to win the money, I have a brand spanking new $15,000 up for grabs tonight. That's right, it's not a Wild Wednesday, but it's still a good Wednesday. $15,000 in my opinion is still pretty wild. Here's how you claim your money if you win. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Then I'll give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think, meant to get your juices going! After each clue you get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you'll win that entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball with the first secret item. What could it be? Mystical, magical. Here comes clue number one: THE DEPARTED. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THE DEPARTED. Interesting. What could it be? I want to say so many things, but I feel like everything is going to be misleading or potentially leading... potentially misleadingly leading. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: THE DEPARTED. "We Woop 818" said Pluto—cuz it was a planet that departed. "Vinnie T" said airport. "Not That Gary" said Blockbuster Video—it's a movie, they've departed.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: NOT A NOVEL NOVEL. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. THE DEPARTED, NOT A NOVEL NOVEL. Interesting, very interesting. You get three more clues if you're new here, and then we go again for $10,000 on the next puzzle. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Baroque Goof" said remake. "Horned Polygon" said egg—maybe because of Game of Thrones? I don't know.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: MANY FRIENDS AREN'T FRIENDS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. MANY FRIENDS AREN'T FRIENDS. That is your third clue. The earlier you answer, the better shot you have at winning some money. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Frothy Bagel" said high school. So true, me and Frothy Bagel were equally losers. "My Tai Bruce" said Leonardo DiCaprio.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: COVERED IN POSTERS THAT ARE EASY TO LIKE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. THE DEPARTED, NOT A NOVEL NOVEL, MANY FRIENDS AREN'T FRIENDS, COVERED IN POSTERS THAT ARE EASY TO LIKE. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Sloppy Rocket 308" says Superman. "Skilled Bear" said I don't know. Not a skilled bear! "Real Right" said magazine.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue for this puzzle: THIS CLUE WAS META BEFORE META. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. For those wondering, "meta" does in fact legally rhyme with "meta," so we're safe. Time is up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was FACEBOOK.
Clue 5: "THIS CLUE WAS META BEFORE META." The company that is now Meta started with FACEBOOK. 6,447 got it right.
Clue 4: "COVERED IN POSTERS THAT ARE EASY TO LIKE." It is full of people who post on the internet, and the Like button is a famous function. 2,257 got it right.
Clue 3: "MANY FRIENDS AREN'T FRIENDS." Just because you're a FACEBOOK friend doesn't mean you're a real-life friend. It's really sad. 726 got it right.
Clue 2: "NOT A NOVEL NOVEL." Well, it's not actually a book, and it's no longer new. 2 got it right.
Clue 1: "THE DEPARTED." Well, FACEBOOK was famously originally called "The Facebook," so the "the" departed.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 8,035 people guessed on clue one and two people got it right! If those people are Mark Zuckerberg and Eduardo Saverin... Eduardo, hold onto your money, bud! Kaw Fam and Wildart 1960 are our best guessers! You are each going home with $2,500!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, the prize doubles to $10,000. Let us load the crystal ball. $10,000 in there! I wish you the best of luck.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: First clue is: EXTREMELY DEPRESSING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. EXTREMELY DEPRESSING. I see some fun answers already, which is ironic for such a sad first clue. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: EXTREMELY DEPRESSING. "Hot Crouton" put onion—because it makes you cry. "Rudy Terrier" put Starbucks—maybe because you have to tamp down espresso? "Nifty Slacks" said losing best guess. That is depressing!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: CHANGE IN PLANS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. EXTREMELY DEPRESSING, CHANGE IN PLANS. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Pe-y" put breakup. "Mike Money Mo" put rain. "Franny Pack" put CPR. "Jenny Arcade" put diapers. At any age, that is a change in plans!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: STARTS AT THE BOTTOM AND STAYS THERE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Does an onion start at the bottom? Kind of, it's a root vegetable. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Lefty Alien" said penny. "Whiskey the Cat" said feet. "Canadian Thermostat" said the bottom—because it's cold.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: A HOLE IN ONE YOU DON'T CELEBRATE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. A HOLE IN ONE YOU DON'T CELEBRATE. If there's a hole in your onion, that's usually a bad sign. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Jamming Man" said worm. "Thrice Psalm 26" said grave. It's between onion and grave!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Fifth and final clue: A RUBBER FAIL WHEN YOUR CAR HITS A NAIL. 20 seconds starts now. EXTREMELY DEPRESSING, CHANGE IN PLANS, STARTS AT THE BOTTOM AND STAYS THERE, A HOLE IN ONE YOU DON'T CELEBRATE, A RUBBER FAIL WHEN YOUR CAR HITS A NAIL. Time is up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was a FLAT TIRE. The worst type of tire.
Clue 5: "A RUBBER FAIL WHEN YOUR CAR HITS A NAIL." Well, that one speaks for itself. 7,554 got it right.
Clue 4: "A HOLE IN ONE YOU DON'T CELEBRATE." No one ever says, "Yeah, I caught a FLAT TIRE!" 459 got it right.
Clue 3: "STARTS AT THE BOTTOM AND STAYS THERE." No matter where you go on a FLAT TIRE, the flat part always stays at the bottom. 137 got it right.
Clue 2: "CHANGE IN PLANS." You're on the way to do one thing, and now you have to change plans to change a tire instead. 265 got it right.
Clue 1: "EXTREMELY DEPRESSING." It's a total bummer, but your tire is literally depressed—so depressed you shouldn't drive.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 9,916 people guessed on clue number one and exactly 93 people got it right! You're each going home with over $100 tonight!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to Monates Babs 27, Upper Satchel 961, Rab Kitten Glimmer, and 90 more! That's it for this edition of Best Guest Live! We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Come back tomorrow and don't forget to bring your best guest! Good night!

clues: THE LONG AND THE SHORT OF IT
ENJOY A ROAST FROM COAST TO COAST
WHERE THE OCEAN MEETS LAND AND IT'S COVERED IN SAND
PAY THE PERK OR GET A SMIRK
EXTRA MONEY FOR A JOB WELL DONE

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! Wow, and we got a lot of first-time players here. The Real K, welcome in! Grinch 9210, welcome! This is the game where you have to decipher clues to solve a mystery. It's like Sherlock Holmes but without that weird silly hat. Well, you might actually have a silly hat on, but that's okay too.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be absolutely anything. Then I'm going to give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: After each clue I'll give you 20 seconds on the clock to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Load that crystal ball. Tonight I'm going to start with clue number one. I'm feeling a little crazy.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number one: THE LONG AND THE SHORT OF IT. The clock starts now. "Chile William 32" said an accordion. I used to go to parties and play air accordion! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here's your second clue: HAS ITS UPS AND DOWNS. Clock starts now. First clue was the long and short of it; 11,000 of you guessed. "Partly Dowy 243" said his wife Betsy. Good luck to you and Betsy tonight! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: EXTREMELY EDGY. Clock starts now. 12,000 people guessed on the second clue. "Sirly Clown 734" said a U2 groupie because Edge is the guitarist. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: ENJOY A ROAST FROM COAST TO COAST. Clock starts now. "Howy Wowee 41" said Jeff Ross. That's not me, but I get the roast reference. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your final clue for $5,000: WHERE THE OCEAN MEETS LAND AND IT'S COVERED IN SAND. Clock starts now. Clue one: Long and short. Clue two: Ups and downs. Clue three: Extremely edgy. Clue four: Roast from coast to coast. Time is up. Let's find out what's in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It is BEACH. I'm a son of a BEACH! That's a dad joke.
Clue 5: "WHERE THE OCEAN MEETS LAND AND IT'S COVERED IN SAND." That's just what it is. 4,955 of you guessed correctly.
Clue 4: "ENJOY A ROAST FROM COAST TO COAST." People love to sunbathe; you go out there and you roast. 30 of you got it correct.
Clue 3: "EXTREMELY EDGY." The BEACH is where the edge of the water hits the sand. It is the land's edge. 5 guessed correctly.
Clue 2: "HAS ITS UPS AND DOWNS." You've got sunrise, sunset, and the tide goes up and down. One person got it correct.
Clue 1: "THE LONG AND THE SHORT OF IT." The BEACH is either long this way or short this way to the water.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 11,000 of you guessed on clue one and seven of you got it correct! You are each taking home $714.28! Congratulations Gucci Pilot, Noisy Tester, and HL Hill!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The prize is doubling to $10,000! Load the crystal ball. Clue number one: MORE THAN WORDS. Clock starts now. "Pure Thermos 373" thinks it's a song. "Watery Cactus" says sentences. Punctuation counts! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Second clue: PORTION CONTROL. Clock starts now. "Kickball Kevin 24" thinks Ozempic. "Dreamy Music 688" said diet. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: END CREDITS. Clock starts now. First clue was More than Words, second was Portion Control. "Northern Dwights 102" said key best grip boy. That's very inside Hollywood! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Fourth clue: PAY THE PERK OR GET A SMIRK. Clock starts now. "Warm Popsicle 35" thinks it's a barista. "Utah Wife" says Cameo. I'm not on Cameo! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: EXTRA MONEY FOR A JOB WELL DONE. Clock starts now. 5 seconds left. Time is up. Let's find out what is in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It is A TIP! Randy just handed me one.
Clue 5: "EXTRA MONEY FOR A JOB WELL DONE." 20% is customary. 5,838 correct guesses.
Clue 4: "PAY THE PERK OR GET A SMIRK." If you don't A TIP, you may get a little attitude back. I'm a good tipper. 255 got it right.
Clue 3: "END CREDITS." It's the money or credits you give at the very end of the meal. 18 got it correct.
Clue 2: "PORTION CONTROL." You control the portion of money they receive. One person got it correct.
Clue 1: "MORE THAN WORDS." It's way more than words. It's nice to say thank you, but money is more meaningful.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 11,724 people guessed on clue one and none of you got it right! So the one person who guessed it on clue two wins the whole $10,000! Grizzly Rose 39, you win $10,000!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: I'm getting tipped for this episode! Congratulations to all our winners. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Come back tomorrow and don't forget to bring your best guest! Good night! Oh look, there's the credits... I can't read them, they're backwards!

clues: MAJOR PLAYER FROM A WELL-KNOWN HOOD
TICKLED PINK TO BE TICKLED IN RED
THE RUBY MUPPET YOU'VE HEARD LIVES CLOSE TO BIG BIRD
JUMPS THROUGH HOOPS TO CLOSE THE LOOP
A LEATHER STRAP THAT GIVES PANTS A WRAP

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! Hello, I am your host Hunter March. I want to do some shout outs: Maserati, first-time player; Pro Owl 390, first-time player; and then GMA of 13—one grandma of 13! Pretty young to be a grandma.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: It's a big day here at Netflix Games: Overcooked! All You Can Eat launches today! Guys, genuinely, I love Overcooked. It is a great game to play with friends, especially if you like yelling at those friends. This version lets you play with Netflix-exclusive chefs from Stranger Things and K-pop demon hunters. It's a great chance to find out if the Demogorgon makes a decent burrito! Shout out to our talented friends at Ghost Town Games and Team 17 for all of their excellent work.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Anyway, here's something else that's super interesting: the rules. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues, but remember, the clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball. Someone called me out for saying "mystical magical" every time, so I'm not going to say that anymore. Look at that boring crystal ball. Anyway, let's get to clue number one: SPEAKS HIGHLY OF EVERYTHING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: While we wait, I just want to say thanks for playing. Hi everybody playing on Discord! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: SPEAKS HIGHLY OF EVERYTHING. "Chillfest 9990" from Fort Worth said yelling. "Muggy Mesa 344" said Siri. Someone said the Pope.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: BOOKS SMART AND STREET SMART. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. If someone had said the Pope is book smart, I would say yes, but about one book! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: BOOKS SMART AND STREET SMART. "Pickle Thawn" said teacher. "Mama Bear 057" said mom. "Normal Hyjinks 198" said Snoop.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: MAJOR PLAYER FROM A WELL-KNOWN HOOD. 20 seconds starts now. I also just realized Snoop Dogg speaks highly of everything—that was a late guess, but I like the effort. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: MAJOR PLAYER FROM A WELL-KNOWN HOOD. "Comm Crew 103" said owl. Someone else said Robin.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: TICKLED PINK TO BE TICKLED IN RED. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. This could go in way too many directions. Shout out to the people on TikTok and Reddit, I try to see all of them! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: TICKLED PINK TO BE TICKLED IN RED. My favorite guess was from "Askew Potato" who said Santa. We've never done Santa!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: THE RUBY MUPPET YOU'VE HEARD LIVES CLOSE TO BIG BIRD. 20 seconds starts now. I know I said "puppet," that's on me, but it was written correctly on screen! Time's up. Let's find out what's been inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was ELMO!
Clue 5: "THE RUBY MUPPET YOU'VE HEARD LIVES CLOSE TO BIG BIRD." A lot of you figured it out there. 6,059 got it right.
Clue 4: "TICKLED PINK TO BE TICKLED IN RED." Well, "Tickle Me ELMO" and his entire body is red. 4,916 got it right.
Clue 3: "MAJOR PLAYER FROM A WELL-KNOWN HOOD." ELMO loves to play, and Sesame Street is a well-known neighborhood. 310 got it right.
Clue 2: "BOOKS SMART AND STREET SMART." He helps kids learn to read (book smart) and he lives on Sesame Street—and that place is dangerous! 189 got it right.
Clue 1: "SPEAKS HIGHLY OF EVERYTHING." Well, ELMO has an iconically high voice. "Hi, I'm ELMO!"

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 34 people got it right on clue one! That means 34 people are going home with $147.05 each! Congratulations to Simple Daylight 97, Fuzzy Healer 272, Dwiki Wiki, and I Crash Bikes.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, prize doubles to $10,000. Load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: MIDDLE MANAGEMENT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: MIDDLE MANAGEMENT. "Cheeky Tomb 87" said elf. "LA 2368" said target—because the center is the middle? "Bendy Straw" said donut holes.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: GET IT TOGETHER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Do the earlier answers still work? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: GET IT TOGETHER. "Kanani" from San Diego said glue. "Mary Russell" said Oreo.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: TOTALLY WASTED. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Keep in mind, "wasted" is spelled maybe differently than you'd assume. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: TOTALLY WASTED. "VP Chedda" said Human Resources—man, that guy hates his job. "Jean Claude Van Damme" was another guess—he's got a snatched waist!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: JUMPS THROUGH HOOPS TO CLOSE THE LOOP. 20 seconds starts now. It's like a call with HR, right? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Fancy Nancy" said circus animal. Someone else said dog.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: A LEATHER STRAP THAT GIVES PANTS A WRAP. 20 seconds starts now. Your final 20 seconds. ELMO does not need this! Time's up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was A BELT.
Clue 5: "A LEATHER STRAP THAT GIVES PANTS A WRAP." That speak for itself. 2,743 got it right.
Clue 4: "JUMPS THROUGH HOOPS TO CLOSE THE LOOP." Your A BELT is a loop and it goes through the hoops (loops) of your pants. 3,594 got it right.
Clue 3: "TOTALLY WASTED." People wear a A BELT right around their waist. 6,625 got it right.
Clue 2: "GET IT TOGETHER." The primary job for a A BELT is to just pull it all together. 1,997 got it right.
Clue 1: "MIDDLE MANAGEMENT." At most one, maybe two. A A BELT goes right around your midsection. 1,336 got it right!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: I didn't think anyone would get it on clue one, but 1,336 people got it right! They are sharing $10,000, which means $7.48 each!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to Evil Maiden, Text Farm, and Wyatt Earth. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Have a great weekend everybody and come back on Monday! Don't forget to bring your best guest! Good night!]

clues: A CRASHING NOISE SPIKE FOLLOWING A LIGHTNING STRIKE
IF YOU HAVE THE ITCH DON'T START FROM SCRATCH
DON'T BE A JERK AND NUKE YOUR FISH AT WORK
NOT MUCH WAITING WHILE YOUR FOOD IS RADIATING

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! Yes, how exciting! I'm your host Hunter March. As I'm sure you already know by this outfit, it's National Meatball Day! This wasn't accidental, people. Your meatball celebrations are probably in full swing right now, so we wanted to give you another reason to celebrate. We've got a special deal from our friends at Partana—it's going out to everyone who just plays in round two.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Partana is a premium Italian food brand rooted in generations of Sicilian traditions, so it's kind of a meatball day no-brainer. Make sure you stick around for round two to get that great deal. And as always, we also have $15,000 going to some winners! But first, let's celebrate the rules.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item. Then I'll give you five clues written like riddles to make you think. You get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball. It’s like a glass meatball! Here comes clue number one: ENCORE PERFORMANCE. 20 seconds starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Happy Monday! I hope you guys had a lovely weekend. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: ENCORE PERFORMANCE. "Kirk 67" from New York said Eminem—he had an album called Encore. "Chosen Ron" said Rush Hour 2.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: OUT OF SIGHT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. ENCORE PERFORMANCE, OUT OF SIGHT. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: OUT OF SIGHT. "Kate PL" said sunset. "Bald Hill" said worm. Crazy pull!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: HALF OF A DYNAMIC DUO. 20 seconds starts now. What would a worm's duo partner be? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: HALF OF A DYNAMIC DUO. "Savage Poncho" said moon—cuz the sun is the other half. "Jx011" said Batman.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: OKAY BOOMER. 20 seconds starts now. If you've never heard that phrase, you are the boomer! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: OKAY BOOMER. "Savage Otter 821" said Cher. She's great! "Strong Kraken 891" said meme.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: A CRASHING NOISE SPIKE FOLLOWING A LIGHTNING STRIKE. 20 seconds starts now. This is pretty easy if you ask me. Good luck! Time's up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was THUNDER.
Clue 5: "A CRASHING NOISE SPIKE FOLLOWING A LIGHTNING STRIKE." Almost everyone got it. 10,387 got it right.
Clue 4: "OKAY BOOMER." THUNDER makes a booming sound—kind of the biggest boom. 199 got it right.
Clue 3: "HALF OF A DYNAMIC DUO." The other half is of course Lightning. 106 got it right.
Clue 2: "OUT OF SIGHT." You hear THUNDER but you do not see it. 38 got it right.
Clue 1: "ENCORE PERFORMANCE." THUNDER happens after a lightning strike; it is the encore to lightning.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 11 people got it right on clue one! Those 11 people are going home with $454.54 each! Congratulations Lucy 2008, Batman 42.92, and The Flick Chick!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, prize doubles to $10,000. Everyone who submits an answer gets a voucher for $15 off an order of $75 or more from Partana. Load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: THINK INSIDE THE BOX. 20 seconds starts now. Very ironic for this show! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THINK INSIDE THE BOX. "Ratic" said cat—they always climb in boxes. "Buff Pencil 385" said Jack. "Little Titan" said the letter O. You are too smart for this show!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: IF YOU HAVE THE ITCH DON'T START FROM SCRATCH. 20 seconds starts now. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Sleepy Cat" said idea—don't even worry about building it.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: DINNER WITH A VIEW. 20 seconds starts now. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: DINNER WITH A VIEW. "Big Dum Dum" put recipe. I wasn't calling them a big dum dum, that's the username! "John With The Wind" said airplane food.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: DON'T BE A JERK AND NUKE YOUR FISH AT WORK. 20 seconds starts now. I want to apologize to the people I called dum dums—no one's guesses are stupid! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Big Boss Rick Ross" said sushi. "Dumpster Fire" also a guess.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: NOT MUCH WAITING WHILE YOUR FOOD IS RADIATING. 20 seconds starts now. Your final chance at $10,000! Time's up. Let's find out what's in the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was A MICROWAVE.
Clue 5: "NOT MUCH WAITING WHILE YOUR FOOD IS RADIATING." That's how they work. 3,516 got it right.
Clue 4: "DON'T BE A JERK AND NUKE YOUR FISH AT WORK." Most people don't enjoy the smell of fish cooked in a A MICROWAVE in the office. 5,592 got it right.
Clue 3: "DINNER WITH A VIEW." Most have that little window to see inside. 227 got it right.
Clue 2: "IF YOU HAVE THE ITCH DON'T START FROM SCRATCH." They are used to heat food up, not cook from scratch. 7 got it right.
Clue 1: "THINK INSIDE THE BOX." You heat your food by literally placing it inside a box.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 19 people got it right on clue number one! Each is going home with $526.31!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to Abe Froman 80, Nutty T232, and Ragged Collie 769! Thank you to Partana for today's deal. See you tomorrow! Good night!

clues: WHO'S IN THE MOOD FOR SURF ON TURF
FOUR-WHEELED SLAB YOU CAN HEEL FLIP AND GRAB
A SECURE CLOSET-LIKE BOX FOR SCHOLARS AND JOCKS

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Come on! We're going! Welcome to Best Guest Live! I’m your host Howie Mandel. That's me right here. We're celebrating the release of the brand-new season of One Piece, which premiered on Netflix today! We know that you're excited to join the Straw Hat crew on their latest swashbuckling adventure, but I'll have you know that we've been known to buckle a swasher or two ourselves!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It's true—we don't have a zany cast of villains, I can't stretch my arms like saltwater taffy, and there's about a 0% chance of a cool sword fight breaking out, but we do have something under our straw hats that makes us worth your while. You know what that is? $15,000!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: So before you go on a treasure hunt with Luffy and the gang, let's dig up some treasure right here. Here’s how the game works. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be absolutely anything. Then I’m going to give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles, so they're meant to make you think. It's more fun that way!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in the correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load the crystal ball with the first secret item. What could it be? Well, here comes the clues. Clue number one: MOVING TRUCKS. The clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: MOVING TRUCKS. Is that a pirate? Do I sound like a pirate? No, I don't. "Lance By Chance 43" thinks it's Transformers. Those are moving trucks! "Sockhead 29" said a U-Haul. "Tree Branch 22" says a crab—I don't get that, but you could be right because it's a riddle! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: ON TOP OF THE WORLD. The clock starts now. First clue was Moving Trucks. "Burnt Omelet 22" said Santa. He's got that moving truck on top of the globe! "Titanic Mink 464" said skiing. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: SUPPORTIVE WHEN THINGS GET TRICKY. The clock starts now. We've already got almost 14,000 guesses in. "Cosmolina" said a dolly. "412" said girdle—it's supportive when things get tricky! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: WHO'S IN THE MOOD FOR SURF ON TURF? The clock starts right now. "Chili Sauna 323" thinks it's Red Lobster. I get it! Surf and turf. I like the way you guys think. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: FOUR-WHEELED SLAB YOU CAN HEEL FLIP AND GRAB. The clock starts now. Last chance! MOVING TRUCKS, ON TOP OF THE WORLD, SUPPORTIVE WHEN THINGS GET TRICKY, SURF ON TURF, and FOUR-WHEELED SLAB YOU CAN HEEL FLIP AND GRAB. Time's up. Let's find out what's in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It is A SKATEBOARD! Look at that!
Clue 5: "FOUR-WHEELED SLAB YOU CAN HEEL FLIP AND GRAB." It's a slab with wheels. Heel flip and grab are skateboarding tricks. 7,631 guessed correctly.
Clue 4: "WHO'S IN THE MOOD FOR SURF ON TURF?" It's a surfboard, but for when you're on land. 1,151 got it correct.
Clue 3: "SUPPORTIVE WHEN THINGS GET TRICKY." A A SKATEBOARD bears your weight when you're doing tricks; it's what you support yourself on. 888 correct guesses.
Clue 2: "ON TOP OF THE WORLD." A A SKATEBOARD literally holds you a few inches above the Earth's surface. 308 of you got that correct.
Clue 1: "MOVING TRUCKS." Trucks are the metal parts fastened to the bottom of a skateboard, and when you're skating, they are moving!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 7,404 of you guessed on clue one and 990 got it right! You are each splitting $5.05. I love when a lot of people win!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The prize doubles to $10,000! Let's load the crystal ball for the second game. Here’s your first clue: PUBLIC AND PRIVATE. The clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: PUBLIC AND PRIVATE. "Biscuit Guy 7" said the internet. "Just the Ox" said the bathroom. It could be a public restroom! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: HOLD PLEASE. The clock starts now. 18,335 of you took a guess on that first one. "Heather My Nest" said my hand. "Cody 2K" said stocks. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: DON'T SWEAT THE SMALL STUFF. The clock starts now. "Reviserin 25" said yoga. You don't sweat the small stuff, it's public or private, and you hold positions. I like the way you think! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: SCHOOL SUPPLIES. The clock starts now. "Endless Whis 305" said a backpack. "Without A Net 904" said gym shorts. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: A SECURE CLOSET-LIKE BOX FOR SCHOLARS AND JOCKS. The clock starts now. I'm not much of a poet, and my poetry reading is a little bit out. You're making me sweat my small stuff! Time's up. Let's find out what's in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It's A LOCKER! And you know what's weird? This is my locker! How did you get my locker? I'm embarrassed because I love the butterflies.
Clue 5: "A SECURE CLOSET-LIKE BOX FOR SCHOLARS AND JOCKS." For people in school and for your jockstrap and sports gear. 6,673 guessed correctly.
Clue 4: "SCHOOL SUPPLIES." A school supplies you with your A LOCKER, and you put your supplies in it. Where else are you going to put your stuffed pony? 98 guessed correctly.
Clue 3: "DON'T SWEAT THE SMALL STUFF." It holds both small items and sweaty items. Instead of carrying it all, you put it in here. 6 got it correct.
Clue 2: "HOLD PLEASE." It holds your belongings so you don't have to carry them around. One person got it correct.
Clue 1: "PUBLIC AND PRIVATE." It is a private area in a public space, and they are in both public and private schools.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 18,335 guesses on the first clue and two got it correct! That means you each taking $5,000! Our best guessers are Megala Donut and The Goblin Mode!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Congratulations again to all of our winners! Go watch the all-new season of One Piece right now on Netflix! We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Good night!Would you like me to analyze the specific strategies used by the winners of Round 2 tonight?

clues: USE YOUR HEAD BUT WATCH YOUR HANDS

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! Hello everyone and I’m excited to be back. I’m your host Hunter March. We are approaching a major milestone here on Best Guest Live—we’re getting close to 20,000 winners! That’s pretty amazing.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: We’ve paid out real money 20,000 times and people have won every conceivable amount from $248 to $200,000. 20,000 is an amazing amount of winners, so stay tuned. If we pass that milestone, we’ll celebrate it! For now, let’s see how many people will take home a piece of $15,000.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they’re meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load the crystal ball. There it is! Here comes clue number one: SETTLE THE SCORE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: SETTLE THE SCORE. It could be a lot of things, but I guess that’s the whole point of the show. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: SETTLE THE SCORE. "Hannah C" said tie. "Jolly Headache" said referee.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: OFTEN OVERDUE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. A referee? Sometimes they're wanted, sometimes not. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: OFTEN OVERDUE. "Dr G" from Pennsylvania said karma. "Tan Doggy 44" said time out.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: TAKE OUT LEFTOVERS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Clue three down. Hello to everyone playing on the Discord! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: TAKE OUT LEFTOVERS. "Fruity Patuti" said baby. "Holly Go Brightly" said Moo Goo Gai Pan.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: RETURN. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. This is your second to last clue to get your answer in! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: RETURN. "Pablo 134" from the Bronx said Uno return. "Martin the Martian" said trilogy—the return of the return!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: AN IOU FROM THE IRS. 20 seconds starts now. This is your final clue for $5,000. If you know it, type it in. If you don't, type something fun! Time's up. Let’s find out what's inside the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was a TAX REFUND.
Clue 5: "AN IOU FROM THE IRS." Well, that is exactly a TAX REFUND. 2,356 got it right.
Clue 4: "RETURN." It is the money returned to you from your tax return. 214 got it right.
Clue 3: "TAKE OUT LEFTOVERS." It is the leftover amount from what the government took out of your paycheck for your taxes. 14 got it right.
Clue 2: "OFTEN OVERDUE." Well, you paid over the amount that was due to the IRS. 27 got it right.
Clue 1: "SETTLE THE SCORE." Once you receive your TAX REFUND, the IRS has essentially gotten even with you.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 8,791 people guessed on clue one and exactly one person got it right! Mac Deo, you win $5,000!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The prize doubles to $10,000. Load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: SPEAKS MULTIPLE LANGUAGES. 20 seconds starts now. We're trying to hit that 20,000 winner mark! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: SPEAKS MULTIPLE LANGUAGES. "Pinkish Jade" said a hug. "Bry Pilot" said Siri. "Tamir Bob" said music.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: FOUR SCORE. 20 seconds starts now. Is that how it's spelled? I wasn't around for Abe Lincoln. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: FOUR SCORE. "Melm 217" said wink. "Newkerk" said math. Someone else said Connect Four.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: 8 OZ OF EARTH. 20 seconds starts now. I love this clue! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 8 OZ OF EARTH. "Alaska Brook 12" said summer. "Hundrel Parrot" said parrot—maybe they know exactly what a parrot weighs?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: USE YOUR HEAD BUT WATCH YOUR HANDS. 20 seconds starts now. I don't think parrots have hands! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: USE YOUR HEAD BUT WATCH YOUR HANDS. "Pizza Bagel" said penny—Abe Lincoln's head? "Ben There" said poker.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: GLOBAL DOMINATION IS THE GOAL. 20 seconds starts now. Final chance! Time's up. Let's find out what's in the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: It is the WORLD CUP!
Clue 5: "GLOBAL DOMINATION IS THE GOAL." The goal of the teams is to become the world champion at the WORLD CUP. 857 got it right.
Clue 4: "USE YOUR HEAD BUT WATCH YOUR HANDS." Soccer players can use their heads to touch the ball, but not their hands! 125 got it right.
Clue 3: "8 OZ OF EARTH." Earth equals World, 8 ounces equals a Cup. WORLD CUP! 43 got it right.
Clue 2: "FOUR SCORE." The WORLD CUP happens every four years and you see the best scores in the world. 28 got it right.
Clue 1: "SPEAKS MULTIPLE LANGUAGES." The tournament has fans from all over the world who speak many different languages.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 15,644 people guessed on clue one and 8 people got it right! Each is going home with $1,250! Congratulations to Frog In Denial, Miss Ginger Moon, and Boiled Milk!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations to all of our winners! Check your win history for any vouchers you might have won. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. See you tomorrow! Good night! Would you like me to look for the player count information for the first clue of Round 1?

clues: WHAT'S UP WHEN IT GOES DOWN
RAIN OR SHINE IT IS DIVINE
A HANDHELD CANOPY THAT KEEPS YOU DRY FROM THE SKY
AUDIENCES WOULD ROOT FOR HIS STUDDED JUMPSUIT
A HOME BASE FULL OF GRACE
ONE NAMED KING THAT LOVED TO ACT AND SING

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Behoop out leap house! Come on everybody! Welcome to Best Guest Live. I’m your host Howie Mandel. Before we even start tonight’s game, I have a special announcement about tomorrow. Before you watch the Oscars this weekend, you can watch the Guesties right here tomorrow! It’s going to be a celebration of Best Guest Live’s excellence. So tomorrow night, put on your nicest tux or fanciest gown and get ready for a night of excitement and glamour.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here’s another exciting thing: Hunter mentioned this yesterday, we are getting so close to 20,000 of you winners! Hunter and I have a bet—if we pass 20,000 on a night that I’m hosting, he’s got to get a tattoo of me on his neck. So I am hoping for a lot of winners tonight! Let's just mess up Hunter's neck.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: As if all that wasn’t exciting enough, we’ve also got $15,000 on the line tonight. Here’s how you get to sharing it. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be absolutely anything. Then I give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they’re meant to make you think, people!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: After each clue I’ll put 20 seconds on the clock. You submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It’s got to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you got to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load that crystal ball. Here comes clue number one: UNDERCOVER AGENT. 20 seconds is on the clock.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: UNDERCOVER AGENT. I hope we break 20,000 tonight. Can you imagine a tat of me on Hunter's neck? "Busted Washcloth" said mattress. "Chubby Oatmeal 843" said CIA. I'm thinking the pop star and the agent—how stupid am I? That's why I'm hosting and you're playing! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: I WAS FRAMED. Clock starts now. "Hardcore Sprinkle" said Roger Rabbit. "Brisk Rhombus 10" said the FBI. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here’s your third clue: WHAT'S UP WHEN IT GOES DOWN. Clock starts now. I’m just embarrassed. Am I red? "Matlin the Hat 23" said the internet. When the internet goes down, nothing's up! "Samira 111" said a shadow. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here is your fourth clue: RAIN OR SHINE IT IS DIVINE. Clock starts now. "Embolo 293" said a tent. "Benny and the Pets" said the weather. "Walker" said sunset. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: A HANDHELD CANOPY THAT KEEPS YOU DRY FROM THE SKY. Clock starts now. Come on, people! Even a guy who thought it was CIA could figure that out. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It’s an UMBRELLA. I don't want to open it inside; it's bad luck!
Clue 5: "A HANDHELD CANOPY THAT KEEPS YOU DRY FROM THE SKY." This is an UMBRELLA. Ella, ella... I was going to sing more, but my boss Jeff doesn't want to pay for it. 6,697 of you got it correct.
Clue 4: "RAIN OR SHINE IT IS DIVINE." It protects you from rain and provides shade from the sun. 1,575 got it correct.
Clue 3: "WHAT'S UP WHEN IT GOES DOWN." You put it up when the rain comes down. 226 got it correct.
Clue 2: "I WAS FRAMED." The UMBRELLA itself contains a whole frame underneath. 15 got it correct.
Clue 1: "UNDERCOVER AGENT." It is the agent that provides cover when it rains or there is too much sun.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 9,826 of you guessed on clue one and 104 got it correct! That means each of you is getting $48.07! Congratulations to Mike Hotzber, Prissy Bissy 1, and Misty Music 8.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The prize doubles to $10,000. Load that crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number one: STONE AND DINNERBREAD ROYALTY. Clock starts now. "Gumball 923" says pizza. I don't know, this is the most confusing episode I've ever been part of. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Second clue: SHAKY AT BEST. Clock starts now. 10,185 of you took a guess on that first one. "Fancy Brand 756" said earthquake. "Graceful Tart 51" said salt. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: AUDIENCES WOULD ROOT FOR HIS STUDDED JUMPSUIT. Clock starts now. "Luffy" said Jester—I've read your name before! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Fourth clue: A HOME BASE FULL OF GRACE. Clock starts now. "Jumpy Doily 287" says Yankee Stadium. "J Benny DL" says castle. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: ONE NAMED KING THAT LOVED TO ACT AND SING. Clock starts now. Time is up! Let’s find out what's in that crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That’s right, ELVIS PRESLEY is in the building!
Clue 5: "ONE NAMED KING THAT LOVED TO ACT AND SING." He goes by one name, ELVIS. 1,929 got it right.
Clue 4: "A HOME BASE FULL OF GRACE." His home was Graceland. 2,846 got it correct.
Clue 3: "AUDIENCES WOULD ROOT FOR HIS STUDDED JUMPSUIT." 5,092 got this correct.
Clue 2: "SHAKY AT BEST." He was at his best when his legs were shaking and he was making iconic shakes. 527 got it right.
Clue 1: "STONE AND DINNERBREAD ROYALTY." A stone is a "rock." Dinner bread is a "roll." Rock and roll royalty! ELVIS is the King of Rock and Roll.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 10,185 guesses on that first clue and 158 of you got it correct! That means each of you is taking $63.29! Congratulations to Jeffy 100, Griffy 92, and Brother 00.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That is it for this edition of Best Guest Live! We didn’t hit 20,000, so tomorrow Hunter will be here with a brand new clean neck. Don't forget tomorrow is the first annual Guessie Awards! Good night everybody! Howie Mandel has left the building!

clues: DESPITE THE NAME IT'S OFTEN SHARED
A PHOTO TAKEN BY ONESELF FOR SOCIAL MEDIA OR A SHELF
HAS A REPUTATION FOR BAD TASTE
OFTEN GETS A STAMP OF APPROVAL
A FLAPPED POUCH THAT HOLDS MAIL SENT BY SNAILS

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Tonight it's the first annual Guessie Awards celebrating excellence in the field of Best Guest Live! And here's your host Hunter March. Welcome, welcome, welcome! Hello, welcome to Best Guest Live! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: I am so excited about the Guessies tonight. Tonight is all about celebrating you, the amazing players who genuinely make Best Guest Live the greatest mobile game show that I've ever hosted. Yeah, it's the only one, but whatever! Anyway, a little later in the show I'll be giving out the first ever Guessie Award to one of you, which is exciting.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: As always, we still have $15,000 up for grabs, but it's time for everyone's favorite part of the show: the rules. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues, but remember the clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. This whole suit thing really throwing me for a loop, I come here dressed in sweats every day! Anyway, make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. With that being said, it is time for round one!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the extra fancy crystal ball. Here comes clue number one: ME MYSELF AND I. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. A reminder, we could hit 20,000 winners tonight! We are within 40ish. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: ME MYSELF AND I. "Alli Rose" from Washington said name. "Sally Says 43" said game show host.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: AT ARMS LENGTH. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. "Stampede" is already putting a guess—that was the fastest guess I've ever seen. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: AT ARMS LENGTH. "Unknown Sound" said mirror. "Chypes" from Durban said hug. "Stamp Pete" said perfume.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: ABOUT FACE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I don't wear a suit often, so let your friends know I'm wearing a tux—it could be a big selling point! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ABOUT FACE. "Halifodala" said salute. "Vitamin R94" said watch.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: DESPITE THE NAME IT'S OFTEN SHARED. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Also, I forgot if we're doing this because it's the Oscars this weekend... I just realized that, my bad. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: DESPITE THE NAME IT'S OFTEN SHARED. "Winter Lynn" said ego—too smart for us! "Woolly Wolf 94" said yawn. "Mary Hopscotch" said personal data.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: A PHOTO TAKEN BY ONESELF FOR SOCIAL MEDIA OR A SHELF. 20 seconds starts now. Your final 20 seconds. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was a SELFIE. I was lying about the taco thing.
Clue 5: "A PHOTO TAKEN BY ONESELF FOR SOCIAL MEDIA OR A SHELF." Well, you take it by yourself and it's a SELFIE. 7,291 got it right.
Clue 4: "DESPITE THE NAME IT'S OFTEN SHARED." It's all about you when you're taking it, but then you share it online. 1,086 got it right.
Clue 3: "ABOUT FACE." Not only are SELFIEs literally about your face, but you hit the button to turn the camera around, which does an "about face." 1,751 got it right.
Clue 2: "AT ARMS LENGTH." You often take a SELFIE from as far as you can extend your arm. 1,276 got it right.
Clue 1: "ME MYSELF AND I." It is taken by you, of you, for you, most of the time.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 353 people got SELFIE on clue one! That means 353 people are sharing $14.16 each! And by the way, that right there is 20,000 winners! That also means I won the tattoo bet with Howie, so next time Howie is going to get a tattoo of my face on his neck!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Now it's time for the Guessie Awards. Our category is "Outstanding Short Film About Winning Money on Best Guess Live."
Nominee 1: Kevin from Raleigh with Stompy Guy.
Nominee 2: Mike and Melody with Daddy Daughter Win.

Nominee 3: The Betting House family with Family Goes Bananas.Hunter March: Use the emojis to vote! Heart for Stompy Guy, Money for Daddy Daughter Win, Clapping for Family Goes Bananas. The winner gets a trophy, merch, and six months of free Netflix!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Prize doubles to $10,000. Load the crystal ball. Clue number one: SHOVED BY THRILLSEEKERS. 20 seconds starts now. How great were those videos? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: SHOVED BY THRILLSEEKERS. "Gregathon 2519" said joystick. "Hunter Fan" said coffee—shoved down their throats. "Silly Sidewalks" said bears.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: AN OPEN AND SHUT CASE. 20 seconds starts now. Clue one: Shoved by thrillseekers. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: AN OPEN AND SHUT CASE. "Dead Man 2606" said door—definitely shoved by thrillseekers. "Big Head 91" said sleep.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: HAS A REPUTATION FOR BAD TASTE. 20 seconds starts now. I think doors is out, bears are in! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: HAS A REPUTATION FOR BAD TASTE. "Nikita" said garlic—I disagree! "Fuzzy Player 435" said Nerds.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: OFTEN GETS A STAMP OF APPROVAL. 20 seconds starts now. I think it's clicking for people! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: OFTEN GETS A STAMP OF APPROVAL. "Omnadragon 480" said seal. "Kyle BC and U22" said lower back.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: A FLAPPED POUCH THAT HOLDS MAIL SENT BY SNAILS. 20 seconds starts now. Final chance! Time's up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was an ENVELOPE. It took me three ENVELOPEs to figure that out.
Clue 5: "A FLAPPED POUCH THAT HOLDS MAIL SENT BY SNAILS." It holds snail mail. 2,872 got it right.
Clue 4: "OFTEN GETS A STAMP OF APPROVAL." An ENVELOPE gets a stamp which approves it for mailing. 1,627 got it right.
Clue 3: "HAS A REPUTATION FOR BAD TASTE." ENVELOPEs taste bad! 119 got it right.
Clue 2: "AN OPEN AND SHUT CASE." First you open the ENVELOPE, then you shut it. 145 got it right.
Clue 1: "SHOVED BY THRILLSEEKERS." What do thrillseekers do? They "push the ENVELOPE."

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 59 people got it right on clue one! Each is going home with $169.49! Congratulations to Coach DBS73 and Arhan Echo Soul.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Now, for the Guessie! The emojis were too equal to perceive the difference, so all three nominees win 6 months of free Netflix and exclusive merch! Congratulations to Stompy Guy, Daddy Daughter Win, and Family Goes Bananas!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Thank you everyone! Good night!

clues: SEARCH FOR OMEN IN A MIRROR
A CLOWN FISH IS THE STAR IN THIS FILM FROM PIXAR
FOUR LIT LETTERS THAT ARE OUT OF THE DOOR GETTERS

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guest Live! Welcome back! Happy Monday, i'm your host Hunter March. We have our crystal ball filled with all sorts of things over time—we've had everything from 67 to SPEED BUMP to TAYLOR SWIFT.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: But here are a few things that will never be in the crystal ball: my wife Betsy, for example. You guys have guessed my wife Betsy so many times; none of you will ever win with that. Also, curse words. It will also never be Hunter March. I've seen inside of the crystal ball; I cannot fit, I am far too large! That was not a joke.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Anyway, regardless of what's in the crystal ball, it is always accompanied by money—$15,000 today. Here’s how you get yours. Each episode of Best Guest Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues. But remember, those clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we do not accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball. I’m not in there, neither's my wife Betsy, but everything else is a potential. Here's clue number one: MAKEUP WITH NO MAKEUP. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: MAKEUP WITH NO MAKEUP. I'm only thinking of curse words right now, but that can't be it! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: MAKEUP WITH NO MAKEUP. "Vinnie T" said test. "Skittleman 112" said wig—makeup with no makeup? It acts as an enhancer.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: A GUILT TRIP. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Test or wig? Do they still work for a guilt trip? A guilt trip can be a test... oh, makeup with no makeup because it's a test! Now I'm back into "test." Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: A GUILT TRIP. "Devoid Market 131" from Miamisburg, Ohio, said vacation—they're really manifesting. Someone from Homestead, Florida, said late.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: BASED ON CURRENT EVENTS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. A lot of people thinking of current events and just cursing in their head—that's not the answer! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: BASED ON CURRENT EVENTS. "Welty Elf MF" from Edgerton, Kansas, said prison. "Jenster 12" said apology.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: SEARCH FOR OMEN IN A MIRROR. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. This one is throwing me for a loop. Hello to everyone playing on Discord! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: SEARCH FOR OMEN IN A MIRROR. "Pretty Happy" said mime—stuck in a box, mirror. "Frank No Furer" said Candyman—the terrifying one with the mirror. It's not that!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: A CLOWN FISH IS THE STAR IN THIS FILM FROM PIXAR. 20 seconds starts now. If anyone gets this wrong, I’m going to be really disappointed. Google just that and you'll figure it out. Time's up. Let’s find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was FINDING NEMO. And believe me, all the clues fit.
Clue 5: "A CLOWN FISH IS THE STAR IN THIS FILM FROM PIXAR." 8,644 people got it right.
Clue 4: "SEARCH FOR OMEN IN A MIRROR." Search equals FINDING. Omen in a mirror? What's the reverse of "Omen"? NEMO. 125 got it right.

Clue 3: "BASED ON CURRENT EVENTS." Well, Marlin and Dory, they got to ride something on their way to Find NEMO, and in this movie, it just so happens to be the "current" currents. One person got it right.
Clue 2: "A GUILT TRIP." The dad character, Marlin, feels guilty throughout the entire trip trying to find his son. One person got it right.
Clue 1: "MAKEUP WITH NO MAKEUP." The dad’s trying to "make up" with his son, and they’re both clown fish—clowns that don’t wear makeup.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 9,408 people guessed on clue one and nobody got it right! The one person on clue number two is getting the full $5,000! That person is Jace Guitar! Jace, if you are watching, send me a message on Instagram!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, the prize doubles to $10,000! Load the crystal ball. Clue number one: ESSENTIAL FOR CODING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ESSENTIAL FOR CODING. Jace Guitar, I wouldn’t be surprised if you threw your phone against a wall out of excitement, but if you’re still playing, try to go two for two! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ESSENTIAL FOR CODING. "Cloudy Chime" said CPR—for what's it called, to "code" something? "Sleepy Tones" said DNA.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: FOLLOW THE LEADER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Following the leader might not be a bad idea, because Jace Guitar is leading the pack! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: FOLLOW THE LEADER. "Bella GK" said mouse. "Bountiful Thimble 43" said lemmings.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: INSIDE OUT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. CPR seems a little more dangerous now if we're thinking about "Inside Out." Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: INSIDE OUT. "Noteworthy Nate" said appendectomy. Someone named "Hi" said Pixar again. Incorrect.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: GUIDANCE FROM ABOVE. 20 seconds starts now. This one might be a real doozy. $10,000 on the line! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: GUIDANCE FROM ABOVE. "GPS"—definitely guidance from above from the satellites. "Joy"—guidance from above? That's sweet. "Sundial"—exactly what I thought, but it's incorrect.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: FOUR LIT LETTERS THAT ARE OUT OF THE DOOR GETTERS. 20 seconds starts now. This one still stumped me a little bit, but good luck. Time's up. Let's find out what was actually inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was an EXIT SIGN.
Clue 5: "FOUR LIT LETTERS THAT ARE OUT OF THE DOOR GETTERS." The four letters are EXIT and they usually are lit up to get you out. 4,134 got it right.
Clue 4: "GUIDANCE FROM ABOVE." They’re usually located on a wall just above the door and they give you guidance on where the EXIT is. 5 got it right.
Clue 3: "INSIDE OUT." They literally show you how to get "out" from the "inside." Only one person got it right.
Clue 2: "FOLLOW THE LEADER." The sign is the "leader" you follow when exiting. Zero got it right.
Clue 1: "ESSENTIAL FOR CODING." Most commercial buildings are required by building "code" to have EXIT SIGNs.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 10,430 people guessed on clue number one and zero people got it right. That means the one person who got it on clue three is going home with $10,000 all to themselves! That person is Valued Bird 748!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for this edition of Best Guest Live! Congratulations again to Valued Bird 748 and Jace Guitar! We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Good night everybody!

clues: SITE TO CITE TO PROVE YOU'RE RIGHT
ONLINE BRATANNICA BUT EVEN MORE GIGANTICA
CAN HAPPEN IN VEGAS JUST LIKE "THE" MOVIE
NOW YOU'RE SICK AND SUCH LAST NIGHT YOU DRANK TOO MUCH

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Out me up! Leap out! Everybody have some! Welcome to Best Guess Live! How’s it going? Hello, how you doing? I’m your host Hunter March. Happy St. Patrick’s Day to all of you who celebrate! For those of you who have big plans tonight, please have a great time, but also please be very careful out there.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Traditionally, you’re supposed to wear green on St. Patty’s Day. That’s been the rule for as long as I can remember. And of course, at Best Guess Live, we are suckers for tradition. So all of our winners today—this is actually kind of exciting—are going to win something green, and it’s going to be something green that you’re going to then stuff into your pockets because it’s $15,000! It’s not bad, better than getting pinched.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here’s how you can get some of that money. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues. Remember, those clues are written like riddles—they’re meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you’re the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you will win the entire prize spot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is for $5,000. Let us load the crystal ball with the first secret item. Mystical, beautiful... what could it be? Here comes clue number one: WONDERFUL WHEN YOU ARE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: WONDERFUL WHEN YOU ARE. Good luck! To those of you who have been playing for a long time, maybe playing on our Discord or Reddit, hi everybody! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: WONDERFUL WHEN YOU ARE. "Little Xany" from Kennesaw, Georgia, said smile. "Eagle Eyes 209" said wife. "Matt the Hat" said twin.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: ALL FOR NOTHING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Let’s think about this. A smile? A twin? Clue number two: ALL FOR NOTHING. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ALL FOR NOTHING. "Pudgy Mango 529" said dream—wonderful when you are, then it goes away. "Jelly Bean Jean 04" said luck. "Horrible Joy 365" said parenting. Why would you think we’d make that the answer?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: GREAT WORK EVERYBODY. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I actually do hope to be saying that to you in a second! I hope today is the first puzzle in which every single person playing wins. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: GREAT WORK EVERYBODY. "Browns 01" from Portland, Maine, said team. "Mia's Mama 68" said Best Guess Live.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: SIGHT TO SITE TO PROVE YOU'RE RIGHT. Look at the spelling, people! 20 seconds on the clock starts now. We’ve got one more clue after this. This one's worth $5,000. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: SIGHT TO SITE TO PROVE YOU'RE RIGHT. "Cherry Lady O2" said rainbow. Someone else said IMDb.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: ONLINE BRATANNICA BUT EVEN MORE GIGANTICA. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Let’s go over them: WONDERFUL WHEN YOU ARE, ALL FOR NOTHING, GREAT WORK EVERYBODY, SIGHT TO SITE TO PROVE YOU'RE RIGHT, and ONLINE BRATANNICA BUT EVEN MORE GIGANTICA. Time is up! Let’s find out what’s in the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was WIKIPEDIA!
Clue 5: "ONLINE BRATANNICA BUT EVEN MORE GIGANTICA." Well, it’s like the encyclopedia but with way more articles, and it’s online. 3,063 got it right.
Clue 4: "SIGHT TO SITE TO PROVE YOU'RE RIGHT." WIKIPEDIA can be very helpful for proving your point in an argument, and you "site the site" to do that. 1,876 got it right.
Clue 3: "GREAT WORK EVERYBODY." Reference to the fact that anyone can edit articles on WIKIPEDIA. Only 6 people got it right.
Clue 2: "ALL FOR NOTHING." It’s all the information in the world available at no cost. 5 people got it right.
Clue 1: "WONDERFUL WHEN YOU ARE." Well, it is fantastic when you are wondering about something; it is literally wonderful when you are full of wonder.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 6,788 people guessed on clue one and exactly one person got it right! That means $5,000 is going to Devoid Market 131! Our best guesser of this first puzzle!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, prize doubles to $10,000! Before we start, our second winner last night, ValueBird 748, was playing their 97th puzzle and finally won $10,000! Perseverance pays off. Let’s load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: YOU'RE NOT WELCOME. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Simple, easy. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: YOU'RE NOT WELCOME. "Mint and Tea" said enemy. "C Bickl" said here—they just finished the sentence. "Coral Greater 589" said diary.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: IT'S ALL OVER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Honestly, with some diaries, you open them and it is just a lot of... middle school haircuts. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: IT'S ALL OVER. "Leading Sugar 306" said jail. "TF 1249" said lice. "Chosen Dryer 988" said flu. "Eric the Purple 214" said a newspaper.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: A BLAST FROM THE PAST. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Newspaper might work for this! A blast from the past. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: A BLAST FROM THE PAST. "Yam Lover 75" said Ex. "Jen's Queen 09" said cannon.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: CAN HAPPEN IN VEGAS JUST LIKE THE MOVIE. 20 seconds starts now. I feel like I've seen everything in Vegas. You can rent tanks and stuff! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: CAN HAPPEN IN VEGAS JUST LIKE THE MOVIE. "Muted Poppy 722" said married. "Purge King 25" said jackpot. "You Think" said leaving.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: NOW YOU'RE SICK AND SUCH LAST NIGHT YOU DRANK TOO MUCH. 20 seconds starts now. Final chance at $10,000! Time's up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was HANGOVER!
Clue 5: "NOW YOU'RE SICK AND SUCH LAST NIGHT YOU DRANK TOO MUCH." A lot of people may be feeling this tomorrow. Happy St. Patty's Day! 6,159 got it right.
Clue 4: "CAN HAPPEN IN VEGAS JUST LIKE THE MOVIE." Well, people are often hung over in Vegas, just like in the movie The HANGOVER. 4,666 got it right.
Clue 3: "A BLAST FROM THE PAST." You were having a good time last night and now you’re suffering the consequences; you’re reminded of your past actions. 8 got it right.
Clue 2: "IT'S ALL OVER." It can feel like your life is ending and you feel it all over your body when you have a HANGOVER. 2 got it right.
Clue 1: "YOU'RE NOT WELCOME." No one ever welcomes a HANGOVER; it is very unwelcome in most households.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 7,687 people guessed on clue one and four people got it right! Maybe they were preparing mentally for the day they're about to have! Those four winners are each going home with $2,500! Congratulations to Lucky Orchid 408, Combine Queen, ProCrown 980, and Souza 1130!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for this edition of Best Guess Live! Congratulations to all of our winners. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Get home safe! Good night!

clues: THE ANSWER IS RIGHT UNDER YOUR NOSE
HAIR THAT IS HIP JUST ABOVE THE LIP
YOU ARE HERE WHEN YOU ARE THERE
SHOP IF YOU DARE AT SPENCER'S GAP AND CLAIRE'S

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Out meow beep house behind! Welcome to Best Guess Live! Live! I’m excited, I’m pumped! I’m your host Hunter March. There is no better way to spend a Wednesday than with tens of thousands of my closest friends, you, a crystal ball, and a big pile of money.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: To me, that’s a recipe for an excellent hump day. In fact, the only thing more fun than having that big pile of money is watching you take it away from me. Honestly, it really grinds my gears. You know how much money I could have if you guys stopped winning it? I was well off before this show! Anyway, if anyone on Earth tries to take my money I will fight tooth and nail, but if it’s you specifically, you watching right now, help yourself. You deserve it.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: You’ve really had a hard week. This is your chance to make some money, and here’s how you can do it. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; now it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues—just five—but remember clues are written like riddles; they’re meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you’ll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we do not accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load the crystal ball with the first secret item. Okay, it’s looking pretty good. Here comes clue number one: TRENDY POPUP. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: TRENDY POPUP. 14 seconds left. For those of you that are new, welcome to the game. For those of you playing on Discord, hello! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: TRENDY POPUP. "Playing Blanket 862" from El Paso said BTS. "Crimson Code 261" said tent. "Como 93" said banana—I have no idea how that one works at all.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: MID COVER BAND. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. TRENDY POPUP, MID COVER BAND. Two clues down, three to go. Time's up.
