lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: MID COVER BAND. "Slothzilla" from San Antonio said "Skiims"—like the Kim Kardashian thing? What were you guys laughing at? What did I almost say? "Exo Ear" said flash mob.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: TEMPORARY WHEN DAIRY. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I thought we'd give you a little extra rhyme today. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: TEMPORARY WHEN DAIRY. "Pink Butter 9" said pimple. That's so gross sounding. "Enormous Mole" said belt—the "dairy belt"?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: THE ANSWER IS RIGHT UNDER YOUR NOSE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. It's been everything from the Grand Canyon to ChatGPT, but this answer is right under your nose. Can it get any closer? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THE ANSWER IS RIGHT UNDER YOUR NOSE. "Icy Cashew" said burp. "Polar Glowworm" said milk. "Squat Opal" said smell.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: HAIR THAT IS HIP JUST ABOVE THE LIP. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Final chance for $5,000. If you think you got it right, pull out your second phone and start recording! Time's up. Let’s find out what was in the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was a MUSTACHE. For anyone wondering what I’d look like without a beard, this is it.
Clue 5: "HAIR THAT IS HIP JUST ABOVE THE LIP." Well, they can be very hip and they grow just above... you guys get it. 5,711 got it right.
Clue 4: "THE ANSWER IS RIGHT UNDER YOUR NOSE." That's usually where you'll find a MUSTACHE. 4,658 got it right.
Clue 3: "TEMPORARY WHEN DAIRY." You get a MUSTACHE from drinking milk, but it easily goes away—your milk mustache. 74 got it right.
Clue 2: "MID COVER BAND." It is a strip or a band that covers the middle area of your face. Zero people got it right.
Clue 1: "TRENDY POPUP." They pop up above your lip, and lately they've been very trendy.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 8,948 people guessed on clue one and two people got it right! Those two winners are each going home with $2,500! Congratulations to Punctual Crown 8.44 and Brit!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let’s double the prize to $10,000. Load the crystal ball. Here is clue zero: it’s not mustache.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number one: COMBINE THEM ALL. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. COMBINE THEM ALL. If you think you know it, you know it. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: COMBINE THEM ALL. "Harry Sedan 823" said Lego. "Roman 3421" said DNA. "Foreign Gerbil 14" also said Lego. "Bountiful Hat" said Long Island Iced Tea.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: MULTI-LEVEL MARKETING. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. COMBINE THEM ALL, MULTI-LEVEL MARKETING. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: MULTI-LEVEL MARKETING. "Neb Dirt" said Meta—feels like it at times. "Rudy Fractal" said jukebox. "Squirly Ben" said recipe.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: SEE YOU IN COURT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I know the answer, but for you guys, good luck! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: SEE YOU IN COURT. "Ganty Holiday" said what was on everyone's mind: jury. "Neo 85" from Northport said pyramid. "Captive Dog" said Disney.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: YOU ARE HERE WHEN YOU ARE THERE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. No question mark at the end, I was just confused. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: YOU ARE HERE WHEN YOU ARE THERE. Someone said in the club with your fam. "Danny Z 2112" said map. "Demogorgons 113" said Zoom.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five: SHOP IF YOU DARE AT SPENCER'S GAP AND CLAIRE'S. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Final chance! Shop if you dare at Spencer's, Gap, and Claire's. Time's up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was THE MALL. "Prime Event" said Amazon, which is slowly killing the mall.
Clue 5: "SHOP IF YOU DARE AT SPENCER'S GAP AND CLAIRE'S." When you are at THE MALL, you can commonly find at least one of those stores. 5,848 people got it right.
Clue 4: "YOU ARE HERE WHEN YOU ARE THERE." There's usually a map—many of them—that say "you are here" at THE MALL. 580 people got it right.
Clue 3: "SEE YOU IN COURT." The food court! 634 got it right.
Clue 2: "MULTI-LEVEL MARKETING." THE MALL is full of marketing and generally has multiple floors. 975 got it right.
Clue 1: "COMBINE THEM ALL." Well, when you combine "them" and "all," you get "THE MALL."

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 10,487 people guessed on clue one and 129 people got it right! Not bad for Wednesday! Those 129 people are going home with $77.51 each! Congratulations to Bright Path 2, NBA U24, and Extra Smoothie!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for this edition of Best Guess Live! Thank you guys so much for playing. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Come back tomorrow and don't forget to bring your best guest! Good night!

clues: ONCE IT'S ALL GONE MORE WILL PILE ON
THE GARBAGE WE FEED UNWANTED LETTERS WE DON'T NEED
A CHEESY SAUCY BAKED PIE WE ALL LOVE TO BUY

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Behind me, meh, out out beep out! Come on, welcome to Best Guess Live! I’m your host Howie Mandel. Wow, it’s my one chance to host this week, so I’m hoping for a lot of winners! We got a lot of first-time players: Spiral Eyelid 70, Monnique 2025, and Mystical Dreamer 4, welcome to the party!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Let’s do some stretches to warm up. This is what we usually do to get ready. Think, two, three, four... decipher, two, three, four! Think once more and hold... keep thinking... and stop! Shake it out and cool down while I tell you the rules.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load a crystal ball with a secret item; it could be absolutely anything. Then I’m going to give you five clues written like riddles. You get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. It has to be the exact answer with no typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let’s win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Load that crystal ball. Here comes the first clue: WASTE OF TIME. 20 seconds on the clock.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: WASTE OF TIME. $5,000 is on the line. "Joe Says 83" said an old calendar. "Mel Kennedy Mason" said pickleball—I love pickleball! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: WASTE OF SPACE. Clock starts now. We've already got over 5,000 submitted answers. "Perfect Chaos 989" said golf—you just angered a lot of doctors! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: WASTE OF PAPER SOMETIMES. Clock starts now. Think about how I said that. "Dusty Washcloth 54" hit a parade. "The Amber Show" said trees—you don't live in the Amazon, do you? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: ONCE IT'S ALL GONE MORE WILL PILE ON. Clock starts now. "Rachel Always Wins 454" said taxes. Let's find out if you win this time, Rachel! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: THE GARBAGE WE FEED UNWANTED LETTERS WE DON'T NEED. Clock starts now. Clue one: Waste of Time. Clue two: Waste of Space. Clue three: Waste of Paper. Clue four: Once it's gone more pile on. Time is up!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: This just in: the answer is JUNK MAIL. Look at that, I got a lot of it!
Clue 5: "THE GARBAGE WE FEED UNWANTED LETTERS WE DON'T NEED." JUNK MAIL goes straight into the trash; that's what we "feed." 2,623 got it correct.
Clue 4: "ONCE IT'S ALL GONE MORE WILL PILE ON." We all receive an endless supply of spam; it never stops. 349 got it correct.
Clue 3: "WASTE OF PAPER SOMETIMES." In the form of snail mail, it's a total waste of paper. 228 got it correct.
Clue 2: "WASTE OF SPACE." It takes up space in your mailbox and your inbox. 24 got it correct.
Clue 1: "WASTE OF TIME." It takes time to shuffle through it when you could be doing literally anything else.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 5,160 guesses on the first clue and three of you got it right! You are each splitting $1,666.66! Congratulations to Saintly Gumbo 801, F100D3D, and Third Dance 486.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Prize doubles to $10,000. Load that crystal ball. Clue number one: CATCH SOME Z'S. Clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: CATCH SOME Z'S. "Marginal Champ 909" said zebra tag. "Jason 3612" said a nap. "Tanny Tan" said sheep. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: A DISAPPEARING PUZZLE. Clock starts now. Almost 11,000 guesses on that first clue. "Busted Biscuits 33" said a zipper. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: CIRCLE IN THE SQUARE. Clock starts now. "Brief Bark 879" said Tetris. "Howie is Adorbs 243" said boxing. I don't know why I like that answer, but thank you! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: IT'S A TOSSUP. Clock starts now. "Sali Eclair 293" said marriage. "Khaki Hutch 710" said Pac-Man. "M Shark 7773" said layup. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: A CHEESY SAUCY BAKED PIE WE ALL LOVE TO BUY. Clock starts now. "Sneaky Acrobat" said "puzza." Oh no, puzza! Spelling counts! Time is up. Let's find out what's in the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It’s PIZZA! Who ordered the puzza?
Clue 5: "A CHEESY SAUCY BAKED PIE WE ALL LOVE TO BUY." That is what this is! 5,420 got it right.
Clue 4: "IT'S A TOSSUP." That's how they make it—they toss and spin the dough. 484 got it right.
Clue 3: "CIRCLE IN THE SQUARE." A PIZZA is traditionally a circle in a square box. 73 got it right.
Clue 2: "A DISAPPEARING PUZZLE." Slices are cut into shapes like a puzzle, and as you eat, the circle disappears. 359 got it right.
Clue 1: "CATCH SOME Z'S." PIZZA is also known as "Za," which has two Z's!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 10,743 of you guessed on clue one and 459 of you got it correct! That means each of you is taking home $21.78! Congratulations to Dear Sier 67 and Destin Adam.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That is it for Best Guess Live! Congratulations again to all the winners. We are here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Come back tomorrow! Good night!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Out me up! Leopold beep out! Welcome to Best Guess Live. I’m excited—today's a big day. I'm your host Hunter March. Thank you for being with us tonight, especially since the new movie Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man debuts on Netflix today! If you are a fan of the series like I am, this movie is a much-anticipated continuation of the Shelby family's story.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: But before you immerse yourself in the seedy underbelly of 1940s England... no, I tried and I'm done trying, I will no longer be weird. Can I interest you in $15,000 right now? Here’s how it works: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Then I’ll give you five clues written like riddles. You get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball. Mystical, magical... what could it be? Here comes clue number one: ARCHITECTURAL DIGEST. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ARCHITECTURAL DIGEST. We're off to the races! "Valicious One" said Legos. "Kyle" says "Angel, will you marry me?" Wait, he messaged me before the show—this is real! Angel, if you’re named Angel and dating a Kyle, he’s proposing right now! Good luck! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue two: LETTER TWO GOT A RASH. 20 seconds on the clock. I apologize to every other Angel and Kyle who have been together for eight years and just had a very awkward moment. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: LETTER TWO GOT A RASH. "K Marie" from Toledo said grapes. "Dragon 989" said Statue of Liberty.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue three: IT'S NOT THE HAIRDO BUT OFTEN GETS COMBED. 20 seconds starts now. "Cyan Coffin" said wig. "Laugh 370" said lice. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue four: HONEY I'M HOME. 20 seconds starts now. Fun fact: my grandfather was on I Love Lucy multiple times—the shoe salesman, the doctor! "Chatty Monster" said perm. "Burly Pencil" said the 50s. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: AN ABODE FOR BEES SO STAY AWAY PLEASE. Your final chance. Good luck! Time's up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was a BEEHIVE.
Clue 5: "AN ABODE FOR BEES SO STAY AWAY PLEASE." 5,690 got it right.
Clue 4: "HONEY I'M HOME." The home where they make their honey. 3,045 got it right.
Clue 3: "IT'S NOT THE HAIRDO BUT OFTEN GETS COMBED." It’s not the BEEHIVE hairdo, but hives have honeycombs. 2,068 got it right.
Clue 2: "LETTER TWO GOT A RASH." The second letter of the alphabet is "B," and "Hives" is a rash. BEEHIVE. 320 got it right.
Clue 1: "ARCHITECTURAL DIGEST." Their home is full of honey you can digest.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 8 people got it right on clue one! Each is going home with $625.00! Congrats to Jay Remy, Maria 628, Daisy Dw, Stout Sprout, and Yeezys.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Prize doubles to $10,000! By the way, Kyle just messaged back—Angel said YES! Congratulations to Kyle and Angel!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: MIND READERS UNITE. 20 seconds starts now. "Clingy Porcupine" said Comic-Con. "Pro Squirrel 105" said twins. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue two: RIVALRY FOR DNA. 20 seconds starts now. "Baked Goodies" said ESPN. "Nancy" said RNA. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue three: FRIENDS ARE NOT WELCOME. 20 seconds starts now. "Gastly Rose" said reruns. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue four: BATTLE THY NEIGHBOR FOR MONEY. 20 seconds starts now. "Black Dog 239" said Clue. "Minute Cake" said gambling. "Amanda Demands" said Girl Scout cookies. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue: POLL THE COUNTRY FOR THE MOST; STEVE HARVEY IS THE HOST. Final chance at $10,000! Time's up! Let's see the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was FAMILY FEUD.
Clue 5: "POLL THE COUNTRY FOR THE MOST; STEVE HARVEY IS THE HOST." 5,647 got it right.
Clue 4: "BATTLE THY NEIGHBOR FOR MONEY." There is always another family right across from you. 529 got it right.
Clue 3: "FRIENDS ARE NOT WELCOME." It’s almost always family members competing, not friends. 70 got it right.
Clue 2: "RIVALRY FOR DNA." A rivalry is a feud, and you share DNA with family. FAMILY FEUD. 7 got it right.
Clue 1: "MIND READERS UNITE." Because you’re trying to guess what America thinks!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Nobody got it right on clue one! So the 7 people on clue two are splitting $10,000! That is $1,428.57 each! Congratulations to Echo Symbol, Sure Snail, Elarto, Gimod, and Don Nick.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That’s it for tonight! Go watch Peaky Blinders and have a great weekend! See you Monday! Good night!

clues: YOU HAVE TO GIVE TO RECEIVE
TO GET THE ANSWER PLEASE PULL FORWARD

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live, everybody! Happy Monday! I’m your host Hunter March. Before we play today’s game, I want to give a special shout-out to a very special couple. If you were playing on Friday, you know who I’m talking about—Kyle and Angel from Mesquite, Texas! Kyle proposed right here on Best Guess Live during Friday’s game. Well, I guess technically I proposed to Angel... which actually makes me think I have two fiances now, which is a huge issue. Regardless, we’ll get it all sorted out. Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: I can't promise that anybody else is going to get a diamond ring out of tonight's game, but I do promise that there's $15,000 up for grabs. Now, I propose we go over the rules. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Before each round, we load our crystal ball with a secret item. Then I'll give you five clues written like riddles.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue, you've got 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. It has to be the exact answer with no typos. Everyone that locks in the correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball. Here's your first clue: VERY TENSE SITUATION. 20 seconds starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: VERY TENSE SITUATION. Proposing is scary—good job on you, Kyle! "Sofa Loaf" from Benton Harbor said a knot. "Ripe Lemming" said muscle. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue two: ALTERNATING POINTS OF VIEW. 20 seconds starts now. Clue one: Very tense situation. Clue two: Alternating points of view. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ALTERNATING POINTS OF VIEW. "Luffy" said debate. "Kenzosaurus" said yoga. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue three: SPRING FORWARD FALL BACK. 20 seconds starts now. Three clues down, two to go. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: SPRING FORWARD FALL BACK. "Blue Dream 4" said time—time does spring forward and fall back.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue four: FORM FITTING FABRIC ON A PLUS-SIZE FRAME. 20 seconds starts now. "Star 679911" said Spanx. "Moshop 379" said sheets—the fitted sheet. "Gleaming Champ 72" said corset. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue: JUMP HIGH BOUNCE HARD OFTEN IN YOUR OWN BACKYARD. 20 seconds starts now. I think it's all starting to make sense! Time's up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was A TRAMPOLINE.
Clue 5: "JUMP HIGH BOUNCE HARD OFTEN IN YOUR OWN BACKYARD." You can have a blast on one right outside your home. 3,715 got it right.
Clue 4: "FORM FITTING FABRIC ON A PLUS-SIZE FRAME." It features a taut canvas fabric stretched out on a big metal frame. 349 got it right.
Clue 3: "SPRING FORWARD FALL BACK." The springs send you forward into the air, and gravity makes you fall back down. 274 got it right.
Clue 2: "ALTERNATING POINTS OF VIEW." When you're jumping, your point of view is literally changing—you're up here, then you're down there. 37 got it right.
Clue 1: "VERY TENSE SITUATION." The mesh you jump on is stretched out with lots of tension.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 65 people got it right on clue one! Each is going home with $76.92! Congratulations to GDY2 Shoes, m Destroyer, Zoe Girl, and Emily.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, the prize doubles to $10,000! Let's load the crystal ball. Clue number one: DO NOT ENTER. 20 seconds starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: DO NOT ENTER. This is not a trick! "Salty Dryer 221" wrote "exit"—that was last week’s answer! "Awesome Fuel 797" said zoo. "Clingy Porcupine" said Gandalf—"Thou shalt not pass!" Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue two: OUTSIDE YOUR WHEELHOUSE. 20 seconds starts now. "Stray Ferret" from Jacksonville said jail. I hope jail is outside your wheelhouse! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue three: YOU HAVE TO GIVE TO RECEIVE. 20 seconds starts now. "Vim V" said ATM—you have to give your card to receive money. "Barney Rubble Fan 387" said a baby. What does that mean?! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue four: TO GET THE ANSWER PLEASE PULL FORWARD. 20 seconds starts now. I'll do the voice: "To get your answer, please pull forward!" "Ginger Snap" said tug-of-war. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue: TOO LAZY TO GO INSIDE ORDER HERE FROM YOUR RIDE. 20 seconds starts now. Final chance to enter! Time's up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was THE DRIVE-THRU.
Clue 5: "TOO LAZY TO GO INSIDE ORDER HERE FROM YOUR RIDE." Drive-thru windows make ordering very convenient. 527 got it right.
Clue 4: "TO GET THE ANSWER PLEASE PULL FORWARD." You pull your car forward to pick up your order. 986 got it right.
Clue 3: "YOU HAVE TO GIVE TO RECEIVE." First you give your order, then you give your money, then you receive your food. 8 got it right.
Clue 2: "OUTSIDE YOUR WHEELHOUSE." The wheelhouse is your car; the drive-thru experience happens just outside of it. Only one person got it right!
Clue 1: "DO NOT ENTER." There is no reason to go inside—the windows are for the outside.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: One person got it right on clue one, meaning they're going home with $10,000 all to themselves! Congratulations to Just Josh and U91! Unbelievable!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for this edition of Best Guess Live! Congratulations to Just Josh and U91 and Jace Guitar who won earlier. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Good night!

clues: WAKE UP, YOU'LL MISS THE ANSWER
BRIEF SNOOZE DURING THE DAY, FEELING FRESH NOW...YAY!
CHILLIN', SPILLIN', OR A LAIR FOR A VILLAIN
IF THE LAVA'S A-FLOWIN', YOU BEST BE A-GOIN'

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Up me up! Out up! Come on everybody, we’re going! Welcome to Best Guess Live! Hello, hello, how you doing? I’m your host Hunter March. Witnessing my dreams come true every single day when I get to say my favorite four words in the English language: "Here's how you play!"

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues written like riddles. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball. It's looking good today! Here's your first clue: A LIMITED ENGAGEMENT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: A LIMITED ENGAGEMENT. This is the warm-up! "Phoenix Fire 87" from Raleigh said speed dating. "Peeps Reloaded" said gym. "Many Mini" said Vegas. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue two: SEMI-RETIRED. 20 seconds starts now. Vegas is out—everyone there is fully retired! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: SEMI-RETIRED. "Minor Isle 440" said Laboo. "Samari Sam" said Lent. "Gnarly Hopscotch" said an old trucker.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue three: DISCO POWER CAT. 20 seconds starts now. "Alyssa Cratch" put Pokemon—I think she was thinking Pikachu! That is a disco power cat. "Dr G" put me. "David Rules" put John Travolta. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue four: WAKE UP YOU'LL MISS THE ANSWER. 20 seconds starts now. "Ruthless Albert" from San Antonio put dream—if you wake up, you'll miss the answer. Good one. "391" put Tamagotchi. "Cheesy Candy" put alarm. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: BRIEF SNOOZE DURING THE DAY FEELING FRESH NOW YAY. 20 seconds starts now. If you think about it before you lock in, I’m right there with you. Time's up. Let's find out what was in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was NAP.
Clue 5: "BRIEF SNOOZE DURING THE DAY FEELING FRESH NOW YAY." You guys get it—that's a NAP. 9,569 people got it right.
Clue 4: "WAKE UP YOU'LL MISS THE ANSWER." The answer is NAP, so if you wake up, you're missing the NAP. 1,176 got it right.
Clue 3: "DISCO POWER CAT." Those are all types of naps: a disco nap, a power nap, and a catnap. 143 got it right.
Clue 2: "SEMI-RETIRED." You're not fully retired for the night, but you're taking a little break from the day. 169 got it right.
Clue 1: "A LIMITED ENGAGEMENT." You're engaging in sleep for a limited amount of time.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 7,001 people guessed on clue one and six people got it right! Each is going home with $833.33! Congratulations to Vilma Agular, Lemon Rocket, RN Dog Mom, and Angelic Ford.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, the prize doubles to $10,000. Load the crystal ball. Clue number one: WAIT FOR IT... 20 seconds starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: WAIT FOR IT... If you know it, don't wait! "Purple Cobra 831" said flower. "Verse Peak 781" said DJ—waiting for the beat drop. "Charlie's House" said jury duty. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Second clue: YOU'RE GETTING WARMER... (with ellipses). 20 seconds starts now. I missed an ellipses in a recent episode and got a talking to, so I'm hitting them hard! "Tater" said summer. "Mount Blazeit" said Hades. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Third clue: RUN... 20 seconds starts now. Clue one: Wait for it. Clue two: You're getting warmer. Clue three: Run. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: RUN... "Mama Bear 057" from London, Ohio, said tag. "Chili Con Barney" said pantyhose—you get a run in your pantyhose! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue four: CHILLING SPILLING OR A LAYER FOR A VILLAIN. 20 seconds starts now. Chilling, spilling, or a layer for a villain. 5 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: "Bork Lover" said bath. "Broken Bandana 225" said jail.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue: IF THE LAVAS ARE FLOWING YOU BEST BE A GOING. 20 seconds starts now. Final chance for $10,000! There's only a couple things this could be. Time is up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was VOLCANO.
Clue 5: "IF THE LAVAS ARE FLOWING YOU BEST BE A GOING." 3,371 people got it right.
Clue 4: "CHILLING SPILLING OR A LAYER FOR A VILLAIN." VOLCANOes can be dormant (chilling) or active (spilling), and movie villains often live in them. 66 got it right.
Clue 3: "RUN..." Because that is what you do when one erupts! 581 got it right.
Clue 2: "YOU'RE GETTING WARMER..." You're getting closer to the answer, and they are hot! 23 got it right.
Clue 1: "WAIT FOR IT..." You never know when a VOLCANO is going to erupt!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 10,990 people guessed on clue one and nine people got it right! Those nine people are each going home with $1,111.11! Congratulations to 10th Server, Mama J-bird, and Meade Death.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for Best Guess Live! Congratulations to all of our winners. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. See you tomorrow! Good night!

clues: I'LL TAKE A BLT, MAKE IT SPEEDY, AND HOLD THE WITCH
DON'T MAKE A STINK. THAT SAND MAKES YOU SINK
WHEN THE REEF SAYS, "WHERE'S THE BEEF?"
RHYMING DISH OF SEA AND MEAT THAT MANY PEOPLE LOVE TO EAT

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Out! Me out out behind! Beep! Everybody looking! Welcome to Best Guess Live! I’m Howie Mandel, I’m going to be your host. It's so much fun to put this show together every day. When you really stop and think about it... I just stopped and thought about it. The real winners on Best Guess Live are us—the staff and crew! It’s us, people, because we get to experience the joy of helping you win. Although I guess that would make you the winners... but since we get to help you win, we’re the winners? Okay, I'm confused.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: I guess it’s one of those classic win-win situations. But when there’s more than one winner, it’s like a win-win-win situation! And when there’s a lot of winners, it’s like a win-win-win-win-win! You get it. Here are the rules. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be absolutely anything.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Then I’ll give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they make you think! After each clue you’ll get 20 seconds to submit an answer. But you only get one best guess per round. Not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load the crystal ball. We got a lot of first-time players: Tiny Might 03 and Royal Poncho 353, welcome! Here comes clue number one: MASS TRANSIT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: MASS TRANSIT. $5,000 at stake! A lot of people taking a shot right off the bat. "Fake Domain 175" said subway. "I Need Tuition Money" (is that a name or a request?) said Crocs. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: HELP WANTED SIGN. Clock starts now. Almost 11,000 submitted answers already. "Skinny Horseshoe 298" said crying—because you want help if you're crying? A lot of people cry on the subway, I get it. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: WRONG WAY, SUCKER. Clock starts now. I like saying "sucker." "Jimmy Domino 234" said popsicle stick. "Little Miss Riot" said jail. Mass transit to jail? You live in a rough neighborhood! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: I'LL TAKE A BLT, MAKE IT SPEEDY, AND HOLD THE WITCH. Clock starts now. These are good riddles! "Burly Ice Cream 484" said a big luxurious tiger. Okay! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: DON'T MAKE A STINK. THAT SAND MAKES YOU SINK. Clock starts now. Clue one: Mass Transit. Clue two: Help Wanted Sign. Clue three: Wrong Way, Sucker. Clue four: BLT hold the witch. Time is up! Let's find out what's in that crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It’s QUICKSAND! Wait a minute, was that me going down in the video? You guys are nuts!
Clue 5: "DON'T MAKE A STINK. THAT SAND MAKES YOU SINK." When you're in the QUICKSAND, you don't make a stink. You want to stay still or you'll sink. 5,358 got it correct.
Clue 4: "I'LL TAKE A BLT, MAKE IT SPEEDY, AND HOLD THE WITCH." A BLT is a sandwich. "Hold the witch" leaves "Sand." "Make it speedy" is "Quick." Put it together: QUICKSAND. 403 got it right.
Clue 3: "WRONG WAY, SUCKER." QUICKSAND sucks you down, which is not the direction you want to go! Zero got it right.
Clue 2: "HELP WANTED SIGN." When you start sinking, it’s a sign you want help! Zero got it right.
Clue 1: "MASS TRANSIT." Your body is a mass, and QUICKSAND moves it.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Almost 11,000 people guessed on clue one and nobody got it right! That means the 403 people on clue number four are splitting the $5,000. That’s $12.40 each!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The prize doubles to $10,000! Let's load that crystal ball. Clue number one: POPULAR FIRST DATE. Clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: POPULAR FIRST DATE. "Charlie's Horse 38" said January—that is a popular first date, in the top 12! "Ripe Cheetah 452" said movies. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: OPPOSITES ATTRACT. Clock starts now. Almost 13,000 people guessed on that first clue. "Mark It Dude 95" said crossing your eyes. I’m going to guess you’re single. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: FANCY MEETING YOU HERE. Clock starts now. "Car 585" said Tinder Platinum. "I've Got 2325" said In-N-Out. That is a good burger. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: WHEN THE REEF SAYS, "WHERE'S THE BEEF?" Clock starts now. "Dorothy Marker 21" said a cowfish. "Olly Pauly 10" said Arby's. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: RHYMING DISH OF SEA AND MEAT THAT MANY PEOPLE LOVE TO EAT. Clock starts now. Last chance! Time's up. 30,000 submissions! Let's see the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It is SURF AND TURF! I love lobster.
Clue 5: "RHYMING DISH OF SEA AND MEAT THAT MANY PEOPLE LOVE TO EAT." SURF AND TURF rhymes and it's sea and meat. 5,424 got it correct.
Clue 4: "WHEN THE REEF SAYS, 'WHERE'S THE BEEF?'" The reef is the surf; the beef is the turf. 941 got it right.
Clue 3: "FANCY MEETING YOU HERE." They are both fancy proteins. When does a lobster meet a steak? Fancy meeting you here! 8 got it correct.
Clue 2: "OPPOSITES ATTRACT." One is from the land, one is from the sea, and they attract each other on the dish. 6 got it correct.
Clue 1: "POPULAR FIRST DATE." Seafood and cows grew up in different neighborhoods. This is their first time meeting! Plus, it's a popular order on a date.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 12,787 guesses on clue one and zero people got it right. So the six of you on clue number two are splitting $10,000! That is $1,666.66 each! Congratulations to Loud Py 95, Sushiro 9091, and Leis!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That is it for Best Guess Live! Congratulations to the winners. Now head over to Netflix for their exclusive coverage of tonight’s MLB season opener between the Yankees and the Giants happening right now! It's the first time the MLB season has kicked off on a global streaming service. Go check it out! See you tomorrow! Good night!

clues: DON'T WHINE, IT'S FOR OPENING THE WINE
BACK IN BLACK COVERED BY THE WHITE STRIPES
THE OBVIOUS REFEREE OF THE ANIMAL KINGDOM

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Out! Out! Out! Behind! Come on, we're going! Welcome to Best Guess Live! Hello, hello, how we doing? I’m your host Hunter March. It's only Thursday and we have already added 55 people to the winner circle this week. That means we are averaging over 168 winners per day—that’s a lot!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: You know how many winners other game shows have every day? Jeopardy? One max. Wheel of Fortune? One. Family Feud? Five, but it's only one family. Let's be honest, it's only going to one person. Technically, we're 168 times financially better than those shows. That's not my opinion; that's just math, people!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: If you want to get on the winner's train and help us continue our undeniable dominance, here is how the show works. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues. Remember though, those clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. And if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you win that entire prize pot! Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball with the first secret item. Possibilities are endless! Here comes clue number one: MUST OR BUST. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: MUST OR BUST. "Loyal Geranium 492" from Florida said high five—maybe you get left hanging, it's a bust. "Scooby Pup 101" said darts—you got to get it right on the right number. "Turtle Pie" said gas station bathroom—I don't like that their name is Turtle Pie either. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: PLUNGING NECKLINE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I'll let you think. It's not my game to lose for you; it's your game to win for you. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: PLUNGING NECKLINE. "Miss Ginger Moon" from Orlando said elevator—ideally not plunging too fast. "Twisted Sunset 503" from Detroit said V-neck. "Terrific Fish" said giraffe—they've got a very long neck.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: FOR OPEN-MINDED PEOPLE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I don't look great in a V-neck, so for me, V-neck's out. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: FOR OPEN-MINDED PEOPLE. "Southern Bell 619" said zipper—I guess if your brain surgeon left a zipper, cool. "JNL20" said burped people.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: YOU KNOW THE DRILL. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. One more after this. 13 seconds left. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: YOU KNOW THE DRILL. "Zelda Husky" from Jacksonville said mullet. Yeah, you know the drill, time to let people know we fight!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five, your fifth and final clue: DON'T WHINE IT'S FOR OPENING THE WINE. 20 seconds starts now. Thank you for playing on this Thursday! Over 30,000 people are in here right now trying to win this $5,000. Time is up. Let’s find out what's inside the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer is a CORKSCREW. I one time on a date tried to open a wine bottle for the first time and destroyed the cork—it ruined everything.
Clue 5: "DON'T WHINE IT'S FOR OPENING THE WINE." Well, that is how you open a bottle of wine. 7,079 got it right.
Clue 4: "YOU KNOW THE DRILL." CORKSCREWs look like drills; they’re just little drills. 7 got it right.
Clue 3: "FOR OPEN-MINDED PEOPLE." It’s very useful when opening wine is on your "mind." 4 people got it right.
Clue 2: "PLUNGING NECKLINE." You plunge a CORKSCREW into the "neck" of a wine bottle. One person got it right!
Clue 1: "MUST OR BUST." When you want a nice bottle of wine, you need a CORKSCREW or you got to bust the wine bottle open.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 7,031 people guessed on clue one and everyone busted! The one person on clue number two is going home with $5,000! Our best guesser for today is Spacious O!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, the prize doubles to $10,000! Load the crystal ball. Clue number one: STRANGELY FAMILIAR. 20 seconds starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: STRANGELY FAMILIAR. A lot of things are strange, a lot are familiar. "Everything is Cake" from Columbia said fan. "Domestic Ranch 803" said salsa. "Clean Garbage" put Benedict Cumberbatch—he's Doctor Strange! Our resident nerd figured that out immediately. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: CONFUSING IN A LINEUP. 20 seconds starts now. Must you get your clue in now, or do you risk a potential bust? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: CONFUSING IN A LINEUP. "Alpha Ash 4" from New Hampshire said twin. "Fiona Rules" said triplets. Everyone's on the same path.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: APPEARS IN BOOZE BRANDS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. If you don't win tonight, there's always tomorrow! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: APPEARS IN BOOZE BRANDS. "Strict Cyborg 207" from New York said mascot. "Donald Truck 211" said alcohol—could you imagine if it was that easy for a clue three?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: BACK IN BLACK COVERED BY THE WHITE STRIPES. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. This show was created by a very musical group of people and they wanted me to sing the Back in Black part. I'm not great at that Australian accent. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: BACK IN BLACK COVERED BY THE WHITE STRIPES. "Flat Chair 227" from North Carolina said NASCAR—a road with white stripes. "Affordable Dynamite" put banger.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue: THE OBVIOUS REFEREE OF THE ANIMAL KINGDOM. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Final chance for $10,000! Time's up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was a ZEBRA!
Clue 5: "THE OBVIOUS REFEREE OF THE ANIMAL KINGDOM." Referees look exactly like ZEBRAs. 6,005 got it right.
Clue 4: "BACK IN BLACK COVERED BY THE WHITE STRIPES." Their body is black and they're covered in white stripes. 1,336 got it right.
Clue 3: "APPEARS IN BOOZE BRANDS." The end of "Boo-ze" is "ZE" and the start of "Br-ands" is "BR." Put them together: ZEBRA. 20 got it right.
Clue 2: "CONFUSING IN A LINEUP." Do all ZEBRAs look exactly the same? It'd be very confusing in a lineup. 23 got it right.
Clue 1: "STRANGELY FAMILIAR." They have the familiar shape of a horse but they look insane—worst camouflage ever.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 7,568 people guessed on clue one and exactly one person got it right! That means one person is going home with $10,000! Congratulations to Y Dog!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for this edition of Best Guess Live! Congratulations to both of our winners. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Come back tomorrow! Good night!

clues: LOOK DIFFERENT BUT LOOK THE SAME
PART OF HER CREW WHEN SHE SAYS "I DO."
BIGFOOT TO GRAVEDIGGER... LIKE PICKUPS BUT WAY BIGGER

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Behoop out up! Come on, we're going! Welcome to Best Guess Live! Happy Friday everybody, I’m your host Hunter March. Last night we had one winner in our first round and one winner in the second round—very big paydays! I just wanted to share some interesting stats about our winners.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Spacious O, who won the $5,000 corkscrew puzzle, has played a total of 68 rounds and this was their very first win. Well deserved! W Dog, who won $10,000 last night with the word "zebra," sent us a video of her win. Before that win, she had played 91 rounds! Tonight could be your night, so just stick with it everybody!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues written like riddles. After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer and we don't accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Remember, if possible, make a video of you and your friends playing! Send it to us with the hashtag #bestlive. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball. A lot of money here! Here comes clue number one: ON DECK CIRCLE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: ON DECK CIRCLE. Over 25,000 people playing right now, welcome! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue one: ON DECK CIRCLE. "Ditterton" from East Liverpool, Ohio, said Minecraft. "Birthday Bobby" said helipad on a yacht. Sound like Birthday Bobby has a yacht, so I hope he does not win tonight!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue two: THE CHOSEN ONES. 20 seconds starts now. Tonight you guys could be the chosen ones! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: THE CHOSEN ONES. "Diagonal Peep 37" from Massachusetts said hug—making a circle with your arms? "Whiny Spinach" said The Sims. "Danny Dimes 39" said Hunter and Howie—I do feel chosen!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue three: HER SIDE OF THE STORY. 20 seconds starts now. I've got some Howie stories... oh, do I have some Howie stories! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: HER SIDE OF THE STORY. "Danny J1122" said coin—the other side of the coin? "Austin Ryan" said jury duty. "Amanda" says the truth.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue four: LOOK DIFFERENT BUT LOOK THE SAME. 20 seconds starts now. Good luck! Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: LOOK DIFFERENT BUT LOOK THE SAME. "Twisted Sunset 503" from Detroit said mirror. "Grapple Space 303" said sink—Grapple Space, I don't think tonight's your night.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: PART OF HER CREW WHEN SHE SAYS "I DO." 20 seconds starts now. I think a lot of people are going to figure it out on this last clue. Time is up. Let’s find out what was inside the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was BRIDESMAIDS.
Clue 5: "PART OF HER CREW WHEN SHE SAYS 'I DO.'" Her bridesmaids are the besties; they deserve to be there when she's saying I do. 4,001 got it right.
Clue 4: "LOOK DIFFERENT BUT LOOK THE SAME." They traditionally dress very alike but are usually all very different looking people. 133 got it right.
Clue 3: "HER SIDE OF THE STORY." In the love story that is a wedding, they all stand at the bride's side. 106 got it right.
Clue 2: "THE CHOSEN ONES." The bride carefully chooses her bridesmaids—they're the chosen ones. 6 people got it right.
Clue 1: "ON DECK CIRCLE." The bridesmaids are her close "circle" of friends that just stand off to the side of her, usually on the same deck of the wedding ceremony.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 9,362 guesses on clue one and zero people got it right! That means the six people on clue number two are going home with $833.33 each! Congratulations to Tango Pyramid 223, Master (or Jodster), Furry Pest, and DadFed.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, the prize doubles to $10,000! Before we start, let's check out this video from Annie Miller from Lancaster, Ohio, who won $10,000 on a recent round. She says it was very much needed and she's so grateful. We are grateful to you, Annie!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let’s load the crystal ball. Clue number one: HIGH ROLLER. 20 seconds starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: HIGH ROLLER. "Padman" said a lint roller. "Better Wins" said poker. "Hilly Meadow 58" said curlers. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue two: HEAVY METAL STAR. 20 seconds starts now. "Bella's 123" from Newark, New Jersey, said a limousine. "Fran the Man" said Thomas the Tank Engine—literally one of the heaviest metaliest stars there is! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue three: SUPERSIZ ME. 20 seconds starts now. "Jordan's Parents" from Jacksonville said Sun. "Dry Bathtub 456" said protein shake. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue four: RIDE OF FRANKENSTEIN. 20 seconds starts now. Frankenstein's monster... was his ride fire? "CB Ultimate 467" said Tower of Terror. "Great Nate 98" said jet ski—the most California answer ever. Time is up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: BIGFOOT TO GRAVEDIGGER LIKE PICKUPS BUT WAY BIGGER. 20 seconds starts now. Final chance! Time is up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was MONSTER TRUCK!
Clue 5: "BIGFOOT TO GRAVEDIGGER LIKE PICKUPS BUT WAY BIGGER." Bigfoot and Gravedigger are iconic monster trucks. 3,844 got it right.
Clue 4: "RIDE OF FRANKENSTEIN." Frankenstein's "monster" and trucks. MONSTER TRUCK. 192 got it right.
Clue 3: "SUPERSIZ ME." They are supersized versions of regular trucks. 169 got it right.
Clue 2: "HEAVY METAL STAR." They are made of very heavy metal and are the stars of the shows. 188 got it right.
Clue 1: "HIGH ROLLER." They are very tall and "rolling" is literally their thing.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 7,677 people guessed on clue one and 291 people got it right! They are each going home with $34.36! Congratulations to Mona Wills, Vanilla Corgi, Rosco P, and Soul Train!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for this episode of Best Guess Live! Congratulations again to all of our winners. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. Come back on Monday! Good night everybody!

clues: DO YOU SEE WHAT I SEE?
THINGS WILL NEVER BE THE SAME
BILLOWY PUFFS HIGH IN THE SKY ARE A TREAT FOR THE EYE
A SNARKY REMARKY THAT MAY TURN DARKY
YA KNOW WHAT I LOVE? GERMS
SHARPLY IRONIC WHEN YOU SAY "OH, THAT'S ICONIC."

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Behoop house behind! Out me up! Come on, we're going! Welcome to Best Guess Live! I’m your host Howie Mandel. Look who's here: Squat Bus 946 is returning! I love when you come back. Scan Man 0425, first-time player, welcome to the party!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Since we started doing this show, a lot of people come up to me in the street and ask, "Howie, how do you come up with those clues?" And I always tell them the same thing: "I don't know what you're talking about, my name is Gary, leave me alone."

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: But tonight I'm finally revealing the truth. We have a clue mine in southern Oklahoma where hundreds of brave workers dig clues out of the earth and ship them to Los Angeles where they're polished by a staff in our clue warehouse. At least I assume that's how it works—I don't really pay much attention in the meetings.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, each round has one puzzle. Before each round we’re going to load a crystal ball with a secret item; it could be absolutely anything. Then I give you five clues written like riddles. You get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. It has to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball. Here comes clue number one: YOU JUST SAW IT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: YOU JUST SAW IT. "Big Bad Ben 78" said a magician's assistant. You just saw it! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: DO YOU SEE WHAT I SEE. Clock starts now. We've already had over 10,000 guesses. "Gone Twirl 454" said it's an illusion. This is not an illusion—I'm actually gorgeous! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: THINGS WILL NEVER BE THE SAME. Clock starts now. "Limitless Coffee 343" said a lava lamp—cool answer. "Makimo 1" said the past. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: 100% NATURAL SUNBLOCK. Clock starts now. "Flighty Earthworm 586" said a tree. "Brenda 420" said a tan—kind of? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: BILLOWY PUFFS HIGH IN THE SKY ARE A TREAT FOR THE EYE. Clock starts now. I think I just gave it to you, people! Time is up! Let's find out what's in the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It's a CLOUD!
Clue 5: "BILLOWY PUFFS HIGH IN THE SKY ARE A TREAT FOR THE EYE." People have been staring up at them since time began. 4,842 got it correct.
Clue 4: "100% NATURAL SUNBLOCK." Clouds are natural and they block the sun. 693 got it correct.
Clue 3: "THINGS WILL NEVER BE THE SAME." Clouds are constantly moving, changing, and disappearing. Wait five minutes and it's totally different. 57 got it correct.
Clue 2: "DO YOU SEE WHAT I SEE." People often interpret clouds to be different objects—one sees a dog, another sees a tiger. 28 got it correct.
Clue 1: "YOU JUST SAW IT." The crystal ball is literally filled with clouds! When it got loaded, I said "You just saw it!" I was trying to help you people!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 10,332 of you guessed on clue one and 44 of you got it correct! You are each splitting $113.63! Congratulations to EC Money Sniper, Hannah 4SI, and Robotic Crab 701.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Prize doubles to $10,000! Tomorrow, everyone who plays is going to get a special offer from Skechers. It's going to be a prize-a-palooza! But right now, let's load the crystal ball. Clue number one: A DIRECT MISDIRECT. Clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: A DIRECT MISDIRECT. "Shiny Sunset 562" said a goalie. A goalie deflects, but a "ten" is closer! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: OH THIS CLUE IS GREAT (said sarcastically). Clock starts now. It's not only what I say, it's how I say it! "Hilarious Tombstone 432" said Tony the Tiger. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: A SNARKY REMARKY THAT MAY TURN DARKY. Clock starts now. "Forever Freddy 76" thinks the answer is an insult. "Easterly Jin 259" said a comma. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: YOU KNOW WHAT I LOVE? GERMS (said sarcastically). Clock starts now. You know I hate germs! "Sunny My Honey 92" said a bathroom door handle. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: SHARPLY IRONIC WHEN YOU SAY "OH THAT'S ICONIC." Clock starts now. Final chance to guess! Time is up. Let's see the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It is SARCASM! Oh wow, this is the best game I've ever been part of! (sarcastic)
Clue 5: "SHARPLY IRONIC WHEN YOU SAY 'OH THAT'S ICONIC.'" That was me portraying SARCASM. 735 got it correct.
Clue 4: "YOU KNOW WHAT I LOVE? GERMS." You know I hate germs; I was being sarcastic! 1,431 got it right.
Clue 3: "A SNARKY REMARKY THAT MAY TURN DARKY." Sarcasm is snarky; that’s what it is. 4,386 got it right.
Clue 2: "OH THIS CLUE IS GREAT." If you sensed the sarcasm in my voice, you got it! Over 3,000 got it right.
Clue 1: "A DIRECT MISDIRECT." You mean the opposite of what you say.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 9,638 guesses on clue one and 46 of you got it correct! Each of you is taking $217.39! Congratulations to Correct Price 226, We Floss 491, and T-Mac 719.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That is it for Best Guess Live! Congratulations to the winners. I'm hosting again tomorrow, and everyone gets a deal from Skechers! Good night!

clues: IT'S ALL FUN TILL YOU TRY TO RUN
HER SHOES THAT ARE FANCY BUT MAKE IT HARD TO DANCY
DOMINATES THE OLYMPICS BUT WON NO MEDALS
HIS NAME MEANS FETCHERS & HAS HIS OWN LINE OF SKECHERS

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Aha! Out! Leop beep out! Come on everybody going! Welcome to Best Guess Live! I’m your host Howie Mandel. Thank you! I’m so glad that I am hosting tonight more than any other night because we are doing that thing where everybody who submits an answer on the second puzzle—everybody who submits an answer, right or wrong—is going to get a great deal!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: I love saying that word, I've always loved saying "deal." And tonight the deal is from a company that I know very well and I love: Skechers! My Skechers! So if you are the kind of person who wears shoes, or you're thinking about starting to wear shoes, you definitely want to stick around for round two. Don't go anywhere! You want to slip into that game—see, I slipped in as I typed!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: But hey, you'd want to stick around for both rounds anyway because you know what we have today? $15,000 cash! Buy a lot of shoes for that. But first, I got to lay down the law by reading these rules. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be absolutely anything.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Then I’m going to give you five clues written like riddles. After each clue I’ll put 20 seconds on the clock for you to submit an answer. But you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you’re confident before you lock it in. It’s got to be the exact answer and we don’t accept typos. Everyone that locks in the correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let’s load that crystal ball. What could possibly be loaded into that ball? Here's clue number one: BALANCE BEAM TEAM. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: BALANCE BEAM TEAM. Start guessing, people! "Odd Todd 72" said pigeons—oh, like pigeons on a line? "Gooey Ley 210" said swing. I don't understand the answers, I understand the clue. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number two: ACHILLES GREETINGS. The clock starts now. Does pigeons still make sense? We already have almost 9,000 guesses. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: ACHILLES GREETINGS. "Goofy Picnic 385" said "Sup." Sup? Why? Explain to me how a greeting... okay, "Sup." I don't know why I'm not comprehending English today.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: RAISE THE STAKES. Clock starts now. Clue one: Balance Beam Team. Clue two: Achilles Greetings. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: RAISE THE STAKES. "Moody Caterpillar 436" said a traveling circus. Do caterpillars actually get moody? How do you tell if a caterpillar is moody? It just won't come out of its cocoon?

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: IT'S ALL FUN TILL YOU TRY TO RUN. Clock starts now. "Mighty Mac 59" said my knees. I get that! "Megie Mukau" said tightrope. "Nikki Lou" said stilts. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: HER SHOES THAT ARE FANCY BUT MAKE IT HARD TO DANCY. Clock starts now. Final chance! Clue one: Balance Beam Team. Two: Achilles Greetings. Three: Raise the Stakes. Four: Fun till you try to run. Time's up. Let's find out what is in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It's HIGH HEELS! No, I’m not going to put them on. I don’t wear anything but Skechers. These are so much less comfortable than Skechers.
Clue 5: "HER SHOES THAT ARE FANCY BUT MAKE IT HARD TO DANCY." Heels are often considered fancy, and they make it really hard to dance. 3,401 of you got it correct.
Clue 4: "IT'S ALL FUN TILL YOU TRY TO RUN." I really recommend being cautious when running in high heels. It doesn't look easy. 438 got it correct.
Clue 3: "RAISE THE STAKES." The heels are like stakes, and they raise you up! 755 got the right answer.
Clue 2: "ACHILLES GREETINGS." Achilles as in "Heel" and greetings as in "Hi." Put that together: High Heels. 650 got it correct.
Clue 1: "BALANCE BEAM TEAM." You have a team of two heels and you have to balance on them when you walk as if you are on a balance beam.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 8,636 guesses on that first clue and six of you guessed correctly! Each of you is getting $833.33! Randy, you want these back? These are Randy's shoes! Our best guessers were Olive Eagle 501, Gracie 612, and Former Realm 5.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: It's almost time for round two. Everyone who submits an answer on this puzzle—wrong, right, or misspelled—is going to get a voucher for 40% off one item at Skechers! Not only do they make amazing shoes, they also have incredible taste in spokespeople.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: If you're not sure how to collect your voucher, just hit the three lines in the top left corner of the app, push the "Win History" button, then click on the "Vouchers" tab. Click the ticket, scratch it, and that's it! 40% off Skechers.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: We also have a $10,000 prize on the line. Load that crystal ball. Clue number one: CURIOUS ORIGINS. Clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: CURIOUS ORIGINS. Put in anything just to get that 40% off! "Fluid Bricks" thinks George—Curious George. "P Ditterton" says Minecraft. "Vin Gonna Win" says a monkey. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue two: ICON OF WESTERN CIVILIZATION. Clock starts now. Over 8,000 guesses already. "Angry Butterfly 205" said Ronald McDonald. "Perfect Plague 300" said Cher. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number three: POETRY IN MOTION. Clock starts now. "Forever Freddy 333" said a '72 Pinto. "Frequent Host 317" said DJ. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue number four: DOMINATES THE OLYMPICS BUT WON NO MEDALS. Clock starts now. "Smarty Marty 87" said commercials. "King James YCS" said Muhammad Ali. Didn’t he win a medal? He lit the torch! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: HIS NAME MEANS FETCHERS AND HAS HIS OWN LINE OF SKECHERS. Clock starts now. You should have this! "ChiliFest 990" said "I don't know"—but you still get 40% off! Time's up. Let's find out what is in the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That's right: SNOOP DOGG! He's pretty cool.
Clue 5: "HIS NAME MEANS FETCHERS AND HAS HIS OWN LINE OF SKECHERS." Dogs fetch. That's his name and he has his own line of Skechers. 332 got it correct.
Clue 4: "DOMINATES THE OLYMPICS BUT WON NO MEDALS." Snoop was featured prominently in the Olympic coverage in 2024 and 2026. He was everywhere, but no medals for him. 178 got it right.
Clue 3: "POETRY IN MOTION." Snoop is one of the best rappers of all time—that is poetry. And he makes you move; it's in motion. 3 got it correct.
Clue 2: "ICON OF WESTERN CIVILIZATION." Snoop is an iconic figure from California, the West. Not to be confused with the East Coast vs. West Coast rap rivalry! 3 got it correct.
Clue 1: "CURIOUS ORIGINS." Snoop is curious, and his first single from his first album was "Who Am I (What's My Name)?"—the origin!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 8,021 guesses on that first clue and four of you said SNOOP DOGG! Each of you is splitting $2,500!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That is it for Best Guess Live! Thank you to Skechers for the great deal. Go scratch off that voucher! Tag me when you go to the store and I'll repost it. Good night everybody!

clues: TINTED EYEWEAR FOR THE DAY TO KEEP UV RAYS AT BAY
TEXTABLE ICONS THAT ARE ELOQUENT BEYOND WORDS

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Happy pap me up! Come on, we're going! Hello, welcome to Best Guess Live! I am your host Karen Fox. Unfortunately, Hunter and Howie had couples therapy today, so they cannot skip that, you guys! I am taking over, but it is a great night because tonight we are celebrating the Season 4 premiere of Love on the Spectrum!

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: I am obsessed with this show! Abby and David, Madison and Tyler—I am so invested. Before you go check out their new season, I want to give you a chance to fall in love right now with $15,000! And some of the Love on the Spectrum cast may just drop by to play along.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Here’s how it works: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item. Then I’ll give you five clues written like riddles. You get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. It has to be the exact answer with no typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue shares the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball. Here comes clue number one: SELECT TO PROTECT. 20 seconds are on the clock!

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: SELECT TO PROTECT. "Many Jenny's 284" said helmet—makes sense! "Compact Vacuum 16" said sword. James from Love on the Spectrum loves swords, so maybe that's James! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Clue number two: LIGHT LIGHT FIXTURE. 20 seconds starts now. "Uneven Feast 411" from Grantsville, Utah, said Tinder. Maybe different lighting going on there? I don't know! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Clue number three: COMPLETELY POLARIZING. 20 seconds starts now. "Hardy Smoothie 593" said polar bears. "Natural Vertex 756" from Denver said magnet.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Wait, we have an answer from Tyler and Madison from Love on the Spectrum! They say: "The answer has got to be the beach! It's where we had our first kiss!" Oh, baby! I could see that being polarizing—some people are mountain people.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Clue number four: SHADY THREE-POINT LANDING. 20 seconds starts now. "Nobody Smith 103" said bad gymnastics. "Polar Glowworm 507" said a plane. I hope they don't have a shady landing! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Final clue for $5,000: TINTED EYEWEAR FOR THE DAY TO KEEP UV RAYS AT BAY. 20 seconds starts now. This is your final chance! Time's up. Let’s find out what was in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: It's SUNGLASSES!
Clue 5: "TINTED EYEWEAR FOR THE DAY TO KEEP UV RAYS AT BAY." That's what they are! 3,980 got it right.
Clue 4: "SHADY THREE-POINT LANDING." They land on your nose and both ears—that's three different points! 934 got it right.
Clue 3: "COMPLETELY POLARIZING." They often have polarized lenses. 3,924 got it right.
Clue 2: "LIGHT LIGHT FIXTURE." They aren't heavy (light), and they are a fixture you put over your eyes because of the light. 286 got it right.
Clue 1: "SELECT TO PROTECT." You choose the style and they protect you from the sun.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: 76 people got it right on the first clue! They are splitting the pot for $65.78 each!

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Round two, the prize is $10,000! Load up the crystal ball. Clue number one: HIGHLY VALUABLE. 20 seconds starts now.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: HIGHLY VALUABLE. "Lofty Pebble 304" said cash money. "Mixed Up Marty 78" said first-class seats.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Tyler and Madison are back! Madison says: "I'm going to guess a Rose! My favorite princess is Belle, and it's romantic!" Gosh, she is so sweet. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Clue number two: GOES WITHOUT SAYING. 20 seconds starts now. "Scary Berry 294" said ghosting. "Navy Mini Map 778" said a ring—a ring speaks a thousand words! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Clue number three: WARNING GRAPHIC LANGUAGE IN USE. 20 seconds starts now. "Surly Kitten 454" says "My Aunt Sheila." She sounds fascinating! "CNC 13" from Cincinnati said a smile. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Clue number four: STATUS SYMBOL. 20 seconds starts now. "Mary Mary Mary 76" said "it's complicated." Is Aunt Sheila a status symbol? Time's up.

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: TEXTABLE ICONS THAT ARE ELOQUENT BEYOND WORDS. 20 seconds starts now. This is your last chance! Time is up. Let's find out what was in the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: It's EMOJI!
Clue 5: "TEXTABLE ICONS THAT ARE ELOQUENT BEYOND WORDS." Symbols you text to say something non-verbally. 4,416 got it right.
Clue 4: "STATUS SYMBOL." A way to communicate your status in the form of a symbol. 238 got it right.
Clue 3: "WARNING GRAPHIC LANGUAGE IN USE." They are a "graphic" way of communicating language. 121 got it right.
Clue 2: "GOES WITHOUT SAYING." The emojis do the talking for you. 1 person got it right!
Clue 1: "HIGHLY VALUABLE." Because a picture is worth a thousand words!

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: No one got it right on clue one, but one person got it on clue two! Timid Halo 566, you take home the full $10,000! That's a lot of sunglasses for you!

lines.speaker: Karen Fox
lines.text: That’s it for this episode! Go watch Love on the Spectrum. See you tomorrow! Good night, you guys!

clues: WHEN THEY'RE FUNNY IT COSTS MORE MONEY
FRAMED ON A CAR LETTERS AND NUMBERS TRACK WHO YOU ARE
TO PROTECT AND SERVE NUMBER ONE
THE EXTERIOR SUIT FOR THIS YELLOW BUNCH OF FRUIT

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Out! Aha! Ow! Me bah! Come on, we're going! Welcome to Best Guess Live! How's it going everybody? Happy Thursday—I’m your host Hunter March. Last night Timid Halo 566 was our only winner on the $10,000 puzzle. It’s pretty amazing, they are yet another example of the power of perseverance. Up until last night, they had played 134 rounds—that’s 67 episodes—and never had a win. Last night's $10,000 win was their first! Now they're the proud owners of a brand new 10K. Congratulations to them!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: For everyone else out there who hasn't won yet, you get to keep playing. Your day is coming. And if not—if you don't win tonight—you can always hit up Timid Halo for a loan! They've got it. Your next chance to win money is just moments away. $15,000 up for grabs tonight.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Before each round we’re going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I’ll give you five clues. But remember, the clues are written like riddles—they're meant to make you think, meant to get you going.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: After each clue you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer, like the exact specific thing. Tonight, like every other night, we're not accepting typos. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball with the first secret item. There it is guys! Here is clue number one: OFTEN TWO-FACED. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: OFTEN TWO-FACED. Good luck to everybody playing right now on this Thursday night! "Every Mer 255" said mirror—definitely often can be two-faced. "Angelic Patunia" from Masipeka, New York, said coin. "Two or Dare" put twins—very literal. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: PERMISSION TO DISH? 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Often two-faced and permission to dish. "Guitarist Zag" from Trustville, Alabama, put sandwich. "Grill Master 45" put buffet—those buffets are always so two-faced! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: WHEN THEY'RE FUNNY IT COSTS MORE MONEY. 20 seconds starts now. I think buffet is out—I've never been to a buffet where they say, "Knock knock, that’s going to cost you." "Jake W State" from Nashville put clown. "Vicious Turkey 398" put game show hosts. Am I funny? I hope so. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: STATE YOUR IDENTITY. 20 seconds starts now. Clue one: Often two-faced. Two: Permission to dish. Three: When they're funny it costs more money. I think somebody's got to be figuring it out by now. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: STATE YOUR IDENTITY. "MLA Chick" from Spencer, Massachusetts, said ID. State your identity, for sure. When your ID is funny it costs more money... you know that old DMV trick.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number five, your fifth and final clue: FRAMED ON A CAR LETTERS AND NUMBERS TRACK WHO YOU ARE. 20 seconds starts now. Final 20 seconds! I also want to say, how great was our guest host last night? Didn't she do an amazing job? Time is up. Let’s find out what's inside the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The answer was a LICENSE PLATE! And this one, I am very excited to put on my car. I don't think it's real and I will get arrested for false identity, but it'd be worth it.
Clue 5: "FRAMED ON A CAR LETTERS AND NUMBERS TRACK WHO YOU ARE." Well, that's a LICENSE PLATE. 4,009 got it right.
Clue 4: "STATE YOUR IDENTITY." License plates are customized to each US state. 58 got it right.
Clue 3: "WHEN THEY'RE FUNNY IT COSTS MORE MONEY." You can pay more if you want for a vanity plate, but you can make it funny if you'd like. 15 got it right.
Clue 2: "PERMISSION TO DISH?" Permission as in "license" and dish as in "plate." 3 people got it right.
Clue 1: "OFTEN TWO-FACED." It can be required on the front and back of your car—two-faced.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 9,636 people guessed on clue one and exactly one person got it right! That means one person is going home with $5,000! That person is Evasive Spiral 2!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round two, the prize doubles to $10,000! Load the crystal ball. This is it until tomorrow. Clue number one: MAJOR COVER UP. 20 seconds starts now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: MAJOR COVER UP. 30,000 people are playing right now for $10,000! "Harry Toes 438" said clothes. "Butterfly Kiss" from Port Orchard, Washington, said pickle. If that's the right answer, that's unreal. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number two: A TRUE OUTSIDER. 20 seconds starts now. Pickles are true outsiders—aggressive taste! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: A TRUE OUTSIDER. "Tony" from the Bronx said tent. "Gemin" put paint—you paint the outside of a house. "Curly Pencil 209" said Tom Cruise—he is from The Outsiders.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three: TO PROTECT AND SERVE NUMBER ONE. 20 seconds starts now. "Unholy Fennel" said cast—it protects and serves you. "HardyNat 546" put urinal cake... I can guarantee it'll never be that! Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number four: SLIPPERY WHEN DRY. 20 seconds starts now. Clue one: Major Cover Up. Two: True Outsider. Three: Protect and Serve Number One. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: SLIPPERY WHEN DRY. "Gusty Chestnut" from Utah said skin. "Poly Drip 02" put dress shoes.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Final clue for $10,000: THE EXTERIOR SUIT FOR THIS YELLOW BUNCH OF FRUIT. 20 seconds starts now. Final chance! I'll explain all the answers and find out who's going home with $10,000. Time is up. Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was a BANANA PEEL!

Clue 5: "THE EXTERIOR SUIT FOR THIS YELLOW BUNCH OF FRUIT." Well, that's a BANANA PEEL. 2,037 people got it right. 1,200 people just put "Banana," which is not the correct answer—it is specifically the peel.
Clue 4: "SLIPPERY WHEN DRY." You can slip on it even when the floor is dry. comically you can slip on it no matter what. 38 got it right.
Clue 3: "TO PROTECT AND SERVE NUMBER ONE." Bananas are the number one consumed fresh fruit in the US and banana peels protect them. 1 person got it right.
Clue 2: "A TRUE OUTSIDER." It's obviously on the outside of the fruit. 11 people got it right.
Clue 1: "MAJOR COVER UP." It covers up the part of the banana that you eat.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 10,446 people guessed on clue one and exactly one person got it right! That means one person is going home with $10,000 all to themselves! Congratulations to Current Nacho 84!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for this episode! Congratulations to both of our winners. We're here every weekday at 8 Eastern, 5 Pacific. See you tomorrow! Good night!

clues: YOUR REAL TEETH ARE GONE PUT FAKE CHOMPERS ON
WHEN IT'S NOT RIGHT YOU'RE UP ALL NIGHT
CUSHION ON YOUR BED WHERE YOU LAY YOUR HEAD

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Hey, welcome to Best Guess Live! I'm your host, Hunter March. As you can see around me, it is my birthday! Thank you, thank you, thank you. Apparently, I'm hosting my own surprise party.

The crew has informed me that the answers for tonight's puzzles are actually my presents, so I have not seen any of the clues. I have no idea what's going to be in the crystal ball. We're going to find out together. It is very exciting and, if I'm being honest, terrifying. I don't like surprises, but I love the idea that I get to celebrate with you right now.

Now, instead of singing the Happy Birthday song, I would appreciate it if all of you would just say the rules with me. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Each round has one puzzle. Before each round, we're going to load our crystal ball with a SECRET ITEM. It could be anything.

Then, I'll give you five clues, but remember: the clues are written like riddles. They're meant to make you think. After each clue, you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. Not one per clue—one per round. So, make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer, and we do not accept typos.

Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money. If you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you will win the entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right.

Okay, remember, if possible, make a video of you and your friends playing Best Guess Live. Do it for my birthday! If you love the show, share it with your friends. Let them know it's my birthday and it would mean a lot to me. I love you guys; I really appreciate you. With that being said, let's win some money!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round 1 is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball with the first SECRET ITEM. I have to be careful not to say any guesses early because I genuinely don't know what it is. All right, you guys ready? Here comes Clue Number 1: ALL FOR ONE.

20 seconds on the clock starts now. I know you guys are excited right now, thinking, "Oh, I'm going to get it! I can't wait to get it for Hunter's birthday." Think about how excited I am; I'm about to get two presents! This is really awesome for me. I hope someone guesses a million dollars.
