Keep in mind, these are supposedly what I'm getting for my birthday. Justine from Los Angeles put candle—I hope it's not just a candle. Miss Jean put Superman; I don't know how that would be my gift, but I like it. Shiny Dirt said musketeers. Hey, ALL FOR ONE, those are good guesses so far.

Let's go to Clue Number 2: FILL IN THE BLANK. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Oh, that's what I'm trying to do right now. ALL FOR ONE, FILL IN THE BLANK. This is what you guys feel like every night? This is hard! I'm with you now. Oh boy, 5 seconds left.

Time's up. Wispy Dallas 217 said puzzle. It would be a good gift. Desi D123 said golf. FILL IN THE BLANK—maybe fill in that hole? The golf hole? Forever Dan 234 put funeral. That might even be worse than a puzzle.

Let's go to Clue Number 3: TEMPORARY STRUCTURE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I was just wondering what that could be for my birthday. TEMPORARY STRUCTURE. ALL FOR ONE, FILL IN THE BLANK, TEMPORARY STRUCTURE. By the way, for everyone playing on Reddit, Discord, or sharing it with your friends via text message, hello to you specifically! Thanks for joining me on my birthday.

Time's up. Minute Yam 569 said cake. That is a TEMPORARY STRUCTURE. FILL IN THE BLANK... maybe ALL FOR ONE? Yeah, it's a birthday. Ella Fantastic said Legos; that's a great TEMPORARY STRUCTURE. Mediocre Pigeon said high school. I guess you're there temporarily, but if high school is the present, I'm going to be really bummed.

Let's go to Clue Number 4: AN AMAZING IMPRESSION OF IMPRESSIONS. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I'm playing with you. Let me look at all the clues: ALL FOR ONE, FILL IN THE BLANK, TEMPORARY STRUCTURE, AN AMAZING IMPRESSION OF IMPRESSIONS. I see some guesses coming in already that are very fun.

Time is up. Busy Vertex 428 from Willis, Texas, said "Mime." They do some good body work, but I don't think they talk, so impressions would be hard. Mostly Greg said "Hunter's Australian accent." Oh, I know!

All right, let's move on to Clue Number 5, your fifth and final clue: YOUR REAL TEETH ARE GONE, PUT FAKE CHOMPERS ON. 20 seconds starts now. I don't know why I'm with everyone else that thought a 1967 Ford Mustang Coupe was going to be in that little box behind me. That's on me. I think you guys know what the answer is. Listen, I'm going to be grateful regardless. A gift's a gift.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That was your fifth and final clue. Let's find out together what was inside the crystal ball. The correct answer was... DENTURES.
Happy Birthday! Wow. Now I get why our executive producer didn't want to talk before the show. Yes, the answer was DENTURES. I can't wait to take those home. Thank you guys so much.

Let's take a look at how the clues fit and find out who's taking home the money. Clue Number 5: YOUR REAL TEETH ARE GONE, PUT FAKE CHOMPERS ON. 6,616 guesses; 5,707 got DENTURES. Fake chompers are DENTURES.

Clue Number 4: AN AMAZING IMPRESSION OF IMPRESSIONS. They do an impression of your real teeth, and then you take those impressions of your teeth and you make DENTURES. 2,917 guesses; 118 got it right. I hadn't figured it out by then, so I'm very impressed.
I definitely had not figured it out by Clue 3, which is TEMPORARY STRUCTURE. Well, they go in and out of your mouth. Temporarily in, temporarily out. 5,436 people guessed; 8 people got it right. Wow!

Clue Number 2: FILL IN THE BLANK. Well, when you are missing teeth, they take the place of them. Also, your mouth kind of looks like a blank when you don't have DENTURES or teeth. 5,746 guesses—a lot of guesses. How many people got it right? Absolutely nobody.

Which means for my birthday, those 8 people could be going home with a split of $5,000. Or someone could have possibly gotten it on Clue Number 1: ALL FOR ONE. All of the teeth are in one set for one mouth. Out of 7,379 guesses, zero people got it right.
This means 8 people on Clue Number 3 are each going home with $625! Congratulations to those 8 people. Enjoy it!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That was only $5,000. I say for my birthday alone, let's double it. Let's go to $10,000! Call me crazy; this is for you guys. I don't care if they take it out of my paycheck.

You know how the show works. Let us load the crystal ball. I think we've all collectively learned the lesson that they might not be getting me exactly what I asked for, but who knows? Let's get right into it. Clue Number 1: REST IN PEACE.
20 seconds starts now. There's no way this is going to be a Mustang. All right, REST IN PEACE. Good luck, everybody. $10,000 on the line.

Time's up. Briana Cherry said "age." I guess eventually you age enough that you do REST IN PEACE. Showy Rodeo 711 said "iPod" because it's a dead electronic. That's good. Noisynnap 676 said "your ears rest." I thought you were talking about my ears specifically on my birthday!

Clue Number 2: REPUTATION FOR LOSING ITS COOL. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. I feel like I should be able to figure this out. I'm going to try right now with you guys. REST IN PEACE, REPUTATION FOR LOSING ITS COOL.

Time's up. Fez Aztecs said "sauna." It definitely loses its cool and becomes very hot. That would be an awesome gift; I would take that for sure. Sean Made That 232 said "Smashmouth." I truly hope that is not the gift.

Clue Number 3: NOT A SERIOUS FIGHTER. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. REST IN PEACE, REPUTATION FOR LOSING ITS COOL, NOT A SERIOUS FIGHTER. Hunter, please stop; let me think and focus. Could you imagine if I get the gift and $10,000 because I figured it out?

Time's up. Grassy Ghost 97 said "cat." Cats just kind of "aw" you. It's the DJ said "sleep." Definitely NOT A SERIOUS FIGHTER, I guess. Nobody Nate is continuing with this wave of 2000s fans: "Sugar Ray." Sugar Ray Leonard? I thought Matchbox Twenty was going to be next.

Let's go to Clue Number 4: WHEN IT'S NOT RIGHT, YOU'RE UP ALL NIGHT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Interesting. Hold on... okay, hold on. I might know what it is! Let me write it on my hand just to prove that I knew.

Time's up. I think I know. Original Cakes said "mood." That is very true; when your mood's NOT RIGHT, you can be UP ALL NIGHT tossing. Brendy Bendy Barbara 243 said "conscience." When you don't have a good conscience, it's kind of the same as mood.

Clue Number 5, our fifth and final clue: CUSHION ON YOUR BED WHERE YOU LAY YOUR HEAD. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. It's not what I thought. It's not what I thought when I found out we were doing a presents episode, and it's not what I thought after reading the first four clues. This game is hard, but you guys have been doing an incredible job.

There's a lot of money to be won every single night. In fact, the two people that won last night each had played over 100 games and had never won before. They won $5,000 and $10,000, so congrats to them. Maybe that could be you tonight.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's find out what's inside the crystal ball. The correct answer was... not just any PILLOW! Yes! Turn it around! In the monitor, I thought that was me for a second. Oh wow, this is very cool.
The last clue, CUSHION ON YOUR BED WHERE YOU LAY YOUR HEAD—this is Howie's PILLOW! If he was playing tonight, he would have nailed it. Now, the question is how many of you guys got it right?

Clue Number 5 was a PILLOW. If you didn't get that, I'm sorry. 4,293 guesses; 4,092 got it right. Clearly, we were all onto something. But how many people got it on Clue 4 when I thought I had something? By the way, I thought the answer was "tea." Caffeine versus herbal—I would have lost. 5,401 guesses; 2,018 got it right. If you don't have the RIGHT PILLOW, you're going to be UP ALL NIGHT.
Clue Number 3: NOT A SERIOUS FIGHTER. In fact, a PILLOW fight is usually the least serious of the fights and the most fun. 3,374 guesses; 545 people got it right.
REPUTATION FOR LOSING ITS COOL. A PILLOW usually starts cool, then gets warm, and you're flipping it to the cool side. 8,125 guesses; 1,863 got it right.

Did anyone get it right on Clue 1: REST IN PEACE? Well, it helps you get a good night's rest. That is the whole point of a PILLOW is to help you rest in peace. 8,029 guesses. 603 people got it right for my birthday! They're going home with $16.58 each!

Congratulations to those winners. That is it for my birthday edition of Best Guess Live! Thank you so much for celebrating with me. We're here every weekday at 8:00 Eastern, 5:00 Pacific. Come back Monday, and don't forget to bring your best guess. Good night, everybody! Give it up for... is there new music?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Chris, you want to come up here? Come say hi to everybody. Nobody knows this, but we have a... oh, my sweet boy! Yes, my dog! This is Grizzle. He's famous now. I love you, buddy. Good boy. Happy birthday, Grizz!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! Hello, how are we doing? I'm your host, Hunter March, and I have a very exciting announcement today. Like, so exciting, but I'm not supposed to tell you what it is until after round one. They're stopping me, but I'll get it out. Just our way of building a little bit of suspense. We're like the Alfred Hitchcock of handheld live mobile game shows. A lot of Hitchcock fans watching our content right now.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: And if that big announcement is not enough to keep you intrigued, how about, I don't know, $15,000? Before I do that, I just want to say thanks again for all my birthday wishes from our Best Guess fans. It was my birthday Sunday. We celebrated on Friday with you guys, and a lot of you sent me really nice messages. Thank you so much. In return, I'm sending you guys all a bunch of good luck. I hope you all win.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's get into the rules. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Each round has one puzzle. Before each round, we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item. Now, it could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues, but remember, the clues are written like riddles. They're meant to make you think. After each clue, you'll get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. Not one per clue, one per round, so make sure you're confident before you lock in. It has to be the exact answer, and we don't accept typos.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will share in the money, and if you're the only one to get it right on the earliest clue, you will win that entire prize pot for that round. Bottom line, you want to be fast, but you have to be right. Right now, we got over 24,000 people filing in. Thank you guys for joining. If possible, make a video of you guys or your friends or whoever around you playing Best Guess Live. If you love the show and you just think, "Hey, I want to play with some more friends and send each other how well we did," send it to your friends. Send in the link, play the game with them. #BestGuessLive, find it in the app store. Thanks again. With that being said, let's win some money.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Round one is worth $5,000. Let's load the crystal ball with the first secret item. Okay, oh my god, I feel good today, you guys. I feel very good. What could it be? We'll find out together. Here is your first clue: HOLD IT TOGETHER. Okay, 20 seconds on the clock starts now. HOLD IT TOGETHER.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Okay, 15 seconds remaining. For those of you just joining us tonight, welcome live. We're excited to see you. For those of you returning, which I know a lot of you are because we have people who have now played over a hundred games, tonight could be your night. I'm rooting for you. Time's up. Clue number one, HOLD IT TOGETHER. Wow, over 6,000 guesses on clue number one. Obsidian Monk 134 said glue. I was thinking the same thing. Acidic Stall 392 from Raleigh, North Carolina said, "Not hold it together." All right, could be a good one, I don't know. Little Miss Poodle 254 said girdle. I know we did bobbing for apples once, but not everything is going to be catered to the 50s.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Okay, clue number two: SOUNDS A LITTLE COLD BUT ISN'T TILL IT'S OLD. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Okay, both clues: HOLD IT TOGETHER, SOUNDS A LITTLE COLD BUT ISN'T TILL IT'S OLD. Might be one of the longest clue twos we've ever given you guys, so I'm rooting for you. 7 seconds left. Three, two, one. Clue number two, SOUNDS A LITTLE COLD BUT ISN'T TILL IT'S OLD.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Harty Shackle 767 said, "Igloo. Sounds a little cold but isn't until that's actually a great thing. Igloos are built to be insulating, and then when they get old, they probably lose a little bit of that insulation. That's great." And then Dancer Diane 87 put Ice Cube the rapper. SOUNDS A LITTLE COLD BUT ISN'T TILL IT'S OLD. Oh man, how old does Ice Cube have to get to go cold?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: All right, here's clue number three: TUCK AND ROLL. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Again, if you know the answer, type it in. If you don't know the answer, that's okay. There's two more clues after this one. This is the third of five, and if you can't figure it out at all or you're too late on this one by any chance, well, there's always another puzzle, so stay tuned. Get ready. 3 seconds. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue number three, TUCK AND ROLL. Trippy Tony 222 from Nashville, Tennessee said sushi. Definitely a TUCK AND ROLL. SOUNDS A LITTLE COLD BUT ISN'T TILL IT'S OLD. Maybe it does HOLD IT TOGETHER though, that's great. Fabulous Stone 493 from California said plastic surgery. There's a very California answer.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Okay, let's move on to clue number four, your second to last clue: MEXICAN RAP STAR. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. MEXICAN RAP STAR. Rap, as you guys can see on your screen, spelled with a W. I'm not sure what that means. Not sure if that could mean anything. Who knows, maybe it's a typo. Most likely not. HOLD IT TOGETHER, SOUNDS A LITTLE COLD BUT ISN'T TILL IT'S OLD, TUCK AND ROLL, MEXICAN RAP STAR. Time's up.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: MEXICAN RAP STAR is clue number four. Honorable Frog 109 said poncho. Is that a rap star? Oh, because you wrap it around your body. Poncho, you wrap it around your body and you tuck it, and then maybe you roll it and you turn it into a nice pillow at the end. Yeah, it's a great guess for those two clues, and those two clues alone, that works.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Here's your fifth and final clue. I think people are starting to figure it out: BIG TORTILLA AROUND MEAT BEANS AND CHEESE YES PLEASE. That feels like the explanation of the clue, but that is in fact the clue. So good luck everybody. Clue number five, 20 seconds, go. BIG TORTILLA AROUND MEAT BEANS AND CHEESE YES PLEASE. I do want to say, if you cannot figure this one out, you might be too smart for all of us. You might be thinking of so many different things. Simplify, baby. You got it. Okay, BIG TORTILLA AROUND MEAT BEANS AND CHEESE. Last place, I think a lot of people got this one right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's find out what was inside the crystal ball. The correct answer was a BURRITO. BURRITO! Okay, the correct answer is BURRITO. The question is, who got it right on what clue? Who's going home with potentially $5,000? Let's look at clue number five: BIG TORTILLA AROUND MEAT BEANS AND CHEESE YES PLEASE. It makes a lot of sense now. Now I get it. Clearly the ingredients for a BURRITO. 4,315 guesses, 3,062 got it right. It's a lot of people. lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Hey, welcome to Best Guess Live! I'm your host, Howie Mandel. It is Thursday! Thursday! A lot of people think that Thursday was named after the Norse god Thor, but it is a little-known fact that it's actually named after Thurston Guess, a 15th-century scientist who invented Best Guess Live in his Munich laboratory in 1483, people!

You want to know the funny part? He wasn't even trying to invent Best Guess Live. He was working on a revolutionary new goose aspirin and accidentally created this show. The rest is history! But of course, you learned all of this in your third-grade history class, so you know that, but I just found out today. Let's honor Thurston's legacy by playing tonight for... what should we play for? $15,000!

Here is how it works. Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Each round has one puzzle. Each puzzle has three sets of winners: Gold, Silver, and Bronze. Before each round, we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item; it could be anything. Then I'm going to give you five puzzles, but remember, the puzzles—they're like riddles. They're meant to make you think.

After each puzzle, you get 20 seconds on the clock to submit an answer. But you only get one best guess per round—not per puzzle, per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It's got to be the exact answer. We don't accept typos. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you've got to be right. Everyone that locks in the correct answer on the earliest puzzle will split the Gold prize, which is $3,000! And everyone that locks it in on the second earliest puzzle will get the Silver pot, $2,500. And everyone that gets it on the third earliest puzzle will split the Bronze pot, $2,000.

So if you give a correct answer on one of the three earliest puzzles, you win. And if you're the only one to give a correct answer on one of those puzzles, you take that whole pot for that medal. So don't forget, if possible, this is what I need you to do—and some people did it last night and I reposted you—you take a video of you and your family or your friends playing Best Guess Live and send it to us or post it or collab with me on your socials with #BestGuessLive. I feel like I have so much power when I say that and then I see this. And now we're getting ready to give you your first clue: SUPPLIES ARE LIMITED. And the clock starts now.

SUPPLIES ARE LIMITED. What could it be? I guess that's the game, right? I just, "What could it be?" True Rat King said a penny. You know what? I think they're stopping; they're not going to make any more pennies. Is that true? Did I just make that up? Wow, that's right. I forgot about that. And Amy To Please said polar bears. I heard they're not going to make any more polar bears, right? That's what I thought. Penny for my thoughts.

Here is clue number two: WHAT'S MINE IS YOURS, KINDA. And the clock starts now. So clue number one was SUPPLIES ARE LIMITED. Clue number two is WHAT'S MINE IS YOURS, KINDA. Ten seconds left on that clock. The guesses are flying in. On the first clue, we had over 3,500 guesses, and now I can see you guys pouring in with all your guesses. Here is Cloudy Scroll... Cloudy Scroll said a prenup. WHAT'S MINE IS YOURS, KINDA, unless I leave you and then it's not yours anymore because I have...

Here's your third clue. TAKE NOTE OF THESE THREE LETTERS, and the clock starts now. Clue number one was SUPPLIES ARE LIMITED. Clue number two is WHAT'S MINE IS YOURS, KINDA. And clue number three, TAKE NOTE OF THESE THREE LETTERS. Wow! We have almost 10,000 guesses on the second round. Remember, put in a guess! Put in a guess! Because if it's correct, even if you don't win money, you're going to win a voucher, people. New Lobster 249 said DNA. Ken B P 18 said FYI. For my information? No, I knew that. I knew that. It was just... I was trying to think. I wasn't thinking what it meant, I was thinking how does that fit? Take note, what's mine is yours, for your information, and SUPPLIES ARE LIMITED. There's a limited amount of information. Apparently, you think I have none of it. You're filling me.

Okay, here is your fourth clue. All right, your fourth clue is PUT A PIN IN IT. And the clock starts now. Okay, the first clue was SUPPLIES ARE LIMITED. The second clue was WHAT'S MINE IS YOURS, KINDA. The third clue is TAKE NOTE OF THESE THREE LETTERS. And then clue four is PUT A PIN IN IT. Mm. What could it be? Three seconds left on the clock, over almost 21,000 submissions. Answers. Angry Squirrel Four. I've seen your name before. You've been playing a while. It says a donkey, but if you put the tail on the donkey... TAKE NOTE OF THESE THREE LETTERS. Well, there's another word for a donkey and it starts with an A! Yeah! WHAT'S MINE IS YOURS? Don't get that part, but SUPPLIES ARE LIMITED. You can't get a good donkey these days!

Here's your final clue. Final chance to guess. Clue number five: ALWAYS ABLE TO KEEP YOUR BALANCE. And the clock starts now. Okay, clue number one: SUPPLIES ARE LIMITED. Clue number two: WHAT'S MINE IS YOURS, KINDA. Clue number three is TAKE NOTE OF THESE THREE LETTERS. Clue number four is PUT A PIN IN IT, and clue number five: ALWAYS ABLE TO KEEP YOUR BALANCE. Three seconds left on the clock. We have 27,000 subscribers, answers, time is up.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Should we open the crystal ball? Then say, "Open the crystal ball." Say it. Do what they said. It is an ATM! Yeah! It is an ATM. All right, that's the answer. Let's take a look at how the clues fit and find out who is taking the money. We'll start with clue number five.

Clue number five, ALWAYS ABLE TO KEEP YOUR BALANCE. It keeps track of your account balance. You go to the ATM, you take your money, you look at your balance. It keeps your balance. We have 6,479 guesses. 1,493 of you gave the correct answer. Yes! Okay! This is good!

Clue number four makes sense: PUT A PIN IN IT. How do you get your money? What do you do? You put your PIN in it. You access the ATM by entering your PIN. 5,266 guesses, 472 of you got it correct. Okay.

Okay, here is clue number three. TAKE NOTE OF THESE THREE LETTERS. You literally take bank notes out of your what? A T M! Three letters. See? And 1,900 guesses. That one had the most guesses. 170 of those guesses were correct. That's the new gold standard.

Okay, clue number two: WHAT'S MINE IS YOURS, KINDA. So, the ATM is filled with the bank's money, but it becomes yours when you take it out. So it's kinda yours, just the stuff that comes out, but it's the ATM's. Okay? 5,934 guesses. One correct answer. What do you mean, one correct answer? Somebody here is not listening.

Okay, that's... and now we're down to the first clue. SUPPLIES ARE LIMITED. Okay, most ATMs have a daily limit on what they have in there, plus your supply of what you have in the bank is limited. I know mine is. 3,556 guesses. Did anybody go for the gold? Did anybody get the gold? Yes, one person got the gold. So one person is taking the three thousand dollars, and that best guesser is Petite Mower 675. Congratulations, Petite Mower. You know what's interesting too? Listen to this: Silver, silver went to one person for twenty-five hundred dollars. It's a lot of money. And the bronze went to one hundred and seventy people who are getting eleven dollars and seventy-six cents each. That's a good day.

This was a good day, and a lot of you are going to get that voucher. But if you didn't, remember we got a couple more days that you can go for. So that is it for this episode of Best Guess Live. Congratulations again to all of our winners. We are here every weekday at eight Eastern, five Pacific, so come back tomorrow and don't forget to bring your best guess. Good night everyone.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Welcome to Best Guess Live! How's it going, everybody? I'm your host, Hunter March. Now, one of the things we love about the new prize system is that there are more winners, but I want to talk about one person who took home some serious cash last night. Previously, this person has played 60 puzzles, and in those 60 puzzles, they did win a whopping about 20 bucks. It's pretty good, but last night, Petite Mower 675 won $3,000!

That is a lot of green, and I bring all that up to say it could be you tonight. You might have played 20 puzzles; this could be your first; you could have been here for nearly a hundred. I hope tonight is your night. In fact, it is day four of Fan Appreciation Week, which means even more ways to win. So unless you don't like easy money, stick around for our second puzzle because on that puzzle, along with our regular medal prizes, you could still win a $5 voucher to the Netflix Shop if you haven't already.

Now, with that being said, let's pass out some cash. Here are the rules: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds, one puzzle each. Each puzzle has three sets of winners: Gold, Silver, and Bronze. Before each round, we're going to load our crystal ball with a SECRET ITEM. It could be anything. Then I'll give you five clues, but remember, the clues are written like riddles meant to make you think and keep you on your feet.

After each clue, you get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round. Not one per clue—one per round. So make sure you're confident before you lock it in. It has to be the exact answer, and we don't accept typos. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you have to be right.

Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will split the Gold prize pot of $3,000. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the second earliest clue will split the Silver pot of $2,500. And everyone that gets it on the third earliest clue will split Bronze, which is $2,000. So if you give a correct answer on one of the three earliest clues, you win. And if you're the only one to give a correct answer on one of those clues, you take the whole pot for that medal!
Enough talk, let's win some money! Round 1 is worth $7,500. Let us load the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: All right, creeping up on nearly 30,000 players on this lovely Thursday evening. Crystal ball loaded. Clue number one: 2 FOR 1. 20 seconds on the clock starts now.
Time's up. Clue number one: 2 FOR 1. IGAZV38 said "twins." Checkered Cup said "tandem bike." Definitely a 2 FOR 1.

Let's go to clue number two: A FULFILLING PRACTICE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Now, I've never ridden on a tandem bike, but I cannot imagine it being fulfilling. I cannot imagine it really being anything but awkward. Time's up. Golden Ray 90 says "pregnancy." Two Toads said "therapy."

Let's move on to clue three: DESPITE THE NAME, THEY'RE NOT FOR CUTTING. 20 seconds starts now. Listen, sometimes I'll say something, but go with what's on screen. Five seconds left. Time's up. Wireless Coffee said "dice."
Let's go to clue number four: INCREDIBLE PICK-UP ARTISTS. 20 seconds starts now. Best of luck. 3, 2, 1. All right, INCREDIBLE PICK-UP ARTISTS. Swift Lantern said "stork." That's a good guess.

Let's go to clue five. This is your fifth and final clue on puzzle number one: ASIAN UTENSILS YOU HOLD LIKE TWO PENCILS. 20 seconds starts now. Not only am I rooting for you, I'm a little concerned if you don't get it. Ten seconds left. Good luck everybody. Five seconds. Time is up! Cheeky Tomb 87 said "chipsticks." That hurts.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Let's find out what's inside the crystal ball. The correct answer was CHOPSTICKS!
Clue number five, ASIAN UTENSILS YOU HOLD LIKE TWO PENCILS: That's what they are and that's how you use them. 12,410 people guessed and 11,891 people said CHOPSTICKS.

Clue number four, INCREDIBLE PICK-UP ARTISTS: Their whole purpose is to pick up your food, and if you use a CHOPSTICK the right way, they pick up everyone. 5,512 guesses and 1,667 people got it right, meaning Gold now moves to clue number four.
Clue number three was DESPITE THE NAME, THEY'RE NOT FOR CUTTING. Don't try to chop anything with CHOPSTICKS; it will take forever. 3,571 guesses and 265 got it right.

Clue number two was A FULFILLING PRACTICE. CHOPSTICK expertise is actually very fulfilling if you get good at it, but also it's for filling your tummy; it's for fully filling your tummy. 4,097 guesses and 42 people got it right.

Clue number one: 2 FOR 1. Two sticks, one unit. 4,811 guesses. Exactly 24 people got it right, which means those 24 people are now in Gold and winning $125 each! Silver had 42 people winning nearly $60, and Bronze had 265 people winning $7.54. Congratulations to our Gold winners Need Tuition Money, Codious, and Screechy Meow.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Before we get to the next round, Running Point season 2 is out today on Netflix. Speaking of love, we are celebrating you, our amazing players, all week! Everyone who submits a correct answer on this next puzzle will win a $5 voucher for merch from the Netflix Shop. Each player can only win one voucher per week, so this is one of the last chances to get yours.

Round 2, $7,500 up for grabs. Let's load the crystal ball. Clue number one: INVISIBLE YET VISIBLE. 20 seconds starts now. I'm just letting it sit with James for a second; he almost had a moment. Time's up. Rosski Boboski put "age." Pulpy Naive 178 said "tape." Slippery Yacht 21 said "Botox."

Clue number two: NO MATTER WHERE, IT'S ALWAYS THERE. 20 seconds on the clock. Botox might be out unless we switched it to no matter where in Los Angeles it's always there. Four seconds left. Time is up. Zoto Dog said "air." Kim Possible 330 said "guilt." I think you should talk to someone.

Let's move on to clue number three: ONCE YOU'RE THERE, IT CHANGES. 20 seconds starts now. Honestly, Botox—you think you have enough, but then your face is still moving. Time's up. Fancy Block 2323 said "family reunion." Whole vibe changes.
Clue number four: NOT ACTUALLY MONEY BUT EVERYONE SPENDS IT. 20 seconds starts now. Time is up. Anti-courts 339 said "credit." Powerful Banana said "the night."

Let's go to clue number five, your fifth and final clue. Clue number five: A CLOCK CAN STOP BUT I NEVER DO. 20 seconds starts now. We're living in like a meta version of this puzzle right now. Time's up! Let's find out what's inside the crystal ball.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The correct answer was TIME!
Clue number five, A CLOCK CAN STOP BUT I NEVER DO: Of course, that's what TIME does; it just doesn't stop even though clocks and watches break down. 2,846 guesses and 2,041 got it right.
Clue number four, NOT ACTUALLY MONEY BUT EVERYONE SPENDS IT: Well, that's from the famous phrase "TIME is money." Everyone spends it—spends TIME. 5,491 guesses and 4,361 people got it right.
Clue number three, ONCE YOU'RE THERE, IT CHANGES: As soon as you arrive at any given moment, it's already over. 5,073 guesses and 676 people got it right.
Clue number two, NO MATTER WHERE, IT'S ALWAYS THERE: Wherever you go, literally anywhere, TIME is always passing. 10,018 guesses and only 260 got it right.

Clue number one, INVISIBLE YET VISIBLE: You can't see TIME, but you can see its effects. 8,442 people guessed and exactly 24 people got it right! Those 24 people are splitting Gold for $125 each. Congratulations to our Gold winners Daniela's Western, Leo89, Bass Dude, and Miss World 594. See you tomorrow!

Puzzle 2: AIR, SHADOW, WIND, OXYGEN, WATER

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Welcome to the 100th episode of Best Guess Live! I'm your host, Howie Mandel. Oh, my bad, I didn't mean to... after you. I have a special guest with me tonight: the incredible Hunter March! This is crazy. Go ahead, Hunter, you do it.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Oh man, I'm excited. We're both here to celebrate our 100th episode. We actually, as you guys probably can see, haven't hosted together since the very first episode, and a lot has happened since then.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Yeah, it has been an exciting ride. Let's take a moment to reflect on everything we've been through for the first 99 episodes. And by reflect, I mean roll a montage!
(A montage plays showing past highlights and items like prenups, lemonade, and guest Anthony Anderson.)

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Yes, it's been a fantastic 99 episodes. Our big boss here, Jeff Apploff, just came with his calculator and said if you want to tell anybody, 100 episodes is 200 puzzles. We would not be able to do the show without him.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Yeah, we are just getting started. Let's get to tonight's game. It's $15,000 up for grabs. And now, for your viewing and listening pleasure, we present our hosting duet known as "The Rules."

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Each episode of Best Guess Live has two rounds. Each round has one puzzle. Each puzzle has three sets of winners: Gold, Silver, and Bronze.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Now, before each round, we're going to load our crystal ball with a secret item. It could be absolutely anything. Then we'll give you five clues, but remember the clues are written like riddles; they're meant to make you think.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: After each clue, you're going to get 20 seconds to submit an answer, but you only get one best guess per round—not one per clue, one per round. So make sure that you're confident before you lock it in. It's got to be the exact answer, and we don't accept typos. Bottom line: you want to be fast, but you've got to be right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the earliest clue will split the Gold prize pot of $3,000. Everyone that locks in a correct answer on the second earliest clue will split the Silver pot of $2,500. Then everyone that gets it right on the third earliest clue will split the prize pot for Bronze of $2,000.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: So, if you give a correct answer on one of the three earliest clues, you win. And if you are the only one to give a correct answer on one of those clues, you take the whole pot for that medal. Here we go, baby! Let's do it!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: And don't forget, if possible, take a video of you or your friends or your family playing Best Guess Live and send it to us or post it to your socials with the hashtag #BestGuessLive. Enough talk, let's win some money, Howie! Round 1 for $7,500—load that crystal ball now!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Wow, this is fun. This is very exciting. We should do this more often. Yeah, how many times can we do the 100th episode? We should do it more often. We really should. Okay, here's your first clue. Do you want to do it or should I?

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: I think you should do it.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Okay. Clue 1: CURRENT EVENTS EXPERT. Start the clock! 20 seconds, go. Now, while you guys are thinking, I just want to let you know they did put a line of tape about three feet ahead of us specifically to tell Howie not to move forward for this episode because his head will be out of the frame. And not only that, we actually took the tape away, so now I don't know where to go.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Okay, here's your mark. While he handles the tape, I want to shout out Baggy Trumpet, who wrote "TMZ." Definitely a CURRENT EVENTS EXPERT. 56 Media said "CNN," another CURRENT EVENTS EXPERT. Your time is up!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: So here is your second clue: THE ORIGINAL PHILADELPHIA FLYER. 20 seconds, go! Do I just take the tape? Do I leave the tape while they're guessing? This is distracting. What do I do? This is like leaving the men's room with toilet paper on. How do I know how far to go now? This is a production version of the toilet paper.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Three seconds left. Electric Painting 22 said "Gritty." Yeah, THE ORIGINAL PHILADELPHIA FLYER... Gritty is the mascot for the Flyers. I didn't know that; I thought they were talking about just me with tape on the bottom of my shoe. Dinkleberg put where my head was at: "Cream Cheese." Philadelphia Cream Cheese. I get it. Here is your third clue.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue 3: PUT HIS JOHN HANCOCK NEXT TO JOHN HANCOCK. The clock starts now. Already almost 11,000 guesses. There's... I'm so distracted. Use my back as leverage. Okay, thanks. All right, there we go. See, this is why we have to do two episodes together; we support each other. Look where I put the tape!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Time is up. Some good guesses. Nick Cow said "Rooster." Saucy Glove 31 said "Nicolas Cage." Give them the fourth clue.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The fourth clue: FIGURE OF NOTE ON A C-NOTE. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. Wow, already 16,000 submitted answers. So the first clue was CURRENT EVENTS EXPERT. Second clue was THE ORIGINAL PHILADELPHIA FLYER. Third clue was PUT HIS JOHN HANCOCK NEXT TO JOHN HANCOCK. And the fourth clue: FIGURE OF NOTE ON A C-NOTE.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Three seconds left. Now we're coming up on the final clue. Look at some of the guesses, though. Neon Racer 240 said "Signature," yeah, very specific to Clue 3. Curious Soda 7 said "Mozart," famously a figure of note on a C-note. I guess an actual note. Oh, I didn't even get that. Here is your final clue. This is your final chance to guess in this first round.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue 5: FOUNDING DAD WHOSE INVENTIONS WERE RAD. Clock starts now. FOUNDING DAD WHOSE INVENTIONS WERE RAD. Let's go over all the clues. Clue 1: CURRENT EVENTS EXPERT. Then we had THE ORIGINAL PHILADELPHIA FLYER, PUT HIS JOHN HANCOCK NEXT TO JOHN HANCOCK, FIGURE OF NOTE ON A C-NOTE, and FOUNDING DAD WHOSE INVENTIONS WERE RAD.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: I feel bad; I can't leave this here. I had that on your... I just felt guilty. See what I did there? Unbelievable. All right, time is up. All the guesses are in. You want to find out?

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: I want to go home now. Now I realize I'm the butt of the joke. Let's find out what's inside the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: There it is! The answer was BENJAMIN FRANKLIN!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: They said they were going to give me like a $100 bill... I was supposed to get a $100 bill and I was going to show it. I think they saw you do the tape thing and they want to give it to me now. They feel bad. What did you do with the $100 bill? You got cake! They got cake!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Regardless, let's take a look at how the clues fit and find out who's taking home the money. Clue 5: FOUNDING DAD WHOSE INVENTIONS WERE RAD. He's a Founding Father and was famous for his many inventions. He didn't invent electricity; I think it has existed since the beginning of time, but he found it to fly the kite. 4,399 guesses; 2,525 of you were correct!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue 4: FIGURE OF NOTE ON A C-NOTE. A C-note is a $100 bill, and he's on it. That's why you were going to hand it to me—because I was going to go "BENJAMIN FRANKLIN!" That's what we rehearsed. 4,984 guesses; 2,579 got it right. Gold position has moved down.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Clue 3: PUT HIS JOHN HANCOCK NEXT TO JOHN HANCOCK. He signed the Declaration of Independence along with John Hancock. He did that. I'm learning my American history—you know, I'm from Canada. This show is educational, not only fun. 6,931 guesses; 2,563 got it right.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue 2: THE ORIGINAL PHILADELPHIA FLYER. He lived in Philly and famously flew a kite. He discovered lightning... discovered electricity. 5,154 guesses and 675 of you were correct. No more 2,500. This is interesting.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Which means it all comes down to Clue 1: CURRENT EVENTS EXPERT. Because "current" is an electrical current! Finally, he discovered electricity. My bad, Howie. If he didn't have his idea, then Thomas Edison wouldn't have noticed his idea to come up with the light bulb.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: 5,382 guesses. Out of those, only 47 got it right! Which means those 47 people who won Gold are getting $63.82. Not bad!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: We've got 675 Silver winners at $3.70. And there are a lot of Bronze winners—2,563—each going home with 78 cents. Which... they're already home. Well, 78 cents back in BENJAMIN FRANKLIN's day was like $100, so you just won $100!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: And here's another winner: Roommates has just hit Netflix. It stars Sadie Sandler, Adam Sandler's daughter. She plays a shy college freshman who rooms with the cool girl. What starts as a friendship turns into the Super Bowl of passive-aggression. Kind of like today. Nice shirt, Hunter; brave of you to be wearing it.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Before we start Round 2, don't forget that it's not only our 100th episode; it is the conclusion of our first Fan Appreciation Week. Everyone who gives a correct answer on this puzzle, whether you win the money or not, you're going to get the voucher for $5 at the Netflix Shop.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: And if you've already won that voucher earlier this week, don't worry—we're going to have a brand new Fan Appreciation prize on Monday. This is so much easier than when I host alone. Handsomer, too. Dang. Anyway, let's play Round 2!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: All right, $7,500 at stake. Let's load the crystal ball. 100th episode, 200th puzzle. Jeff says the 1,000th clue is coming up! Here is your first clue: HOMETOWN HERO. The clock starts now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: That was your 996th clue. Figuring out these numbers is more fun for us than it is for them.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Focus on the answer, people! I want you to win the money. Resonance Stat 9999 said "LeBron." Jen Z 2012 said "Philly Cheesesteak." Marka said "Spider-Man." Definitely a HOMETOWN HERO.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here's your second clue: CAN HARDLY CONTAIN ITSELF. The clock starts now. Philadelphia cheesesteak could be the answer then! If HOMETOWN HERO is the first clue, and the second clue is CAN HARDLY CONTAIN ITSELF. We have almost 5,000 guesses already. Bold people!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Funky Quesadilla said "Volcano." A volcano CAN HARDLY CONTAIN ITSELF and they are hometown heroes. Cass 20 Healey said "Hulk." That's a good one. "Wouldn't like me if I was mad"—that was his line.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here's your third clue: FRENCH TOAST INGREDIENT. 20 seconds on the clock starts now. HOMETOWN HERO, CAN HARDLY CONTAIN ITSELF, and FRENCH TOAST INGREDIENT.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: There aren't a lot of those, are there? I think that's the one thing I can make—French toast. My wife doesn't cook. I'm hungry, to be honest. It's a surprisingly difficult thing to be the only thing you know how to make. We're getting a lot of guesses. Somebody said "Eggs." We got "Milk" up there. Remember, it's a riddle! Is it eggs?

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Here's your fourth clue: OVER-THE-TOP POP STAR. The clock starts now. HOMETOWN HERO, CAN HARDLY CONTAIN ITSELF, FRENCH TOAST INGREDIENT, and OVER-THE-TOP POP STAR. Almost 20,000 guesses.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: 10 seconds left. Winning with 5 seconds left is very possible. Louie 33 said "Sun." OVER-THE-TOP POP STAR... the sun? FRENCH TOAST INGREDIENT... the sun? And then Slippery Pop Tart said "Lady Gaga." I regret reading that username.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Let's go to clue five, your final chance to get an answer in. Clue 5: MAKES THE CASE FOR A BUBBLY PERSONALITY. Your final 20 seconds starts now. HOMETOWN HERO, CAN HARDLY CONTAIN ITSELF, FRENCH TOAST INGREDIENT, OVER-THE-TOP POP STAR, and finally, MAKES THE CASE FOR A BUBBLY PERSONALITY.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Time's up! Clue 3 had almost 11,000 guesses. I wonder if 11,000 people put eggs. All right, let's find out what's inside the crystal ball!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: The correct answer was CHAMPAGNE! Wow! But wait, look... show them the CHAMPAGNE! Let's switch my type—I'm ready, Mr. Deville. Oh, right there! (Howie pops a bottle of champagne). Go, go, go! I'm nervous, man. Yay!

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: So let's take a look at how the clues fit and find out who is taking the money. Clue 5: MAKES THE CASE FOR A BUBBLY PERSONALITY. Fun fact: there are about 49 million bubbles in a typical bottle of CHAMPAGNE. I know because I got here early and I counted. 4,421 guesses; 1,638 of you got it correct!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue 4: OVER-THE-TOP POP STAR. CHAMPAGNE often flows over the top of the bottle when opened. Cheers to the 4,500 people who guessed and the 620 who got it right.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: The third clue: FRENCH TOAST INGREDIENT. It's a French wine you can "toast" with! To another 100 episodes! 10,731 guesses on FRENCH TOAST INGREDIENT. 251 got it right, meaning 251 in Gold position right now.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Clue 2: CAN HARDLY CONTAIN ITSELF. CHAMPAGNE actually has a cage around the cork because otherwise, it would just burst open. It can't even contain itself in the bottle. 4,063 guesses; only four of you got it correct! That's the new Gold.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: There was one final clue. Clue 1: HOMETOWN HERO. Technically, CHAMPAGNE has to be produced in the CHAMPAGNE region to be called CHAMPAGNE. Otherwise, it's just sparkling wine. It is a HOMETOWN HERO. 4,115 of you guessed. Good try, everybody, because nobody got it right!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Which means the Gold is going to Clue 2. Four people get $750 each! Our Gold guessers are Serene Swan, Ohio Jaded Bone 557, Smart Canopy 810, and AF Thoslo 563. Congratulations! Silver got $10 each, and 620 people got Bronze for $3 each.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: That is it for the 100th episode of Best Guess Live! Thank you to everyone who makes this show possible. This is where the $100 went—the $100 cake! I'd rather have the $100, but I'll eat the cake. Especially all of you out there playing along.

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Yeah, as a way to show our appreciation, we got this beautiful 100th episode cake. We love you guys! You can't have any of it because that's how cell phones work.

lines.speaker: Hunter March
lines.text: Congratulations again to everyone who has won over our 100 episodes; there's many more to come. We're here every weekday, 8:00 Eastern, 5:00 Pacific. Come back Monday and don't forget to bring your best guess. Goodnight, everybody!

lines.speaker: Howie Mandel
lines.text: Cheers! Cheers to you! No, don't... we're worried about it. Hold on, are we still on the air? Let's get it! Go, pull it! There we go! That was it. Wow, this is like a well-oiled machine.
MOST SUBMITTED WRONG GUESSES:
Puzzle 1: NEWSPAPER, SIGNATURE, DECLARATION OF INDEPENDENCE, EAGLE, GEORGE WASHINGTON
Puzzle 2: EGG, EGGS, CINNAMON, BREAD, SUPERMAN
